## PAGE 00001: Accessing and modifying model element properties

- page_id: `170490715`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490715/Accessing+and+modifying+model+element+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:10:18.869+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: ['container']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Element properties can be accessed with simple setters and getters. For example *NamedElement.name* property:

```java

```

##### Container Properties

Our modeling tools use a composite structure of the model.

Every model element is a container and contains its own children and knows about its own parent.

A model element parent can be accessed with the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.getOwner()***method. Owned children can be received with the *Element.getOwnedElement()*method. Different types of children are stored in separate container properties.

You can access these container properties by names that are described in the UML specification. The *getOwnedElement()*method collects all children from all inner container properties.

The container properties modification and iteration is straightforward using the *java.util.Collection*interface. Property change events are fired automatically when container properties are modified.

Containers implement subsets and unions constraints from the UML metamodel specification. This explains how the modification of one container can affect other containers. Make sure you understand subsets and unions in the UML metamodel. If you want to add some inner Element to the union collection, you need to add it into a specific subset of *union.*

Some containers are read-only. This is true for the most of DERIVED UML metamodel properties.

Some derived properties are editable. For example, *Element.ownedElement*is editable.

It is enough to set one UML meta-association property value and an opposite property will be set too. For example, adding a *Class*into a *Package*can be done in two ways:

```java

```

or

```java

```

The following example illustrates retrieving children of model elements:

```java

```

##### Modifying elements in container

Use standard *java.util.Collection or *java.util.List**methods:

```java
().add(child);
    modelElement.get().remove(child);]]>
```

##### Navigable opposite references

Standard UML metamodel defines all references between Elements in "pairs" - for each primary navigable reference there is an opposite reference. Majority of those opposite references are not navigable (are not exposed in opposite metaclass).

For example *TypedElement* references it's *Type*by reference*type*, but *Type* does not expose *TypedElements* typed by it. Such approach is valid semantically, but makes model traversing challenging.

Our UML metamodel implementation does not follow this restriction and exposes all opposite references.

Some opposite references have "friendly" names defined by UML specification.

For example *NamedElement.clientDependency* opposite reference name is *Dependency.client*.

Other opposite references names are constructed using a pattern *_<primaryReferenceDefiningMetaclassName>Of<primaryReferenceName>*. Such naming pattern is used even latest version of UML specification provides different names for these opposite references.

For example there is a primary reference *type* defined in *TypedElement*. Opposite reference is defined in *Type* has name *_typedElementOfType.*

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

Setters and getters are provided for each opposite reference. For example

```java

```

## PAGE 00002: Accessing dependency matrix data

- page_id: `170490825`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490825/Accessing+dependency+matrix+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:28:39.425+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: ['matrix-data-helper', 'dependency-extractor']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Internal data of a dependency matrix can be used outside the dependency matrix component. You can export the data to any required format.

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.MatrixDataHelper* class to get the data component of a dependency matrix. You will get all row and column elements and access all cell information. You can also check, if the dependency matrix is empty.

The *MatrixDataHelper* class has three methods that are to be used to access data of the matrix. First of all you must check, if the matrix is already built (building the matrix means collecting all the needed data from the model). In some cases, when settings are changed or a lot of model changes occur, you have to rebuild the matrix to get the up-to-date information. Thus the best pattern to use is as follows:

```java

```

****

The duration of the build process depends on both the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor* implementation and the size of the matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection2"><p>Internal data of a dependency matrix can be used outside the dependency matrix component. You can export the data to any required format.</p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.MatrixDataHelper</em> class to get the data component of a dependency matrix. You will get all row and column elements and access all cell information. You can also check, if the dependency matrix is empty.</p><p>The <em>MatrixDataHelper</em> class has three methods that are to be used to access data of the matrix. First of all you must check, if the matrix is already built (building the matrix means collecting all the needed data from the model). In some cases, when settings are changed or a lot of model changes occur, you have to rebuild the matrix to get the up-to-date information. Thus the best pattern to use is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23aa6fb2-b457-4ac4-be52-13cb70e60717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    MatrixData matrixData;
    if (MatrixDataHelper.isRebuildNeeded(matrix))
    {
        matrixData = MatrixDataHelper.buildMatrix(matrix);
    }
    else
    {
        matrixData = MatrixDataHelper.getMatrixData(matrix);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span><strong><br /></strong></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="249c2222-0a33-416c-9360-e744c28bfc11"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The duration of the build process depends on both the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor</em> implementation and the size of the matrix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490985 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00003: Action API

- page_id: `170490985`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490985/Action+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:27.412+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Alf Plugin Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *AlfActionUtil* class also includes a set of static methods that perform operations over the entire active Project. These are:

- parse (with no argument), which parses the Alf bodies of all Elements in the active Project that are annotated as needing recompilation.
- parseAll, which parses all Alf text in the active Project, but does not do any mapping to UML.
- build, which does a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building and cleaning' space='ALFP2024xR1'] of the Alf bodies of all elements in the active project that are annotated as needing recompilation and, optionally, also elements annotated with compilation errors (depending on its includeErrorAnnotated argument).
- clean , which deletes all auxiliary template bindings and does a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building and cleaning' space='ALFP2024xR1'] of all Alf text in the active project.

In all cases, dependencies are registered for all parsed Elements and any errors are recorded as error Annotations on relevant Elements (see also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='The Alf editor' space='ALFP2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Management' space='ALFP2024xR1']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>AlfActionUtil</em> class also includes a set of static methods that perform operations over the entire active Project. These are:</p><ul><li><em>parse</em> (with no argument), which parses the Alf bodies of all Elements in the active Project that are annotated as needing recompilation.</li><li><em>parseAll,</em> which parses all Alf text in the active Project, but does not do any mapping to UML.</li><li><em>build,</em> which does a <ac:link ac:anchor="build-project"><ri:page ri:space-key="ALFP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Building and cleaning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[normal build]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the Alf bodies of all elements in the active project that are annotated as needing recompilation and, optionally, also elements annotated with compilation errors (depending on its <em>includeErrorAnnotated</em> argument).</li><li><em>clean</em><em>,</em> which deletes all auxiliary template bindings and does a <ac:link ac:anchor="clean-project"><ri:page ri:space-key="ALFP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Building and cleaning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[clean build]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of all Alf text in the active project.</li></ul><p>In all cases, dependencies are registered for all parsed Elements and any errors are recorded as error Annotations on relevant Elements (see also <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="ALFP2024xR1" ri:content-title="The Alf editor" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="ALFP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Dependency Management" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490948 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00005: Activating and deactivating an element

- page_id: `170490948`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490948/Activating+and+deactivating+an+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.416+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An execution engine can activate a specific element with values by calling an activateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the ExecutionEngine. To deactivate the element, it will call a deactivateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the same class.

These two methods are mostly called in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element). For example, if an Activity element on an Activity diagram is passed from the ExecutionEngine.execute(element), we use the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods to activate and deactivate the Actions in that particular Activity respectively.

If activateElement() or deactivateElement() is called to a specific element, it will trigger all registered engine listeners including both predefined and user-defined listeners. For example, an AnimationListener is a predefined listener that is the Subclass of an EngineListener. It is used to display a specific element animation (in highlight) on a diagram in MagicDraw.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An execution engine can activate a specific element with values by calling an <span style="text-decoration: underline;">activateElement</span>(Element element, Collection values)) of the ExecutionEngine. To deactivate the element, it will call a deactivateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the same class.</p><p>These two methods are mostly called in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element). For example, if an Activity element on an Activity diagram is passed from the ExecutionEngine.execute(element), we use the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods to activate and deactivate the Actions in that particular Activity respectively.</p><p>If activateElement() or deactivateElement() is called to a specific element, it will trigger all registered engine listeners including both predefined and user-defined listeners. For example, an AnimationListener is a predefined listener that is the Subclass of an EngineListener. It is used to display a specific element animation (in highlight) on a diagram in MagicDraw.</p>
````

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\browserpanel

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490856 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: Adding custom project window

- page_id: `170490856`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490856/Adding+custom+project+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:53:12.788+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw allows to open internal windows for the project and display some project related information in these windows. For example, Validation Result, Used By, Depend on, and etc. It is possible to add a custom internal window using API.

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsManager.ConfiguratorRegistry.addConfigurator( ProjectWindowsConfigurator) to register project window configurator.
- Implement com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsConfigurator and add window using ProjectWindowsManager.addWindow(ProjectWindow[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ProjectWindowsManager.html#addWindow-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindow-).

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/projectwindow*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw allows to open internal windows for the project and display some project related information in these windows. For example, Validation Result, Used By, Depend on, and etc. It is possible to add a custom internal window using API.</p><ul><li>Use<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsManager.ConfiguratorRegistry.addConfigurator(</em><em>ProjectWindowsConfigurator)</em> to register project window configurator.</li><li>Implement <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsConfigurator</em> and add window using <em>ProjectWindowsManager.addWindow(ProjectWindow<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ProjectWindowsManager.html#addWindow-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindow-">)</a>.</em><br /><em><br /></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/projectwindow</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490760 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: Adding new diagram types

- page_id: `170490760`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490760/Adding+new+diagram+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:25:49.454+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Diagram types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Adding new diagram types to the modeling tool consists of two steps:

1. Overriding the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor* class[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new diagram types' space='DEVG2024xR1']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new diagram types' space='DEVG2024xR1']

###### **overrideSte****p #1. Override the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor*class**

Override the abstract class *DiagramDescriptor* and implement the following abstract methods:

- getDiagramTypeId() – this method must return a diagram type id, which is used to identify the diagram
- getSingularDiagramTypeHumanName() – the method returns the diagram type human name
- getPluralDiagramTypeHumanName() – the method returns the diagram type human name in a plural form
- getSuperType() – this method must return a super type (super diagram) of this diagram type
- isCreatable() – returns the flag indicating if this diagram type is creatable
- getSVGIcon() – returns a large icon for this type of the diagram (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new functionality' space='DEVG2024xR1']Adding New Functionality )
- getSmallIconURL() – returns a small icon URL for this type of the diagram (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new functionality' space='DEVG2024xR1']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramActions() – returns the manager of actions used in the diagram
- getDiagramToolbarConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures the described diagram toolbar (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new functionality' space='DEVG2024xR1']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcuts (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new functionality' space='DEVG2024xR1']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramContextConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcut menu actions (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new functionality' space='DEVG2024xR1'] ).

###### **The diagram descriptor example**

For the full source code, see the Open API examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples.*

```java

```

**registerSte****p #2. Register a new diagram type**

The new diagram descriptor should be registered in a modeling tool using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addNewDiagramType(**DiagramDescriptor)***method of the modeling tool you are using. This method can be invoked when a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins' space='DEVG2024xR1'] is initialized.

###### **The diagram descriptor registration example**

For the full source code, see the Open API examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\specificdiagram.*

```java

```

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Adding new diagram types to the modeling tool consists of two steps:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="override"><ac:link-body>Overriding the abstract <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor</em> class</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new diagram types" /></ac:link></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="register"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Registering a new diagram type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new diagram types" /></ac:link></p></li></ol><h4><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3317d2bd-41a9-459e-8a9d-2d599a4b2c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="">override</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Ste</strong><strong>p #1. Override the abstract <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor </em>class</strong></h4><div class="WordSection1"><p>Override the abstract class <em>DiagramDescriptor</em> and implement the following abstract methods:</p><ul><li><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">getDiagramTypeId()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">– this method must return a diagram type id, which is used to identify the diagram</span></li><li><em>getSingularDiagramTypeHumanName()</em> – the method returns the diagram type human name</li><li><em>getPluralDiagramTypeHumanName()</em> – the method returns the diagram type human name in a plural form</li><li><em>getSuperType()</em> – this method must return a super type (super diagram) of this diagram type</li><li><em>isCreatable()</em> – returns the flag indicating if this diagram type is creatable</li><li><em>getSVGIcon()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns a large icon for this type of the diagram (see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getSmallIconURL()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns a small icon URL for this type of the diagram (see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramActions()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the manager of actions used in the diagram</span></li><li><em>getDiagramToolbarConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures the described diagram toolbar (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcuts (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramContextConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcut menu actions (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Adding new functionality" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding New Functionality]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">).</span></li></ul></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><h4><strong>The diagram descriptor example</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3eae444-3eb0-41c0-9fbf-4ffa202ef7ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the full source code, see the Open API examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="961dd4e3-b080-4883-8e31-be3e8009358c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    /**
    * Descriptor of a specific diagram.
    */
    public class SpecificDiagramDescriptor extends DiagramDescriptor
    {
         public static final String SPECIFIC_DIAGRAM = "Specific Diagram";
 
        /**
        * Let this diagram type be a sub type of a class diagram type.
        */
        public String getSuperType()
        {
            return DiagramType.UML_CLASS_DIAGRAM;
        }
 
        /**
        * This is a creatable diagram.
        */
        public boolean isCreatable()
        {
            return true;
        }
 
        /**
        * Actions used in this diagram.
        */
        public MDActionsManager getDiagramActions()
        {
            return SpecificDiagramActions.ACTIONS;
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for a diagram toolbar.
        */
        public AMConfigurator getDiagramToolbarConfigurator()
        {
            return new SpecificDiagramToolbarConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for diagram shortcuts.
        */
        public AMConfigurator getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator()
        {
            return new ClassDiagramShortcutsConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for a diagram shortcut menu.
        */
        public DiagramContextAMConfigurator getDiagramContextConfigurator()
        {
            return new BaseDiagramContextAMConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * Id of the diagram type.
        */
        public String getDiagramTypeId()
        {
            return SPECIFIC_DIAGRAM;
        }
 
        /**
        * A diagram type human name.
        */
        public String getSingularDiagramTypeHumanName()
        {
            return "Specific Diagram";
        }
 
        /**
        * A diagram type human name in a plural form.
        */
        public String getPluralDiagramTypeHumanName()
        {
            return "Specific Diagrams";
        }
 
        /**
        * Resizable svg icon for diagram.
        */
        public ResizableIcon getSVGIcon()
		{
   			ResizableIcon icon = null;
   			try
			{
      			icon = IconsFactory.getSvgIcon(new File("icons/specificdiagram.svg").toURI().toURL());
   			}
   			catch(Exception e){e.printStackTrace();}
   			return icon;
		}
 
        /**
        * URL to a small icon for a diagram.
        */
        public URL getSmallIconURL()
        {
             return getClass().getResource("icons/specificdiagram.svg");
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40564597-ffce-43be-b83e-9848b692e065"><ac:parameter ac:name="">register</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Ste</strong><strong>p #2. Register a new diagram type</strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>The new diagram descriptor should be registered in a modeling tool using the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addNewDiagramType(</em><em>DiagramDescriptor)</em><em> </em>method of the modeling tool you are using. This method can be invoked when a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is initialized.</p><h4><strong>The diagram descriptor registration example</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fd30d68-73a8-4e2b-8b2c-43b3e24c78bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the full source code, see the Open API examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\specificdiagram.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea87ecad-9037-47ab-a3ce-7c801987074c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	class SpecificDiagramPlugin extends Plugin{
        /**
        * Initializing the plugin.
        */
        public void init()
        {
            // Registering a new diagram type
            Application.getInstance().addNewDiagramType(new SpecificDiagramDescriptor());
        }
        /**
        * Always returns true, because this plugin does not have any close specific actions.
        */
        public boolean close()
        {
            return true;
        }
        /**
        * Always returns true, because this plugin does not have any specific suportability conditions.
        */
        public boolean isSupported()
        {
            return true;
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00009: Adding new functionality

- page_id: `170490671`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490671/Adding+new+functionality
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:35.882+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['menu-command', 'button', 'command', 'shortcut-menu-command']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A program actions mechanism enables to add new functionality to the modeling tool and the way to invoke it through GUI. In this case, all menu commands and buttons are considered as actions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using and extending other UI components' space='DEVG2024xR1'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using and extending other UI components' space='DEVG2024xR1']

In this case, all menu commands and buttons are considered as actions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="480c08b7-4c55-41b6-9165-291917dc8362"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="71d29685-16b3-4762-9684-067bac30d19f"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p> </p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using and extending other UI components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Using and extending other UI component]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="248c8d01-fca8-41a3-b981-6c8d55fab471"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using and extending other UI components" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490722 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00010: Adding, moving, deleting model elements

- page_id: `170490722`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490722/Adding+moving+deleting+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:33:14.329+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways to add/move/delete model elements:

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ModelElementsManager
- Call direct functions on Elements

The *ModelElementsManager* is the singleton utility class which checks *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* editing permissions and the session existence before executing the function. Also, it performs a check, if an element can be added to a parent. If the*ModelElementsManager*is not used, a programmer must perform these checks in the code explicitly.

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.NamedElement

Element

(s)

##### Adding element to owner with *ModelElementsManager*

For adding a new model *Element*into a model, use the *addElement(**Element, Element)*method provided by the *ModelElementsManager*.

```java

```

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Operation

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package

Operation

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException

com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException

##### Adding element to owner using a direct function

```java

```

##### Moving element with *ModelElementsManager*

For moving an existing model *Element*, use the *moveElement(**Element, Element)*method provided by the *ModelElementsManager*.

```java

```

If a given model element cannot be moved into a given parent, *java.lang.IllegalArgumentException*is thrown. For example, an *Operation* cannot be moved into a *Package* or an *Operation* cannot be moved into a not locked for editing *Class* (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, *java.lang.IllegalArgumentException* also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException* is thrown.

##### Moving element using a direct function

```java

```

##### Removing element with *ModelElementsManager*

```java

```

##### Removing element using direct call

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\hierarchyremover*

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring model elements' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><p>There are two ways to add/move/delete model elements:</p><ul><li>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ModelElementsManager</em></li><li>Call direct functions on <em>Elements</em></li></ul><p>The <em>ModelElementsManager</em> is the singleton utility class which checks <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> editing permissions and the session existence before executing the function. <span>Also, it performs a check, if an element can be added to a parent. If the </span><em>ModelElementsManager </em><span>is not used, a programmer must perform these checks in the code explicitly.</span></p></div></div><div class="WordSection1">Adding and moving functions are similar with mostly one - move functions does more checking such as checking for a name conflict if <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.NamedElement</em> is moved. The general rule would be to use an adding function for newly created <em>Element</em><em>(s)</em> and use a moving function for already existing elements in the model.</div><div class="WordSection1">More advanced model refactoring functions are described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Refactoring model elements" /></ac:link> page.</div><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Adding element to owner with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><p>For adding a new model <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element </em>into a model, use the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">addElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, Element) </em>method provided by the <em>ModelElementsManager</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b066f9ab-b044-4733-abb5-6f5d06b9fa43"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Package package = ...;
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into package");
    try
    {
        // add a class into a package
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().addElement(classA, package);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div>If a given model element cannot be added into a given parent, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> is thrown. For example, an <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Operation</em> cannot be added into a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package</em> or an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be added into a not locked for editing <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em> (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException</em> is thrown.</div><h3>Adding element to owner using a direct function</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05943c37-2b41-41df-80e3-21a3175ad88f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element parent = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into parent"); 
    if (parent.canAdd(classA))
    {
        classA.setOwner(parent);
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Moving element with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><p>For moving an existing model <em>Element</em>, use the <em>moveElement(</em><em>Element, Element) </em>method provided by the <em>ModelElementsManager</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce0de87-0851-4f30-ab48-85adf5d3be46"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Package package = ...;
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into package");
    try
    {
        // add a class into a package
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().moveElement(classA, package);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div><p>If a given model element cannot be moved into a given parent, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em>is thrown. For example, an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be moved into a <em>Package</em> or an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be moved into a not locked for editing <em>Class</em> (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException</em> is thrown.</p><h3>Moving element using a direct function</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39454626-5057-4dd2-85d9-bbde833669cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element parent = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into parent"); 
    if (com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper.canMoveChildInto(parent, classA))
    {
        classA.setOwner(parent);
    } 
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Removing element with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdbb7ac5-f257-4b44-b687-708af26d1050"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Remove class"); 
    try
    {
         // remove a class
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().removeElement(classA);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project); 

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Removing element using direct call</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8157f4d1-7fc3-4245-97c3-781164a7988a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Remove class"); 
    if (classA.isEditable()))
    {
         classA.dispose();
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\hierarchyremover</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ed0cc5f-904b-4b81-9316-e50cc55cc824"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef8a4586-965a-4ddd-a692-2928b514fae3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff0c20a8-6f81-463f-abbf-70b3ea1f6b2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Refactoring model elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490739 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00011: Advanced utility functions

- page_id: `170490739`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490739/Advanced+utility+functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:11:14.968+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper* for various advanced UML model related functions.

It provides utility methods for

- Some core functions
  - working with element's documentation (Comments)
  - getting/setting Relationships client and supplier ends
  - collecting connected relationships of element.
  - working with Associations - getting member ends, changing navigability
  - working with multiplicity of MultiplicityElements
  - ownership related functions - looking for valid owners, checking ownership and etc.
- Working with Value Specifications
  - Creating value specifications
  - Setting value to Value specifications
- Working with Classifiers
  - collecting derived classifiers, checking legal inheritance
  - collecting inherited members
  - working with redefined elements
  - checking Classifiers compatibility
  - working with Operation parameters, comparing Operations signatures
- Working with InstanceSpecifications
  - creating Slots, initializing slots values
  - working with Links

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InformationFlowHelper* for advanced Information Flows related functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper* for Interaction domain related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StateMachineHelper* for State Machine related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.UseCaseHelper***for Use Case related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ConnectorsCollector* to collect connected Connector elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper</em> for various advanced UML model related functions.</p><p>It provides utility methods for </p><ul><li>Some core functions<ul><li>working with element's documentation (Comments)</li><li>getting/setting Relationships client and supplier ends</li><li>collecting connected relationships of element.</li><li><span>working with Associations - getting member ends, changing navigability</span></li><li>working with multiplicity of MultiplicityElements</li><li>ownership related functions - looking for valid owners, checking ownership and etc.</li></ul></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Working with Value Specifications</span><br /><ul><li>Creating value specifications</li><li>Setting value to Value specifications</li></ul></li><li>Working with Classifiers<ul><li>collecting derived classifiers, checking legal inheritance</li><li>collecting inherited members</li><li>working with redefined elements</li><li>checking Classifiers compatibility</li><li>working with Operation parameters,  comparing Operations signatures</li></ul></li><li>Working with InstanceSpecifications<ul><li>creating Slots, initializing slots values</li><li>working with Links</li></ul></li></ul><p>Use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InformationFlowHelper</em> for advanced Information Flows related functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper</em> for Interaction domain related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StateMachineHelper</em> for State Machine related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.UseCaseHelper</em><em> </em>for Use Case related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ConnectorsCollector</em> to collect connected Connector elements</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490857 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00013: Annotating the elements

- page_id: `170490857`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490857/Annotating+the+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:26:31.995+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['annotation-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the Open API you can add an annotation to any base element (a model element or a symbol on the diagram pane). Annotations are used to highlight and adorn elements in the Containment tree and on the diagram pane. For example validation engine is using annotations for showing invalid elements.

Annotation has the following properties:

- severity - like an error, warning, info, debug, fatal
- kind - a string representing the annotation short name
- text - a string representing the annotation text
- target - a target base element
- actions - an optional list of actions. They are shown in a diagram on a symbol’s smart manipulator.

To add or remove annotations, use***com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationManager*.

Do not forget to call*AnnotationManager.update()*, when you are done with adding or removing in order to refresh UI.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\annotations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Using the Open API you can add an annotation to any base element (a model element or a symbol on the diagram pane). Annotations are used to highlight and adorn elements in the Containment tree and on the diagram pane. For example validation engine is using annotations for showing invalid elements.</p><p>Annotation has the following properties:</p><ul><li>severity - like an error, warning, info, debug, fatal</li><li>kind - a string representing the annotation short name</li><li>text - a string representing the annotation text</li><li>target - a target base element</li><li>actions - an optional list of actions. They are shown in a diagram on a symbol’s smart manipulator.</li></ul><p>To add or remove annotations, use<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationManager</em>.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="694316df-2423-4140-92cc-a88993ad7a03"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not forget to call<em> AnnotationManager.update()</em>, when you are done with adding or removing in order to refresh UI.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\annotations</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

Set to true for parametric models to be automatically recalculated when there is a change in any structural feature value. /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.INITIALIZE_NUMERICAL_VALUE_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.INITIALIZE_NUMERICAL_VALUE</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.INITIALIZE_REFERENCES_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.INITIALIZE_REFERENCES</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.LASTVISITED_COLOR_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.LAST_VISITED_COLOR</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.LOG_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.LOG</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.NUMBER_OF_RUNS_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.NUMBER_OF_RUNS</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.NUMBER_OF_STEPS_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.NUMBER_OF_STEPS</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.OPEN_SIMULATION_PANE_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.OPEN_SIMULATION_PANE</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.PASS_CALLER_CONTEXT_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.PASS_CALLER_CONTEXT</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RECORD_STATE_CHANGE_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RECORD_STATE_CHANGE</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RECORD_TIMESTAMP_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RECORD_TIMESTAMP</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RECORD_VALUE_CHANGE_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RECORD_VALUE_CHANGE</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.REMEMBER_FAILURE_STATUS_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.REMEMBER_FAILURE_STATUS</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RESULT_LOCATION_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RESULT_LOCATION</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RUNTIME_VALUE_TEXT_COLOR_ON_PART_SHAPES_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RUNTIME_VALUE_TEXT_COLOR_ON_PART_SHAPES</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.RUN_FORKS_IN_PARALLEL_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.RUN_FORKS_IN_PARALLEL</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.SHOW_ACTIVE_STATES_ON_PART_SHAPES_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.SHOW_ACTIVE_STATES_ON_PART_SHAPES</td></tr><tr><td>SimulationOptionConstants.SHOW_ACTIVE_STATE_IMAGES_NAME</td><td>SimulationOptions.SHOW_ACTIVE_STATE_IMAGES</td></ Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.fileattachments.FileAttachmentsHelper* to create, remove or save attached files to disk.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490862 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00017: Binary validation rule

- page_id: `170490862`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490862/Binary+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:35.013+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Sometimes it is hard to specify the rule for some advanced concepts in OCL. For example, the string manipulation is rather weak in OCL2.0, hence the writing rule for string formats, parsing strings is very hard in OCL. In such cases the modeling tool offers possibility to write rules in Java language and invoke them when validating.

Common steps for creating a binary validation rule:

1. Create a constraint
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation
4. Specify constrained element(s)
5. Specify the specification language binary
6. Create a Java implementation class (see all possible [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementing a binary validation rule' space='DEVG2024xR1'] )
7. Compile Java code, resulting in one or several classes.
8. Ensure that your modeling tool can find & load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism. The same rules as writing the code for the open API are used here.

These steps are common to all possible binary validation cases and will not be repeated in each case description.

See the *MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java* example in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation* directory.

The implementation class must be in the classpath of the program or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binary validation rule in the plugin' space='DEVG2024xR1']).

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

For example, the string manipulation is rather weak in OCL2.0, hence the writing rule for string formats, parsing strings is very hard in OCL. In such cases the modeling tool offers possibility to write rules in Java language and invoke them when validating.</p><p>Common steps for creating a binary validation rule:</p><ol><li>Create a constraint</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation</li><li>Specify constrained element(s)</li><li>Specify the specification language binary</li><li>Create a Java implementation class (see all possible <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Implementing a binary validation rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</li><li>Compile Java code, resulting in one or several classes.</li><li>Ensure that your modeling tool can find &amp; load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism. The same rules as writing the code for the open API are used here.</li></ol></div><p>These steps are common to all possible binary validation cases and will not be repeated in each case description.</p><p>See the <em>MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java</em> example in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation</em> directory.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc1cc02e-8e2c-401c-be1d-e931a383080b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The implementation class must be in the classpath of the program or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Binary validation rule in the plugin" /></ac:link></span>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490864 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00018: Binary validation rule in the plugin

- page_id: `170490864`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490864/Binary+validation+rule+in+the+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:37.337+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation engine must be able to load the validation rule implementation class. The validation engine is able to load a class if the implementation class exists in the program classpath or if the validation rule implementation class is in the plugin classpath. The plugin must register the plugin’s classloader to the validation system as follows:

```java
. class.getClassLoader());]]>
```

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='DEVG2024xR1']Creating validation rules

The plugin must register the plugin’s classloader to the validation system as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0158529d-a4dc-4061-ab45-df688a662331"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    EvaluationConfigurator.getInstance().registerBinaryImplementers(<PluginClassName>. class.getClassLoader());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /><ac:link-body>Creating validation rules<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00019: Browser

- page_id: `170490845`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490845/Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:51:52.640+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['browser', 'browser-tree']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Model Browser (Containment tree, Diagrams tree, etc.) is a based GUI part for displaying various model trees and panels

```java

```

The browser has five trees by default (some of them are be default hidden):

- containment
- diagrams
- inheritance
- extensions
- search results

The browser has 3 panel by default

- documentation
- properties
- zoom

Last activated tree is marked as active:

```java

```

Every tree is based on Swing JTree and all manipulations can be done by using the API provided by Swing:

```java

```

Selected nodes are accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Node is derived from *javax.swing.tree.DefaultMutableTreeNode*.

**Selecting/opening element in tree**

In order to select some element in tree, you need

- Open(activate) a tree using com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser#getContainmentTree ;
- Call com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Tree#openNode(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement).

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490670 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00020: Building a resource distribution file

- page_id: `170490670`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490670/Building+a+resource+distribution+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:38.766+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate **.zip** files.

This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.

There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:

1) Using the **Development Tools** plugin GUI

1. Install the Development Tools plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.
2. Restart the modeling tool.
3. Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.
4. In the modeling tool main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Resource Distribution File .
5. Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click OK . A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.

The**Development Tools**plugin can be installed from[nomagic.com](https://www.nomagic.com/)or found in the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*).

2) Using command line tool.

In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.

Three arguments are required:

- Resource directory path
- Output directory path
- Result distribution file name

Example:

```text

```

Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate <strong>.zip</strong> files.</p><p>This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.</p><p>There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:</p><p>1) Using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin GUI</p><ol><li>Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li><li>Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.</li><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Development Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Build Resource Distribution File</strong>.</li><li>Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>. A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5659f791-f619-43e5-95fc-bc26a98bc206"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><strong> Development Tools</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> plugin can be installed from </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.nomagic.com/" rel="nofollow">nomagic.com</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> or found in the resource distribution file (.</span><em>rdzip</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2) Using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Three arguments are required:</p><ul><li>Resource directory path</li><li>Output directory path</li><li>Result distribution file name</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03b2273a-6b5a-4114-bc74-74151ee8841a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -cp "lib/*;lib/bundles/*" com.nomagic.magicdraw.resourcemanager.distribution.GenerateResourceDistributionFileTask "E:\myResources" "E:\myBundles" "myCustomResourceDistributionFile"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74a60d1f-d3d7-4766-8732-0a95016063c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
