<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490954 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: Display Values in Diagrams

- page_id: `170490954`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490954/Display+Values+in+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-10T10:59:58.382+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Displaying Runtime Values

When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.

Start by extending *com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider*class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the *context*for which the *ExternalValueProvider* instance was constructed*.* The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the **Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes**project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the *Transmission.mdzip* sample project: **30.0 mi/h** for **carSpeed**and **19.0**for **wheelDiameter**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the runtime values

Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the *Transmission.mdzip* sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:

```java
pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]>
```

To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the *ExternalValueProviders* service, as illustrated here:

```java
// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]>
```

****The full code example is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.*

##### Animating flowing information

Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option **Active Color**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the animation in between the connectors

```java
// select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]>
```

The full code example for this feature is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\flowanimation.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Displaying Runtime Values</h3><p>When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.</p><p>Start by extending <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider </em>class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the <em>context </em>for which the <em>ExternalValueProvider</em> instance was constructed<em>.</em> The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the  <strong>Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes </strong>project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample project: <strong>30.0 mi/h</strong> for <strong>carSpeed </strong>and <strong>19.0 </strong>for <strong>wheelDiameter</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayed_Values.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Display Values in Diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the runtime values</h6><p>Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ac38052-7ee8-43ed-8f46-f0f3fe974dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//Engine Control Unit refers to ECU block in the Transmission sample project
public class EngineControlUnitValueProvider extends ExternalValueProvider
{
	private final Element carSpeedElement;
	private final Element wheelDiameterElement;
	
	//Mimics some model execution process that happens outside this tool and produces values to display
	private final MockExternalExecution externalExecution;

	public EngineControlUnitValueProvider(Element context)
	{
		super(context);
		Project project = Project.getProject(context);
		carSpeedElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::carSpeed", Property.class);
		wheelDiameterElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::wheelDiameter", Property.class);
		externalExecution = new MockExternalExecution(this);
	}

	@CheckForNull
	@Override
	public String getValueText(Element element, List<? extends Element> pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the <em>ExternalValueProviders</em> service, as illustrated here:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0b5143-d3dd-4a29-bfc0-8b89d868f934"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = <...>
	// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f9d9d5b-ed4b-48a7-a50a-738aab1b1876"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Animating flowing information</h3><p>Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option <strong>Active Color</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flow_Animation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Display Values in Diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the animation in between the connectors</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f39e5ca-598c-46ef-a577-bb49460f9e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[			Connector connector = <..> // select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a5b4d2f-fdca-4666-bbd0-7e9d025f71f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example for this feature is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\flowanimation.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490791 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Displaying related elements in the diagram

- page_id: `170490791`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490791/Displaying+related+elements+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:17:17.105+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols* class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:

- What relationship types should be included.
- The depth of a relationship tree.
- Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.

###### **Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayrelated

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols</em> class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:</p><ul><li>What relationship types should be included.</li><li>The depth of a relationship tree.</li><li>Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Display related");
 
    Set linkTypes = new HashSet();
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(Generalization.class));
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(InterfaceRealization.class));
    
    DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo info = new DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo(linkTypes);
    info.setDepthLimited(true);
    info.setDepthLimit(3);
 
    PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you need to invoke the displaying related symbols action.
    DisplayRelatedSymbols.displayRelatedSymbols(view, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayrelated</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490660 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00086: Distributing Resources

- page_id: `170490660`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490660/Distributing+Resources
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:24.093+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

515448144

515448146

515448145

In this section, you will find information about how to share your created resources with other users. The easiest way to distribute data is to store resources in the one zip archive and import files to your modeling tool with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can distribute the following types of resources:

- Custom Diagrams
- Profiles
- Templates
- Samples and Documentation
- Plugins

You can distribute the one resource type or you may distribute the whole set of resources.

515448143

**Related pages**

true

## PAGE 00087: Editing hyperlinks

- page_id: `170490831`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490831/Editing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:18:12.781+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model > Managing hyperlinks
- labels: ['hyperlink-text', 'hyperlink-text-active', 'hyperlink-model', 'hyperlink-model-active']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hyperlinks are implemented using stereotypes and tag values. The *HyperlinkOwner*stereotype has the following its properties (tags):

- hyperlinkText - all simple hyperlinks
- hyperlinkTextActive - an active simple hyperlink
- hyperlinkModel - all hyperlinks to a model element
- hyperlinkModelActive - an active hyperlink to a model element

You can use generic profiling API to edit/add/remove hyperlinks to model elements. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space='DEVG2024xR1'] for more details.

Recommended way is to use utility class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils*. This class provides utility methods for adding/removing/retrieving***com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink* from model Element,

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with stereotypes and tagged values" /></ac:link> for more details.</p><p>Recommended way is to use utility class <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em>. This class provides utility methods for adding/removing/retrieving<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink</em> from model Element, </p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85facba2-7e1a-4a0e-a0f9-4ea748c74ea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with stereotypes and tagged values" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00088: Editing model elements

- page_id: `170490721`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490721/Editing+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:36:31.392+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit some *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, a session with the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager***must be created.

All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.

```java

```

It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To edit some <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, a session with the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em><em> </em>must be created. </p><p>All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cb57726-9021-46e3-a746-c9f00cb8d206"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit class A");
    if (classA.isEditable())
    {
        classA.setName(newName);
    }
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f80fb8f-62ab-4a95-b916-c5df66cb9b23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00089: Element Referencing in Texts

- page_id: `170490927`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490927/Element+Referencing+in+Texts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:59:53.316+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts* class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.

#### INFO: Examples

Examples

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling_tool_installation_directory>\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.*

##### Creating text that refers an element

To create text that refers an element, use:

- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String),
- *ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-)*,
- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText (BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode) **

```text

```

##### **Setting text with element references**

Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using *com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Open API provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts</em> class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8115ff91-29d0-40a0-88a0-8ce2d270a13f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling_tool_installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating text that refers an element</h3><p>To create text that refers an element, use:</p><ul><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String), </em></li><li><em><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-">)</a></em>, </em></li><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText</em><em>(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode)</em><br /><em><em><br /></em></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73066c2a-f742-4f9f-9f82-237e888ddd4b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//creates a text that references an element with default options
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link");

//creates a text that is updated automatically and is displayed using its representation text. 
//This means the elements representation text will be displayed rather than "Link"
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT, 
UpdateMode.AUTOMATIC_UPDATE);

//create a referencing text with a provided display mode and with a default update mode
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT);

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Setting text with element references</strong></h3><p>Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using <em>com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)</em>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0b83594-eaad-44c1-b5ff-fc3d8884f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Setting text for comment body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//create a text with an element reference
String constructedText = "This text refers an element: " + createReferencingText(elementToRefer);
//convert it to a light html. Light html can be used in a comment body, constraint specification, etc. to show th etext containing references
String textForCommentBody = TextUtils.toLightHtml(constructedText);

Project project = Project.getProject(comment);
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Set comment text with reference");
comment.setBody(textForCommentBody);
SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00090: Engine

- page_id: `170490943`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490943/Engine
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-09T16:39:40.878+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

730383780

ExecutionEngine defines how a Behavior e.g. State Machine is executed, including the full logic how their inner elements (e.g. States) are activated. 
Implementing custom ExecutionEngine and registering it's ExecutionEngineDescriptor enables overriding the default behavior of the Cameo Simulation Toolkit for specific: 
 - StateMachine 
 - Interaction 
 - Activity

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94e00025-b4e3-425b-a15c-f89241ded743"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">730383780</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>ExecutionEngine defines how a Behavior e.g. State Machine is executed, including the full logic how their inner elements (e.g. States) are activated.<br />Implementing custom ExecutionEngine and registering it's ExecutionEngineDescriptor enables overriding the default behavior of the Cameo Simulation Toolkit for specific:<br /> - StateMachine<br /> - Interaction<br /> - Activity</p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4bb88949-49a5-415c-a134-4d369914d290" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00091: Environment Options

- page_id: `170490844`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490844/Environment+Options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:53:28.018+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the *global.opt*file that is located in*<user home directory>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<version number>\data.*You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern.

You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.

To add your own environment options

1. Extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup class.
2. Add the extending class to application environment options.

###### **Example of adding custom environment options**

```java

```

###### **Example of accessing environment options**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\environmentoptions

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the <em>global.opt </em>file that is located in<em> &lt;user home directory&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;version number&gt;\data. </em>You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern. </p><p>You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add your own environment options</p><hr /><ol><li>Extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup</em> class.</li><li>Add the extending class to application environment options.</li></ol><h4><strong>Example of adding custom environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92870dc5-f5fc-4dc4-a0e0-eecab4ec361b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	class MyOptionsGroup extends AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup
    {
        ...
    }
    Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
    EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
    options.addGroup(new MyOptionsGroup());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example of accessing environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3e6aa9b-33ff-47cc-bd74-07c542fc3afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
	EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
	int imageDpi = options.getGeneralOptions().getImageResolutionDpi();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\environmentoptions</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490740 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: Event support

- page_id: `170490740`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490740/Event+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:56.822+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to events while changing a model. There is a possibility either to get an event immediately after the property has been changed, or get the event about all the changes in the session, after this session has been closed.

There are four different listener registration types:

- The w hole repository listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements.
- The specific element listener. You will get events about the changes in any property of this element.
- The e lement’s specific property listener. You will get events about the changes of this property.
- The s pecific element type listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements of the specific metatype.

The transaction listener is notified, when all the changes within the session are done and the session is closed.

You can find the code examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\events*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

## PAGE 00093: Example

- page_id: `170490896`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490896/Example

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:

```text

```

See the following sample:

```text

```

We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:

```text

```

There are three steps to do this:

###### **Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags**

We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to *true*.

We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.

###### ****

###### **Step #2. Changing the template file**

Add the following lines to the template file:

```text

```

This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.

Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff98097b-ec0f-47e5-85e0-4b8dd4de7aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DROP VIEW view_name;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>See the following sample:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd214291-a8a7-4b6a-ac42-7b667c2851ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a21e59ae-3cde-48a1-9ae4-018ec906ba60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	DROP VIEW view_name CASCADE CONSTRAINTS;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are three steps to do this:</p><h4><strong>Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags</strong></h4><p>We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to <em>true</em>.</p><p>We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.</p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></h4><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step #2. Changing the template file</strong></h4><p>Add the following lines to the template file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294b4f13-dfa4-4d76-a9ed-8674cea596bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #set($cascadeOption =false)
    ##this line is required to for setting a new variable to the false state.
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.</p><p>Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028221c4-31db-4949-ab08-f30891c9f943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    ## sets a value to a new variable
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )
    #if ($cascadeOption) CASCADE CONSTRAINTS
    #end${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490939 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: Execution

- page_id: `170490939`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490939/Execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.317+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

189375002

189375005

189375003

A simulation execution represents a model execution of a specific element. It is the top level of the model execution which contains running execution sessions and execution engines. A simulation execution will start when the specific element is executed (through the graphical user interface or Java API). The following APIs are available for handling executions.

- Start Execution
- Stop Execution
- Creating and Registering a New Execution Listener

189375000

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490727 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: Finding elements by name or by meta-type

- page_id: `170490727`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490727/Finding+elements+by+name+or+by+meta-type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:45:46.742+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.

For example:

```java
models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]>
```

```text

```

**Finding or creating Classifiers**

A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Retrieving meta information about element' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> for finding elements by the name or type (meta-type) in a project. This utility class provides several internal finders for executing different types of search operations.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fc8c8b9-aa83-401a-a514-2f31e191100e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = ...;
   //find a Model element under the project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is not a recursive search
   Model model = Finder.byName().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find a first Model element under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Model model = Finder.byNameRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");
   //find all Model elements under project primary model with a name "MyModel". It is a recursive search.
   Collection<Model> models = Finder.byNameAllRecursively().find(project.getPrimaryModel(), Model.class, "MyModel");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><br /></pre><p><strong>Finding or creating Classifiers</strong></p><p>A special utility class is available for search or creating Classifiers by metatype, simple or qualified name - <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassifierFinder</em></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3df531-9a85-4f43-a56f-cbcb8d51f363"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Retrieving meta information about element" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490962 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00097: General convention

- page_id: `170490962`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490962/General+convention
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-01-31T10:12:55.695+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Unless specified otherwise, this document assumes that the following specific operations call HTTP methods.

| Operation | HTTP method | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Get or read | GET | Get data. |
| Create | POST | Create new data. |
| Replace attributes | PUT | Replace all existing attributes with the new ones. Attributes with unspecified new values will be reset to null values. |
| Update a (sub)set of attributes | PATCH | Replace some attributes only with the new ones. Unspecified attributes remain unchanged. |
| Delete | DELETE | Delete data. |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw-specific extensions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Unless specified otherwise, this document assumes that the following specific operations call HTTP methods.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 99.5857%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 20.1384%;" /><col class="" style="width: 12.343%;" /><col class="" style="width: 67.5523%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Operation</th><th>HTTP method</th><th>Meaning</th></tr><tr class=""><td>Get or read</td><td>GET</td><td>Get data.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Create</td><td>POST</td><td>Create new data.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Replace attributes</td><td>PUT</td><td>Replace all existing attributes with the new ones. Attributes with unspecified new values will be reset to null values.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Update a (sub)set of attributes</td><td>PATCH</td><td>Replace some attributes only with the new ones. Unspecified attributes remain unchanged.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>Delete</td><td>DELETE</td><td>Delete data.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Authentication" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model manipulation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MagicDraw-specific extensions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490623 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: How plugins work

- page_id: `170490623`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490623/How+plugins+work
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:51:35.971+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: ['plugin', 'init']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On every startup, a modeling tool scans the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins directories' space='DEVG2024xR1'], and searches for subdirectories there:

- If a subdirectory contains the plugin descriptor file, the plugin's manager reads the descriptor file.
- If requirements specified in a descriptor file are fulfilled, the plugin's manager loads a specified class (the specified plugin class must be derived from t he *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin* class). Then a met hod *init()* of th e loaded class is called. Th e *init()* meth od can add GUI components using the actions architecture or do other activities and return from the method. T he *init()* met hod is called only if isSupported() returns true .

The following figure illustrates how do plugins work.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins directories' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Th<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">e <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> meth</span>od can add GUI components using the actions architecture or do other activities and return from the method. T<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">he <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><em>init()</em></span> met</span>hod is called only if <em>isSupported()</em> returns <em>true</em>. </li></ul><p>The following figure illustrates how do plugins <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="820d05a4-ab69-4d48-84c7-ec08aad275c6">work</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="How plugins work" ac:alt="How plugins work" ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="how_plugins_work.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="How plugins work" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be6237d0-46ae-44d8-ae47-134ddfc9ad1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugins directories" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490661 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: How to distribute resources

- page_id: `170490661`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490661/How+to+distribute+resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.550+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Distributing Resources
- labels: ['resource-builder', 'resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:

- Using Custom Resource Building Wizard :

1. Install the Development Tools plugin. Click Help > Resource/Plugin Manager , download the plugin and restart the program.
2. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='DEVG2024xR1']. The structure must not contain the "data/resourcemanager" folders yet.
3. Start Resource Builder (**Tools** > **Development Tools** >******Build Custom Resource...**), select a *MagicDraw* folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.
4. Fill the properties in the provided form.
5. Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click **Finish**. Resource Builder will create the "data/resourcemanager" folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the *MagicDraw* folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.

- Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:

1. Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .
2. Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .
3. Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure: [ATTACHMENT filename='Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png']
4. Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager. For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

Resource Manager supports zip archives only!

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating required files and folders structure' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can distribute custom resources in the following two ways:</p><ul><li>Using <strong>Custom Resource Building Wizard</strong>:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. Click <strong>Help </strong>&gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>, download the plugin and restart the program.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of the resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60754bd1-08fe-4b22-8e1c-f3706d2cc6a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The structure must not contain the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folders yet.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Start Resource Builder (<strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Development Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Build Custom Resource...</strong>), select a <em>MagicDraw</em> folder structure you have constructed in the step #2 step.</p></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Fill the properties in the provided form.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><p>Select the desired resource (a ZIP archive) output file, and click <strong>Finish</strong>. Resource Builder will create the &quot;data/resourcemanager&quot; folder containing an XML file with the properties you provided and assemble everything inside the <em>MagicDraw</em> folder into the ZIP file you specified as the output file. The resource (ZIP) file you have created can be distributed to your team members and installed using the Resource/Plugin Manager.</p></li></ol><ul><li>Distribute manually. The manual distribution consists of the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create required files. For information about what files are required for each type of resource, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Create a Resource descriptor file. For more information about the Resource descriptor file, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link>.</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Archive created files to a zip file. The zip file should include the required files, as well as folders that have a structure matching the structure of a modeling tool. Information about the folders structure for each type of resource and the general view of the file structure is depicted in the following figure:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structure_of_directories_and_files_that_could_be_distributed_through_ResourceManager.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="How to distribute resources" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><em><br /></em></li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Import your prepared data to your modeling tool through the Resource Manager.<p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fe2b8d4-4f2f-4d32-8a11-ff2b796c6db8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about the MagicDraw Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cf381e0-b667-4158-ada3-e128fab3c32d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource Manager supports zip archives only!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc9101d3-2965-4f05-a251-d4bbaa295297"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource manager descriptor file" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490866 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: How to provide a solution for a problem found during validation?

- page_id: `170490866`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490866/How+to+provide+a+solution+for+a+problem+found+during+validation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:08:07.600+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the*com.nomagic.actions.NMAction* class and implement the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction* interface.

See *MyAction.java*, *FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java*, *MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java* and *JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java* examples in <program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotating the elements' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The validation rule returns annotations as validation results to the validation engine. Each annotation can contain a list of action objects that implements how a particular problem found by the validation rule can be solved. In order to create an action for solving, the validation rule’s provider must create a Java class that extends the<em> com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> class and implement the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method. A user will be able to invoke the action from the validation results table or from the browser. In order to enable performing the action on multiple targets, the action class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationAction</em> interface.</p><p>See <em>MyAction.java</em>, <em>FixJavaConstantNamesAction.java</em>, <em>MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java</em> and <em>JavaConstantNameValidationRuleImpl.java</em> examples in &lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e957a260-479d-4d40-9eea-ef16acd7c6f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Annotating the elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490726 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: Identifying elements

- page_id: `170490726`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490726/Identifying+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:52:18.977+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Two methods provide an identifier of an *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*. Either method can be used depending on the situation. This section explains the difference between the two:

1. com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.base.ModelObject.getLocalID()
2. com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation.MDObject.getID()

The meaning of the returned identifiers depends on the project type.

###### Local (.mdzip) projects

Both methods return the same identifier, which is permanent and unique in the scope of the project. In most cases, this ID is unique among different projects, unless you create a copy of the project by copying the file or using the **Save As** command.

#### TIP: Examples

Examples

1. If a project A is used in projects B and C , then the elements of the project A have the same identifiers in both projects B and C .
2. If a project A is copied as a project A1 , and a project B uses the project A , using the project A1 additionally will not be allowed since IDs must remain unique in the scope of the project B .

###### Teamwork Cloud projects

If a local project is added to the Teamwork Cloud (or vice versa), *getLocalID()* consistently returns the same identifier value. Note that several elements with the same local ID may exist in the scope of a single Teamwork Cloud project.

*getID()* returns UUID, which is unique both in the project and in the scope of the whole Teamwork Cloud server repository.

The Teamwork Cloud imports (clones) the contents of used projects. This means copies of the elements are created inside the project. While **getLocalID()** returns the same value for different copies, **getID()** will return a unique value for each copy (project usage).

#### TIP: Example

Example

If a project *A* is copied as a project *A1*, and a Teamwork Cloud project *B* uses both *A* and *A1*, **getLocalID()** returns the same value for corresponding elements of *A* and *A1*, but **getID()** returns different identifiers for the same elements.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

Both identifiers can be used to retrieve the element using the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getElementByID(java.lang.String)*. Be aware that if several elements exist in the project by the provided local ID, only a single one of them is returned.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with UML model' space='DEVG2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490643 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00102: Identifying plugin dependencies

- page_id: `170490643`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490643/Identifying+plugin+dependencies
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:23.998+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that *jar*files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. Also, your *plugin.xml* must define the required plugin too.

To find dependencies

- In the Main toolbar, click Tools > Development Tools > Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...

The Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024xR1'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Also, your <em>plugin.xml</em> must define the required plugin too.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To find dependencies</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Main toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Tools &gt; Development Tools &gt; Find Plugin Dependency by Class Name...<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="715abddf-cc51-4d32-8a21-36295559c0a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Development Tools menu item is available once the Development Tools Plugin is installed.
