<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490744 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00122: Listening to property change events

- page_id: `170490744`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490744/Listening+to+property+change+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:57.075+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *PropertyChangeEvents* support provides the ability to listen to the event by different scopes. The scope depends on how the listener is registered:

- If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.
- If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.
- If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.
- If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.

The *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:

- To listen for the whole repository, use:

```java

```

This listener will get all the property change events from any element.

- To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is performed, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.

- To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.

- To listen for any property changes of the specific element type, use:

```java

```

This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.

*EventSupport* could be disabled from the event firing:

- To check if it is enabled, use:

```java

```

- To stop/ start event firing, use:

```java

```

These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.

###### **Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:**

```java

```

###### **Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The <em>PropertyChangeEvents</em> support provides the ability to listen to the event by different scopes. The scope depends on how the listener is registered:</p><ul><li>If the listener is registered at the whole repository, it receives events about the changes in all the elements.</li><li>If the listener is registered at the specific element, it receives events about the changes in any property of this element.</li><li>If the listener registered with the specific property at the element, it receives events about the changes in this property.</li><li>If the listener is registered with the specific property for the element type, it receives events about the changes in all the elements of the specific type.</li></ul><p>The <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> should be registered to receive these events. There are several different ways to register a listener:</p><ul><li>To listen for the whole repository, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffb93836-0efa-4a84-81ac-387a95a341c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, (RefObject)null);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will get all the property change events from any element.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"> </p><ul><li>To listen for any delete operation in the repository and get notified before the delete action is performed, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf20a9ef-fe34-40a0-9f46-3d0b55acfcc1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepositoryListenerRegistry().addPropertyChangeListener(listener, UML2MetamodelConstants.BEFORE_DELETE);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any model element is set to be deleted.</p><p> </p><ul><li>To listen for any property changes of the specific element, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c70a72de-22c3-4b7a-bae0-1256621e191d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		element.addPropertyChangeListener(listener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified when any element property is changed.</p><p> </p><ul><li>To listen for any property changes of the specific element type, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="caa780fd-eb62-494e-adcb-9e57cf81aba5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getSmartEventSupport().registerConfig(aClass, configs, listener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></div><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This listener will be notified, when any property of any element in the project with this type is changed. If “configs” is NULL, the listener will get all property change events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dce80e5-9c6e-41b1-b514-60eaa771f2dc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>EventSupport</em> could be disabled from the event firing:</p><ul><li>To check if it is enabled, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="971277da-d508-4924-a757-3d984f918130"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().isEnableEventFiring()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>To stop/ start event firing, use:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3628d536-4402-46cf-a926-c4a752530943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[		project.getRepository().getEventSupport().setEnableEventFiring(...)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><div class="WordSection1"><p>These examples show, how to create the property change listeners to listen to the different kind of properties.</p><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific element’s any property changes:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c9d224a-fac7-4b80-b6f2-e788242ff04e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            // evt.getPropertyName() is changed
        }

    });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></div><div class="WordSection3"><h4><strong>Listener for listening to the specific property (NAME) of the specific element:</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0763d8c8-9067-4161-97d8-11a5c302c053"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    element.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            if (PropertyNames.NAME.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // name is Changed
            }
        }
   });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490745 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: Listening to related elements in hierarchy events

- page_id: `170490745`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490745/Listening+to+related+elements+in+hierarchy+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:40:07.025+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener* class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig*, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.

The***SmartListenerConfig*class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.

Use the *SmartPropertyChangeListener*class*createSmartPropertyListener(...)* method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the *SmartListenerConfig*.

****If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the *removePropertyChangeListener(...**)***method.

****

**Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name**

```java

```

**Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the *"Is Abstract"* property**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There is a possibility to listen for changes of the elements, which are somehow related with the given element. For example, we want to be notified, when the element’s owner name is changed. The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartPropertyChangeListener</em> class is dedicated for the situations like this. The main idea of the solution is to provide the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartListenerConfig</em>, which will provide the chain of the property names to listen to.</p><p>The<em> </em><em>SmartListenerConfig </em>class provides static methods for the default configuration of property chains. This is useful, when listening for common property change events.</p><p>Use the <em>SmartPropertyChangeListener </em>class<em> createSmartPropertyListener(...)</em> method to create and register such listener for the particular element, which will be notified with events provided by the <em>SmartListenerConfig</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f47013c6-52be-40ae-ae38-90aad40b75da"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>If the listener is not needed anymore, unregister it using the <em>removePropertyChangeListener(...<strong>) </strong></em>method.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Example #1. Listen to the element owner's name</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e455947-8c4a-48c6-b2c2-35fe80d95f6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   		Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); // listen to the element's name 
        config.listenToNested(PropertyNames.OWNER).listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); //listen to the element owner's name
 
        // create the listener
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
               // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Example #2. Listen recursively to the owner's name and the <em>&quot;Is Abstract&quot;</em> property</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90ac467b-5be6-4826-9d81-0decdf8ca156"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    	Element element = ...; // some element
 
        SmartListenerConfig config = new SmartListenerConfig(); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.NAME); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.IS_ABSTRACT); 
        config.listenTo(PropertyNames.OWNER, config);
 
        SmartPropertyChangeListener.createSmartPropertyListener(element, new PropertyChangeListener()
        {
            public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
            {
                // the change event comes here
            }
        }, config);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490746 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00124: Listening to transaction commit events on session closing

- page_id: `170490746`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490746/Listening+to+transaction+commit+events+on+session+closing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:23:09.399+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Event support
- labels: ['transaction-commit-listener']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The *com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener* is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.

The *TransactionCommitListener* contains *transactionCommited(java.util.Collection<java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent>)***method, which provides a collection of all *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)* that were executed in a transaction.

****

**Create a custom transaction commit listener:**

```java
events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]>
```

This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.

###### **Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project**

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every session creating and closing starts and commits the model editing transaction. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.transaction.TransactionCommitListener</em> is a special listener, which is notified when the all changes inside a transaction are done and the transaction is closed.</p><p>The <em>TransactionCommitListener</em> contains <em>transactionCommited(java.util.Collection&lt;java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent&gt;)</em><em> </em>method, which provides a collection of all <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent(s)</em> that were executed in a transaction.</p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Create a custom transaction commit listener:</strong></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30d64b3d-3441-4403-a926-9b85f42af2ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class MyTransactionListener implements TransactionCommitListener
    {
        public Runnable transactionCommited(final Collection<PropertyChangeEvent> events)
        {
            return new Runnable()
            {
                public void run()
                {
                    for (PropertyChangeEvent event : events)
                    {
                        if (UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED.equals(event.getPropertyName()))
                        {
                            Object source = event.getSource();
                            if (source instanceof Property)
                            {
                                Property property = (Property) source; Element owner = property.getOwner();
                                if (owner instanceof Classifier)
                                {
                                    Classifier propertyOwner = (Classifier) owner;
                                    propertyOwner.setName("Contains (" + propertyOwner.getAttribute().size() + ") attributes");
                                }

 
                                // additionally for this Property we register listener to listen for any property changes in this Element properties.
                                property.addPropertyChangeListener(new DerivedValuePropertyChangeListener());
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            };
       }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>This “transaction commit listener” checks, if a new property is created in a classifier and updates the classifier’s (a property owner) name. All changes are done in the same session.</p><h4><strong>Register the custom “transaction commit listener” into the project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e23f7d2b-8475-417a-8dbc-c98449059aa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    MyTransactionListener transactionListener = new MyTransactionListener(); 
    TransactionManager transactionManager = project.getRepository().getTransactionManager();
    transactionManager.addTransactionCommitListener(transactionListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="491fee21-8713-4bf9-a6c9-2219abb1169b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490901 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: MagicDraw file format

- page_id: `170490901`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490901/MagicDraw+file+format
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:12.903+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['zip-based-file-format', 'plain-text-file-format', 'xmi']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0

MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension "mdr".

##### File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up

The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard.

A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.

###### Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)

A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:

- Meta information about entries records.properties.
- Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.
- Project structure information:

*com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project*.

- Shared and not shared uml model information:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model,*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model*

- Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.
- Project options ends with

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and*

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.*

- Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.

###### Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)

Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.

****

###### Changes Related to Diagrams

Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension <mdOwnedDiagrams>. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The <mdOwnedDiagrams> extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.

Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named <modelExtension>. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in <mdOwnedViews>. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:

**Diagram > DiagramRepresentationObject > DiagramContentsDescriptor > BinaryObject > BinaryObject. streamContentID**

****

###### Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor

```xml
diagram.ecore
	CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo
	
		
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
			
		
	
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3>File format in MagicDraw 3.6-17.0</h3><p>MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) file was a plain xmi file with extensions used to save diagrams information and project structure information (a mount table). The mdzip file was a compressed xmi file. The code engineering information was saved in the different file with extension &quot;mdr&quot;.</p><h3>File format in MagicDraw 17.0.1 and up</h3><p>The inner project structure as well as the file format has been changed in version 17.0.1. An XMI version is upgraded to 2.4 with corresponding changes required by this standard. </p><p>A project is a collection of xmi, text, and binary resources. A native file format contains all resources saved as separate files. All these files are collected to one zip file.</p><h4>Zip Based File Format (mdzip, xml.zip)</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A zip file (xml.zip, mdzip) contains at least the following entries:</span></p><ul><li>Meta information about entries records.properties.</li><li>Project identifier starts with PROJECT-, for example, PROJECT-f41072fa-d1aa-4e90-b7d9- fdd8862ed8af.</li><li>Project structure information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project</em>.</p><ul><li>Shared and not shared uml model information: </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.shared_model, </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model.model</em></p><ul><li>Proxies information backup of other external resources. Starts with proxy.</li><li>Project options ends with </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.personalprojectoptions and </em></p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.options.commonprojectoptions.</em></p><ul><li>Diagrams and other “binary” resources, entry names do not contain any meaningful information, just resource id, like 80a7058a-91b1-41fb-9a3a-cd7a8c6be52d.</li></ul><p> </p><h4>Plain Text File Format (xml, mdxml)</h4><p>Export to a non zip file formats like .xml or .mdxml is a combination of all zip file entries into the one plain xmi file. In this file, a uml model is saved as it was in earlier versions, while other information is saved as xmi:Extension.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Changes Related to Diagrams</h4><p>Until version 17.0.1, diagrams were saved as an xmi extension. All diagrams were saved under one extension &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt;. Both a diagram model and diagram symbols were saved in this extension. The &lt;mdOwnedDiagrams&gt; extension was saved outside a uml:Model element.</p><p>Version 17.0.1 saves the diagram element as a separate element extension named &lt;modelExtension&gt;. This extension is stored in a place where the diagram element is serialized (inside a diagram owner). If one owner has more than one diagram, they may be written in the one extension. In this extension, a diagram element is saved as a normal model element, and all its attributes and references are saved according xmi rules. A diagram element contains DiagramRepresentationObject, which contains information about a diagram type, required features, and so on. DiagramRepresentationObject contains DiagramContentsDescriptor, which describes used elements in a diagram and contains BinaryObject. BinaryObject has the attribute “streamContentID” which identifies a zip entry for diagram symbols. All information is stored in &lt;mdOwnedViews&gt;. The following schema presents finding diagram symbols entry id:<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Diagram &gt; DiagramRepresentationObject &gt; DiagramContentsDescriptor &gt; BinaryObject &gt; BinaryObject. streamContentID</strong></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><strong style="color: rgb(61,61,61);line-height: 1.6666666;"><br /></strong></p><h4>Genmodel for DiagramRepresentationObject and DiagramContentsDescriptor</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da45d653-b7b0-4f71-aaa4-06f4252b8180"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<genmodel:GenModel xmi:version="2.0"
xmlns:xmi="http://www.omg.org/XMI" xmlns:ecore="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore"
xmlns:genmodel="http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/GenModel" modelDirectory="/com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation/src"
modelPluginID="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation" modelName="Diagram
updateClasspath="false"
rootExtendsInterface="org.eclipse.emf.cdo.CDOObject" 
rootExtendsClass="org.eclipse.emf.internal.cdo.CDOObjectImpl"
reflectiveDelegation="true" importerID="org.eclipse.emf.importer.ecore"
featureDelegation="Reflective"
containmentProxies="true" complianceLevel="5.0" copyrightFields="false"
usedGenPackages="../../com.nomagic.ci.metamodel.project/model/binary.genmodel#//binary"
classNamePattern="">
	<foreignModel>diagram.ecore</foreignModel>
	<modelPluginVariables>CDO=org.eclipse.emf.cdo</modelPluginVariables>
	<genPackages prefix="Diagram" basePackage="com.nomagic.magicdraw.foundation"
	disposableProviderFactory="true"
	ecorePackage="diagram.ecore#/">
		<genClasses image="false" ecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//
		AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject">
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/ID"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/type"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/umlType"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramProperties"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/initialFrameSizeSet"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/requiredFeature"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//
			AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramContents"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//AbstractDiagramRepresentationObject/diagramStyleID"/></genClasses>
			<genClassesecoreClass="diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor">
			<genFeatures property="None" notify="false" createChild="false"
			ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/representation"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterName"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute
			diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/exporterVersion"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/usedElements"/>
			<genFeatures property="None" children="true" createChild="true" ecoreFeature="ecore:EReference diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/ binaryObject"/>
			<genFeatures createChild="false" ecoreFeature="ecore:EAttribute diagram.ecore#//DiagramContentsDescriptor/contentHash"/>
		</genClasses>
	</genPackages>
</genmodel:GenModel>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490970 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00126: MagicDraw-specific extensions

- page_id: `170490970`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490970/MagicDraw-specific+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:27:56.770+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1263120387

INLINE

1263120388

INLINE

1263120386

INLINE

**On this page**

1263120388

INLINE

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG2024xR1'] describes how REST API can be used to manipulate data in Teamwork Cloud at the Eclipse modeling framework (EMF) level. However, usingthe API at the EMF level may become difficult when working with larger projects and more complex compositions.

Therefore, to facilitate the most common model manipulation tasks, Teamwork Cloud REST API has MagicDraw-specific extensions that help minimize the number of calls necessary to find the primary MagicDraw UML model within project decomposition.

##### Inspecting model decomposition

Normally, MagicDraw has different kinds of models within Teamwork Cloud resources. The term “models” here refers to the data structure required to store MagicDraw-specific project information that the tool needs when loading up projects on the client-side. Currently, Teamwork Cloud REST API exposes two kinds of such models – **decomposition** and **UML**, as shown in the code below. You can retrieve a list of available models within the master branch and chosen resource revision by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models*.

```text

```

You can further inspect the content of each of the models by issuing GET to */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/{modelId}*, where *modelId* is **decomposition** or **uml** respectively.

##### Decomposition model

This model lists all of the Used Project hierarchy within the chosen resource. The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/decomposition* is shown below.

```text

```

The main root project which the call is executed against has a type of **models:PrimaryProject**. You can find all of its directly used projects within the **models:uses** array. Since the decomposition model covers the full hierarchy, it includes both locally used projects (usually concerning Standard/System profiles) and server projects.

Each of the directly used projects has a type of **models:UsedProject** that has its own decomposition, hence enabling the full breakdown of usage structure from the primary project perspective covering both, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The **id** attribute refers to the project ID of **esiproject:EsiProject** type, not the UML model root element.

##### UML model

Different from **decomposition** model previously described, the **UML** model provides a flat view of the structure of the target model. Note that the term **project** should not be confused with the term **UML** model here. The purpose of the **UML** model is to expose direct links to root elements of UML models in primary projects, and both of its, directly and indirectly, used projects.

The excerpt of the results after calling */resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/models/uml*is shown below.

```text

```

The **models:PrimaryModel** type object provides a direct link to the main project’s UML model root element via the **models:root** attribute. Compared to the EMF-based approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG2024xR1'], using the above REST API extension is a matter of a single call and requires only the resource ID and revision number to be known in advance.

1288072285

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='General convention' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model manipulation' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490888 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: Managing code engineering sets

- page_id: `170490888`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490888/Managing+code+engineering+sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:21:17.300+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Code engineering using API
- labels: ['code-engineering-manager', 'java-code-engineering-manager', 'java-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringManager* class provides static API methods for creating, removing, reversing, generating, and adjusting code-engineering settings. The *CodeEngineeringManager* class is used for managing code engineering sets.

When creating the code engineering set, the following fields are required:

- Language and dialect IDs. All languages and dialects IDs are stored in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringConstants class.
- The name of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code engineering set' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .
- The project, where code engineering set will be added.
- The working package.
- The working directory.

New *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* can be created directly via *CodeEngineeringManager*using the *CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String,**Project, Package, java.lang.String)***method.

The language and dialect can be selected from the *CodeEngineeringConstants* class. The dialect is required for DDL and C++ languages.

**•**null *workingPackage*means, that the code engineering working package is *Model* by default

**•**null *workingDirectory*means, that the code engineering working directory is the modeling tool install root

To remove the instance of *CodeEngineeringSet* use the *CodeEngineeringManager.removeCodeEngineeringSet(**CodeEngineeringSet)***method.

All Code Engineering Sets can be retrieved by the *CodeEngineeringManager.getAllCodeEngineeringSets(**Project)***method.

To perform generation of the CodeEngineeringSet object use the *CodeEngineeringManager.generate(**Project**, boolean)***method.

To perform the reverse engineering use the *CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(**Project**, boolean)***method.

###### ****

###### **Languag****e Specific Options**

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.JavaCodeEngineeringManager* provides methods to set Java specific options.These options applie to all code engineering sets in the project.

To add a classpaths for the java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(**Project, java.lang.String[])*

Setting a new classpath will override the old one.

To get applied classpaths for java code engineering sets, use *JavaCodeEngineeringManager.getJavaClasspath(**Project)*.

To resolve collection generics when reversing the java code, *CodeEngineeringManager.setResolveCollectionGenerics(**Project**, boolean)*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <em>CodeEngineeringManager</em> class is used for managing code engineering sets.</p><p>When creating the code engineering set, the following fields are required:</p><ul><li>Language and dialect IDs. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490789 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: Managing compartments

- page_id: `170490789`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490789/Managing+compartments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:00:54.144+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some presentation elements displays information in so called compartments. For example UML Class shape has attributes, operations, ports compartments.

Compartments can show only a subset of information. The rest information can be hidden. For example show only a couple of specific Operations on a Class shape in the diagram.

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager* for managing the visibility of compartment entries.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\compartmentedit

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Some presentation elements displays information in so called compartments. For example UML Class shape has attributes, operations, ports compartments.</p><p>Compartments can show only a subset of information. The rest information can be hidden. For example show only a couple of specific Operations on a Class shape in the diagram.</p><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.CompartmentManager</em> for managing the visibility of compartment entries.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\compartmentedit</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="becf631e-811b-484c-90d3-30aaa50ab94c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490830 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: Managing hyperlinks

- page_id: `170490830`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490830/Managing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-31T16:09:18.603+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing.

It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.

See the following pages for more information:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>UML models may have a different kind of hyperlinks assigned to model elements. There is API for managing these hyperlinks - you can assign new hyperlinks, remove existing. </p><p>It is also possible to register your own hyperlink handler for some custom hyperlink type.</p><p>See the following pages for more information:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8a88dff7-44c7-44b9-af31-4dfaeca31809" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490828 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API allows to change how navigation in a model is handled. Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements.

See these pages for more details:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing double-click behavior' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing hyperlinks' space='DEVG2024xR1']

Using that API you can create and manage hyperlinks, change the mouse double-click behavior on elements. </p><p>See these pages for more details:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Customizing double-click behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing hyperlinks" /></ac:link> </li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490931 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00131: MD Customization for SysML Profile

- page_id: `170490931`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490931/MD+Customization+for+SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:05.094+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile* to use this API class.

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.
- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.

****

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.</p><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p>Usage includes:</p><ul><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li></ul><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.</p><p>For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490832 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: MDEL hyperlink

- page_id: `170490832`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490832/MDEL+hyperlink
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:58:19.204+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model > Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.

For that purpose the **mdel://** protocol is introduced.

Use c*om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils* to retrieve mdel URL of elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.</p><p>For that purpose the <strong>mdel://</strong> protocol is introduced. </p><p>Use c<em>om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em> to retrieve mdel URL of elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490686 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: MDP protocol

- page_id: `170490686`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490686/MDP+protocol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:55.690+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Adding new functionality > Working with icons
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler **mdp://**for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project.

Usage samples:

- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.
- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.

**URL syntax**

| mdp://resource/ | Use the "resource" host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the activitydiagram.png icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png |
| --- | --- |
| mdp://attachedFile/ | Use the "attachedFile" host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID "_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288", use such URL:mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler <strong>mdp:// </strong>for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project. </p><div><p>Usage samples:</p><ul><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.</li><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.</li></ul><p> </p><p><strong>URL syntax</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td>mdp://resource/</td><td><p>Use the &quot;<em>resource</em>&quot; host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the <em>activitydiagram.png</em> icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:</p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png</span></pre></td></tr><tr><td>mdp://attachedFile/</td><td><p><span>Use the &quot;<em>attachedFile</em>&quot; host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID &quot;<em>_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</em>&quot;, use such URL:</span></p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</span></pre></td></tr></tbody></table></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490881 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: Merging and differencing

- page_id: `170490881`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490881/Merging+and+differencing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:38.963+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:

- Merge - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.
- Compare - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip once you have the modeling tool installed.

Project merging consists of three steps:

1. Discovering project changes (differences) . The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.
2. Accepting / rejecting changes . All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.
3. Applying accepted changes . Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).

There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.

The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****You can find the code examples:

- for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\merge
- for Compare API: < installation_direc tory>\openapi\examples\compare

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip</em> once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.</li><li><strong>Compare</strong> - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip</em> once you have the modeling tool installed.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Project merging consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><strong>Discovering project changes (differences)</strong>. The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.</li><li><strong>Accepting / rejecting changes</strong>. All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.</li><li><strong>Applying accepted changes</strong>. Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).</li></ol><p>There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.</p><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MergeDomain.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Merging and differencing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples:</p><ul><li>for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\merge</em></li><li>for Compare API: &lt;<em>installation_direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\compare</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490968 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: Model manipulation

- page_id: `170490968`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490968/Model+manipulation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:27:56.814+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1043546914

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1043546918

INLINE

1043546912

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1043546917

INLINE

Teamwork Cloud operates at the EMF level, while MagicDraw operates at the UML level, which is on top of EMF. REST API, which is the Teamwork Cloud API, also operates at the EMF level.

Therefore, Teamwork Cloud is not affected by UML-specific implementation, and all derived properties may not be saved in the Teamwork Cloud database. The best-known derived property is *owner*. This is the result when there is no such “*setOwner”*in REST API.

Even though all non-derived properties are saved in the Teamwork Cloud database, they are saved as raw EMF data. The user is required to have knowledge about UML models to manipulate data. For example, an applied stereotype is saved as a hierarchy of *InstanceSpecification*, *Slot*, and *ValueSpecification*.

This section describes how to traverse into a UML model and create some elements in it.

##### MagicDraw project

In Teamwork Cloud terminology, a MagicDraw project is referred to as a *resource*. Although REST API can be used to create a Teamwork Cloud resource, it is only a bare project MagicDraw cannot read. A MagicDraw project is composed of many MagicDraw-specific meta-data such as project options, primary project, and used projects configuration. The best way to create a MagicDraw project is to create it by MagicDraw and submit it to the Teamwork Cloud server.

##### MagicDraw primary project

To traverse through a UML model, the primary project must be identified. The model data starts at the revision level. Issuing GET to */revisions/{revisionId}* shows the first-level object in the revision. UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in **rootObjectIDs**.

```text

```

Among these entries, the first element with the **esiproject:EsiProject** type specifies the primary project named **main decomposition project**. Other **esiproject:EsiProject** objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element **429f969a**. You need to load element **429f969a** and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in **kerml:esiData**. There will be an element named **UML Model**. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.

```text

```

Take the first **esiproject:EsiDataSection** from the feature's sections list, which is **b3e68e8e**. The excerpt of the **b3e68e8e** data is shown below.

```text

```

The first ID in the **rootElements** list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the **uml:Package** type or derived from it.

##### Traversing to a MagicDraw model

Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from */elements/{elementId}*. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in **kerml:ownedElement**.

Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to */elements*. In this case, you need to put UUID in the **uuid.txt****file to load elements.

```text

```

The command to load those elements is:

```bash

```

In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.

##### Creating elements

To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the **UMLClass** attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set **owningPackage** in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.

There are two URLs to create element(s), */resources/{resourceId}/elements* and */resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}*. Please note that the *elementId* in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:

- Specified from the *elementId* in */resources/{resourceId}/elements/*{elementId}**. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element. text
- Specified from kerml:nsURI .
- Specifying the whole Ecore in **kerml:ecore**. This mode is rarely used. text\r\n\r\n 
 <\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]>

The following script creates an element:

```text

```

Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter *batch=true* in the URL. The script is shown below:

```text

```

##### Applied stereotypes

Applied stereotypes stored in the **appliedStereotype** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.

```text

```

To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.

```text

```

The UUID **8dbdc804** is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:

```text

```

##### Tagged values

Tagged values stored in the **taggedValue** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.

```text

```

To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.

To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:

```text

```

The command will be:

```bash

```

UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in <strong>rootObjectIDs</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56ba1f1d-55d3-42ed-8c75-d9773deaf9ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
