    "pickedRevision": -1,
    "description": "Branch \"xx\" created",
    "@context": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schemas/revision",
    "directParent": 3,
    "dependencies": [],
    "rootObjectIDs": 
	[
      	"429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950",
		"ec6060a3-f3d9-482b-93a3-32af9e19202c",
		"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d",     
		"f7c3ae92-af44-4dab-8163-a199ca05c006",
		"ba3d0700-1062-4baf-a1df-a55a4f31ce54",
		"0f14cd2d-2fd0-4523-950c-627d59e1a43d",
      	"7cd22dea-aaf8-4e08-bd67-5bd975c3f06a",
     	"af1042fa-8b1b-4cf2-bb7d-98dd1b881da3",
		"6d24e5e7-cdff-4e9e-85b8-28b3088f85b6",
		"243020e5-da6c-4896-b32a-fcba0e93ac8d",
		"f7449238-5cd1-41eb-9025-040210b02d93",
      	"4d2459a1-49dc-4eb7-aa82-9bbb4a76b038",
		"b242613d-957e-4aec-9333-e5938f50b2ab",  
		"9b953064-e422-4391-b7d9-43a2d4f14a32",
		"fc997cfd-23c5-4d0b-9953-06667dcde0dd",
		"29d9416b-ead5-4a9d-b530-b23de836f1b8",
		"7af3f24b-2da9-4b31-94b3-a87f15747296"
	],
	"createdDate": "1533051367",
	"ID": "",
    "artifacts": "artifacts"
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Among these entries, the first element with the <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> type specifies the primary project named <strong>main decomposition project</strong>. Other <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element <strong>429f969a</strong>. You need to load element <strong>429f969a</strong> and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in <strong>kerml:esiData</strong>. There will be an element named <strong>UML Model</strong>. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a231d404-e71f-45b7-961f-0acc426c1902"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA["kerml:esiData":
{
	"name": "UML Model",
    "namespace": "com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model",
	"project": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
	},
	"internalVersion": "1",
	"version": "17.0",
	"sections": 
	[
		{
			"@id": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
        }
	]
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Take the first <strong>esiproject:EsiDataSection</strong> from the feature's sections list, which is <strong>b3e68e8e</strong>. The excerpt of the <strong>b3e68e8e</strong> data is shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60584186-0d26-423e-a1e2-d829ed92b6d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"kerml:name": "model",
	"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5/elements",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/ns/magicdraw/esiproject/1.0",
	"@type": "esiproject:EsiDataSection",
    "kerml:owner": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
    },
    "kerml:revision": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5",
    "@context": 
	{
		"esiproject:EsiDataSection": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/esiproject/2014345/EsiDataSection",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325"
    },
    "kerml:ownedElement": [],
    "kerml:modifiedTime": "20180731223607ICT",
    "kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"featuredBy": 
		{
			"@id": "c9256728-4617-4097-8e9e-dc63e2823bf8"
		},
		"rootElements":
		[
			"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d"
		],
		"name": "model",
      	"project":
		{
			"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
      	},
		"properties": []
	},
	"kerml:resource": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18",
    "kerml:esiID": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a",
	"@id": "#b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first ID in the <strong>rootElements</strong> list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the <strong>uml:Package</strong> type or derived from it.</p><h3>Traversing to a MagicDraw model</h3><p>Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from <em>/elements/{elementId}</em>. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in <strong>kerml:ownedElement</strong>.</p><p>Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to <em>/elements</em>. In this case, you need to put UUID in the <strong>uuid.txt</strong><em> </em>file to load elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33153f09-c2b7-48f5-a3ed-31c4b49e6b7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[4eeb2e6d-9cbc-4f59-9d74-69263e52ba54,520bc74d-b5b4-40d5-87cb-9d0b8aba5d2e,7a057ae7-6281-462d-9dcd-de9931633f5c,a6212656-5ad1-4de6-a47f-db656c2c25fd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command to load those elements is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a018be71-340d-4466-a56e-6e1bb98c8511"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: text/plain" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @uuid.txt --insecure "https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.</p><h3>Creating elements</h3><p>To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the <strong>UMLClass</strong> attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set <strong>owningPackage</strong> in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.</p><p>There are two URLs to create element(s), <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements</em> and <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}</em>. Please note that the <em>elementId</em> in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specified from the <em>elementId</em> in <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements/<em>{elementId}</em></em>. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cda4bcb3-e1df-42bb-b58f-96a53a031c9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/magicdraw/UML/2.5.1",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"owningPackage": 
		{
			"@id": "757be712-f397-404d-a5ff-b97567eb240f"
        },
        "name": "c3"
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specified from <strong>kerml:nsURI</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specifying the whole Ecore in <strong>kerml:ecore</strong>. This mode is rarely used.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="352b1dcd-d2c6-4040-970f-2dde4c18d7ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "ikml:Container",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"name": "txdhdhdhd",
		"uri": "http://www.chula.ac.th",
    },
	"kerml:ecore": "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\r\n<ecore:EPackage xmi:version=\"2.0\" xmlns:xmi=\"http://www.omg.org/XMI\" 
	xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\"\r\n
	xmlns:ecore=\"http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore\"
	name=\"ikml\" nsURI=\"http://www.nomagic.com/ikml/1.0\"
	nsPrefix=\"ikml\">\r\n 
	<eClassifiers xsi:type=\"ecore:EClass\" name=\"Element\" abstract=\"true\"><\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following script creates an element:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf6e33b-2609-416e-b360-e1c3d88c33f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter <em>batch=true</em> in the URL. The script is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b654223-c3a5-495c-baa9-8180037d07db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-"https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements/${elementId}?batch=true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Applied stereotypes</h3><p>Applied stereotypes stored in the <strong>appliedStereotype</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c58e346-18a9-4370-b75d-d3dac640b749"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
		"mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e88402a-1b6d-42d0-8124-a5c56c4611c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@context": {
		"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/workspaces/59068af0-a7ae-4663-
b972-3c58a2fde23f/resources/b0585059-c4e3-4d82-90d9-c5386045bb2e/branches/
5dfb978f-0984-47ea-aaec-bdbb5a945687/elements/",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325",
		"uml:Class": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/uml/2014345/Class"
	},
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The UUID <strong>8dbdc804</strong> is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a519b65-4594-4d3b-a2f9-5bf201fa4a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$ curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b
..\get\cookie.txt --insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Tagged values</h3><p>Tagged values stored in the <strong>taggedValue</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790701f-dcda-46eb-9c21-43ede212c123"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
        "mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "d9388ec6-ddca-4c18-9a5c-409d8649731f"
            },
            {
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="048b897c-21c3-47d3-a4d2-a1796364e739"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f2f7a78-453b-49dc-a35d-26fccf8dadc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:StringTaggedValue",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"tagDefinition": {
			"@id": "657a3511-d012-4134-af5d-411a6df78745"
        },
		"taggedValueOwner": {
			"@id": "515073dd-fe25-4327-bfc2-6466375a50f7"
        },
		"value": [
			"somevaluefromrest"
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e2fd526-78f4-4ca3-bc53-f94b7925d50e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b ..\get\cookie.txt
--insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dbb4036-6351-4e7a-a701-2b65f4490680"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043575942</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="REST APIs" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="General convention" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Authentication" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="MagicDraw-specific extensions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490729 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00136: Model traversing, Visitor pattern

- page_id: `170490729`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490729/Model+traversing+Visitor+pattern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:59:03.676+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Here is an example of how to collect all children from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and avoid recursion using a simple *for*cycle:

```java
children = new ArrayList(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]>
```

It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* utility:

- byScope()
- byType()
- byTypeRecursively()

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Here is an example of how to collect all children from the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and avoid recursion using a simple <em>for </em>cycle:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1480f664-32bf-4755-aa6c-63eb86e14427"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Element> children = new ArrayList<Element>(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder</em> utility:</p><ul><li><em>byScope()</em></li><li><em>byType()</em></li><li><em>byTypeRecursively()</em></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87af5a4-da9b-49c0-aab8-6991bc3cdde8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="674518bd-42f1-4fec-be9d-51cc04f7a18e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490734 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00137: ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `170490734`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490734/ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:15:41.228+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model > Model traversing, Visitor pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor* is an enhanced implementation of the *Visitor*pattern and a subclass of *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor*, used for visiting model elements.

Every *visit<element_metatype>*method calls a *visit<element_metatype>*method for a super type.

Model elements *visit…*methods have an additional context parameter of the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext* type. The visitor context is used to track what super type *visit<element_metatype> m*ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.

For example, you can put some code into *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(**Classifier)***and it will be executed for all subclasses of a *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier*.

```java
it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\statistics*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor</em> is an enhanced implementation of the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Visitor </em>pattern and a subclass of <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor</em>, used for visiting model elements. </p><p>Every <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method calls a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method for a super type.</p><p>Model elements <em>visit… </em>methods have an additional context parameter of the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext</em> type. The visitor context is used to track what super type <em>visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; m</em>ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.</p><p><br /></p><p>For example, you can put some code into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(</em><em>Classifier)</em><em> </em>and it will be executed for all subclasses of a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7085da27-f3ae-415f-9197-30427949c8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ModelVisitor myVisitor = new ModelHierarchyVisitor()
    {
        public void visitClassifier(Classifier element, VisitorContext context)
        {
          	//this is my classifier
        }
    };
    Project project = ...;
    Package root = project.getPrimaryModel(); 
    Iterator<Element> it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\statistics</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490621 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: Modeling Tools Developer Guide

- page_id: `170490621`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490621/Modeling+Tools+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-08T16:12:24.871+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the generated javadoc ([https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024xRefresh1/](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024xRefresh1/)), you will find a detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

This guide describes the Open Java API of MagicDraw or a Cameo Suite product, such as Cameo Systems Modeler, and provides instructions on how to implement custom plugins, add actions to the menus or toolbars, change UML model elements as well as create new patterns. Though MagicDraw is used in examples mainly, other modeling tools, since they are based on the MagicDraw modeling platform, perform the same.

The program core code and used libraries are packaged in jars files that are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\lib* (and its subdirectories).

Some plugins also exposes Open API. Plugins libraries can be located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\<plugin_name>*

The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:

- OpenAPI – a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.
- InternalAPI – a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.
- NotAPI – not an API code, it may change in each build.

Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. Annotations of the type (class, interface, enum) declaration:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApiAll | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration only. Each declared member, constructor is annotated explicitly with the @OpenApi or @InternalApi annotation (see the following table). |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @NotApi | NotAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |

Annotations of the member, constructor declaration of the @OpenApi type:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |

Only code from the OpenAPI scope should be used to extend the modeling tool. All the InternalAPI and NotAPI scope code are additionally annotated with the *@Deprecated* annotation. This allows seeing the illegal API usage in all modern IDEs without an additional configuration.

We provide a set of plugin samples in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\examples*. By using these examples, you can find out on how to use an OpenAPI.

In the generated javadoc, you will find the detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\docs*.

Plugins libraries can be located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins\&lt;plugin_name&gt;</em></p><p> The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:</p><ul><li><strong>OpenAPI </strong>– a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">InternalAPI </strong>– a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">NotAPI </strong>– not an API code, it may change in each build.</li></ul><p>Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. <ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To move a symbol on the diagram to another location, you can use the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56154ac-0627-4845-9653-ee18d51a0f3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(PresentationElement view, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17822cd1-f81b-4e4a-953a-f7e105178d09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement view = ...; 
    SessionsManager.getInstance().createSession("Move symbol"); 
    PresentationsElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(view, new Point(50, 50));
    SessionsManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab01b1f8-334a-42da-9009-3d61172a088e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    movePresentationElements(List<PresentationElement> views, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolmove</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b483f3d-c1a6-45f7-9612-a04919e10de5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490923 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: Multi-threading

- page_id: `170490923`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490923/Multi-threading
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:37.685+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The majority of the application code (including OpenAPI) is not thread safe. Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running action with progress' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="484fea0b-5751-4dfb-9861-690ecfc262e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="830e6398-0ff5-46dd-b2fb-15df811ffd40" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Running action with progress" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00142: Notification of presentation element draw

- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification* notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification</em> notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efd26006-93c1-44dc-b100-15ef466ace73"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // An element removal listener
    final PropertyChangeListener removeListener = new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        { 
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.REMOVE.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // symbol removed
            }
        }
    };
 
    // An element drawing listener
    SymbolDrawListener symbolDrawListener = new SymbolDrawListener()
    {
        public void symbolAdded(DiagramPresentationElement diagram, PresentationElement symbol, String actionID)
        {
            // A symbol added
            // A register listener
            symbol.addPropertyChangeListener(removeListener);
        }
    };
    
    // A register draw listener
    SymbolDrawNotification symbolDrawNotification =SymbolDrawNotification.getSymbolDrawNotification(project);
    symbolDrawNotification.addSymbolDrawListener(symbolDrawListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194643810 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00143: OAuth 2.0 authentication

- page_id: `194643810`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/194643810/OAuth+2.0+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-11-08T12:15:36.687+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.

Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.

### OAuth 2.0 integration

OAuth 2.0 specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:

- Authorization code
- Implicit
- Resource Owner Password Credentials
- Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant for more information)

### OAuth 2.0 client registration

To register OAuth 2.0 clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OAuth 2.0**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS2024xR1']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). 
Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='TWCloud2024xR1']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```text
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4deecac1-fe49-4c9f-84b0-27c064f2168c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.</span></p><p><span>Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.</span></p><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">OAuth 2.0 integration</span></h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7784a74b-75b6-4997-9a8b-3c40f14c492f"><ac:rich-text-body>See <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749">OAuth 2.0 specification</a> for more details.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using <em>https://&lt;server_host&gt;:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration</em> endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.</span></p><p>Example JSON:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf15d67d-779a-4d39-8131-3351b1520d4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{

  "response_types_supported": [

    "token",

    "code"

  ],

  "device_authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/device_code",

  "jwks_uri": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/jwks.json",

  "grant_types_supported": [

    "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code",

    "refresh_token",

    "password",

    "authorization_code"

  ],

  "registration_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register",

  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [

    "client_secret_basic"

  ],

  "scopes_supported": [],

  "issuer": "https://localhost:8443/authentication",

  "authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/oauth2/authorize",

  "token_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/token"

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span>Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:</span></p><ul><li><span>Authorization code </span></li><li><span>Implicit</span></li><li><span>Resource Owner Password Credentials</span></li><li><span>Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see </span><a href="https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/device-code/">OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant</a><span> for more information)</span></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><h1>OAuth 2.0 client registration</h1><p><span>To register OAuth 2.0 clients:</span></p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li><span><strong>Use the web UI:</strong> go to the <strong>Settings </strong>app of Web Application Platform and search for <strong>OAuth 2.0 </strong>tab in the<em> </em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><strong>OAuth clients</strong><strong> </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>section.</span></li><li><span><strong>Use <em>registration_endpoint</em> API:</strong> (see the example JSON).<br />Example client registration request when using API:</span></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c55467c-6f7f-40d2-b3d7-f9cb12e1a9ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[POST https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e29a054-c385-40f2-8c3d-5088c5c7cd68"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f58c24d-34d3-4d65-9691-930fbebf89d4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Bearer <access token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490620 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: Open API Deprecation Policy

- page_id: `170490620`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490620/Open+API+Deprecation+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-15T08:39:19.881+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Overview:** This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for the deprecation of APIs within our system.

**Deprecation Process:**

1. **Notification:** APIs that are deprecated will be clearly marked as such in the API javadoc.
2. **Timeline:** Deprecated APIs can be removed in the next major version following their deprecation. For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated in version 2024x (or any 2024x RefreshX), it can be removed in version 2026x.
3. **Replacement APIs:** Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.

**Exceptional Cases:** In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.

**Review:** This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.

**Effective Version:** This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Overview:</strong> This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for the deprecation of APIs within our system.</p><p><strong>Deprecation Process:</strong></p><ol><li><p><strong>Notification:</strong> APIs that are deprecated will be clearly <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="40a81d41-feed-4dc8-918b-8ac1e40460c9">marked as such <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">in the API javadoc</span></ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p></li><li><p><strong>Timeline:</strong> Deprecated APIs can be removed in the next major version following their deprecation. For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="833d9cd5-af83-4d17-a89f-47dc8eb0c173">in version 2024x <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or any 2024x RefreshX)</span>, it can be removed in version 2026x.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li><p><strong>Replacement APIs:</strong> Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.</p></li></ol><p><strong>Exceptional Cases:</strong> In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.</p><p><strong>Review:</strong> This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.</p><p><strong>Effective Version:</strong> This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194643813 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00145: OpenID Connect authentication

- page_id: `194643813`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/194643813/OpenID+Connect+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-11-08T12:16:48.708+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OpenID Connect (further referred to as OIDC) protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of these users.

Authentication server behaves as the OIDC Identity Provider. Web applications that need to access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OIDC Service Provider functionality according to OIDC protocol specification.

### OpenID Connect integration

OpenID Connect specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oidc-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization flows:

- Authorization code flow
- Implicit flow
- Hybrid flow
- Device code flow (extension, see [Device Code Flow - Authorization](https://developers.onelogin.com/openid-connect/api/device-code-auth) for more information)

### OIDC client registration

To register OIDC clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OpenID Connect**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='TWCloud2024xR1']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='TWCloud2024xR1']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```bash
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Web applications that need to access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OIDC Service Provider functionality according to OIDC protocol specification.</span></p><h1><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OpenID Connect integration</span></h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fd1d489-dd1c-459e-8130-59146fd3c2b7"><ac:rich-text-body>See <a href="https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-basic-1_0.html">OpenID Connect specification</a> for more details.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.</span></p><p><span>Example JSON:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6549693e-eb4b-400e-9f3c-45d90018e297"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "response_types_supported": [
    "code token",
    "code id_token",
    "code",
    "id_token",
    "id_token token",
    "code id_token token"
  ],

  ],
  "subject_types_supported": [
    "public"
  ],
  "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported": [
    "RS256"
  ],
  "registration_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/register",
  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [
    "client_secret_basic"
  ],
  "scopes_supported": [
    "openid"
  ],
  "issuer": "https://localhost:8443/authentication",
  "authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/oidc/authorize",
  "token_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/token"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p><p><span>Authentication server supports the following authorization flows:</span></p><ul><li><span>Authorization code flow</span></li><li><span>Implicit flow</span></li><li><span>Hybrid flow</span></li><li><span>Device code flow (extension, see <span style="color: rgb(28,31,42);"><a href="https://developers.onelogin.com/openid-connect/api/device-code-auth">Device Code Flow - Authorization</a></span> for more information)</span></li></ul><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">OIDC client registration</span></h1><p><span>To register OIDC clients:</span></p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li><span><strong>Use the web UI:</strong> go to the <strong>Settings </strong>app of Web Application Platform and search for <strong>OpenID Connect </strong>tab in the<em> </em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><strong>OAuth clients</strong><strong> </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>section.</span></li><li><span><strong>Use <em>registration_endpoint</em> API:</strong> (see the example JSON).<br /></span>Example client registration request when using API:</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21093e5c-a719-472e-8bc9-b59774e0bbc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[POST https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/register

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1fe551d-0a65-4ad7-9275-0e44721b67a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span>You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span>Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h1><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d73af696-184e-4ad8-ade6-123651fb8611"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <ID token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490892 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00146: Oracle DDL generation and customization

- page_id: `170490892`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490892/Oracle+DDL+generation+and+customization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:38.911+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Oracle DDL script generation is based on the Velocity engine. It provides an ability to change the generated DDL script by changing the velocity template. We explain how the Oracle DDL generation works in our modeling tools and how to change the template for specific elements.

Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates. The Velocity documentation can be downloaded from [http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf](http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf).

For more information about Oracle DDL 11g, see [http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm](http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm).

The modeling tool Oracle DDL generation consists of the following three components:

- The Velocity engine. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.
- The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the CG Properties Editor dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the Properties command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in < program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template folder.
- Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.

To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It provides an ability to change the generated DDL script by changing the velocity template. We explain how the Oracle DDL generation works in our modeling tools and how to change the template for specific elements.</p><p>Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates. The Velocity documentation can be downloaded from <a href="http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf">http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf</a>. </p><p>For more information about Oracle DDL 11g, see <a href="http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm">http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm</a>.</p><p>The modeling tool Oracle DDL generation consists of the following three components:</p><ul><li> The Velocity engine. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.</li><li>The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Properties </strong>command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in &lt;<em>program </em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation folder&gt;\</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder.</li><li>Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.</li></ul><p>To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25b2885a-064a-4fc0-bac0-35a022134e4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="03a84b5e-cf4a-4758-b490-c4d3fa411bf6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490974 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=5 -->
## PAGE 00147: OSLC API

- page_id: `170490974`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490974/OSLC+API
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-05-13T11:38:15.424+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:

- OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.
- OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices .
- Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID} . Note that the elementID here is an Element Server ID.

**Note regarding migration**

The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the *server*field of the Teamwork Cloud *esi.oslc* configuration block in the *application.conf* file.

Teamwork Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following [OSLC Architecture Management (AM)](http://open-services.net/ns/am) and [OSLC Configuration Management](https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html)vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.

We expose the following model element properties:

- rdf:type ( http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type ) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource ( http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource ).
- dcterms:modified ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified ) - stands for the last element modification date.
- dcterms:identifier ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier ) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.
- dcterms:title ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/title ) - the name of the element

```xml
May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM
    50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237
    Engine
  
]]>
```

- Currently, querying services are not supported.
- Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.
- We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] . For this, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] .
- We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.

##### OSLC Configuration Management Support

OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of OSLC Configuration Management services is via the *<oslc_config:cmServiceProviders>* property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:

| Teamwork Cloud concept | OSLC Configuration Management concept |
| --- | --- |
| Cameo Model (.Master Resource) | Component |
| Branch | Stream |
| Tagged Commit | Baseline |
| Used Project (Branch) | Contribution of a Stream to a Stream |

##### **OAuth 1.0a authentication**

To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.

OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through Teamwork CloudAdmin as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='TWCloud2024xR1'].

Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):

```js

```

```js

```

Note, that keys generated following this approach will still need to be[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='TWCloud2024xR1']approved by an administrator through Teamwork CloudAdmin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29e5060b-9718-47f8-8cd7-1427d5129831"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:</span></p><ul><li>OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.</li><li>OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices</em>.</li><li>Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID}</em>.<br />Note that the <em>elementID</em> here is an Element Server ID.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Note regarding migration</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3ee46c6-ba1f-49b4-957f-b64ea1c9fea5">server</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em>field of the Teamwork Cloud <em>esi.oslc</em> configuration block in the <em>application.conf</em> file.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1fc8fdab-1c2c-4ca3-9015-33b7f61ad136">Teamwork</ac:inline-comment-marker> Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following <a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am">OSLC Architecture Management (AM)</a> and <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><a href="https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html">OSLC Configuration Management</a><span> </span></span>vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.</span></p><p>We expose the following model element properties:</p><ul><li>rdf:type (<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type">http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type</a>) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource (<a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource">http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource</a>).</li><li>dcterms:modified (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified">http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified</a>) - stands for the last element modification date.</li><li>dcterms:identifier (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier">http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier</a>) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.</li><li>dcterms:title (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/title">http://purl.org/dc/terms/title</a>) - the name of the element<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f154bf0-5a5c-4311-9f23-091bc174fbe7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample RDF/XML representation of element data</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rdf:RDF
    xmlns:oslc="http://open-services.net/ns/core#"    
    xmlns:oslc_am="http://open-services.net/ns/am#" 
    xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"
    xmlns:dcterms="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"  
    xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
    xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#">
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource"/>
    <oslc:serviceProvider rdf:resource="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/services"/>
    <dcterms:modified rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime">May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM</dcterms:modified>
    <dcterms:identifier rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237</dcterms:identifier>
    <dcterms:title rdf:parseType="Literal">Engine</dcterms:title>
  </rdf:Description>
</rdf:RDF>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ul><li><p>Currently, querying services are not supported.</p></li><li><p>Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.</p></li><li>We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ac:link>. For this, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Publishing other types of resources"><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[publish an OSLC resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">OSLC <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Configuration Management Support</span></span></h3><p>OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bb2c04dc-ef20-41c5-9764-299b17fab63d">OSLC Configuration Management services</ac:inline-comment-marker> is via the <em>&lt;oslc_config:cmServiceProviders&gt;</em> property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 47.1682%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 46.5384%;" /><col style="width: 53.5495%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;">Teamwork Cloud concept</th><th style="text-align: center;">OSLC Configuration Management concept</th></tr><tr><td>Cameo Model (.Master Resource)</td><td>Component</td></tr><tr><td>Branch</td><td>Stream</td></tr><tr><td>Tagged Commit</td><td>Baseline</td></tr><tr><td>Used Project (Branch)</td><td>Contribution of a Stream to a Stream</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4d1be4db-c9ac-49ef-8221-e21e5639b4b7">OAuth 1.0a authentication</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></strong></h3><p>To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.</p><p>OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81dd2474-b1c0-4b12-9130-db9c5401d9dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "name": "consumerNameGoesHere",
  "secret": "OAuthSecretGoesHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a2e2db9-97a7-4b9d-98f6-073474ff885b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "key": "generatedConsumerKeyShouldBeHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Note, that keys generated following this approach will <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bd78a089-e091-4aa8-8802-67266a59924c">still need to be </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><span class="inline-comment-marker">approved</span> by an administrator</ac:link-body></ac:link> through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490952 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00148: Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity

- page_id: `170490952`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490952/Parametric+Helper+as+executing+parametric+simulation+from+an+Activity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:58.977+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:

```text

```

An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the *CylinderPipe.mdzip* sample. The **action : ExecuteParametric** is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149034b5-c6af-4ed6-a2ae-39a65ae247a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.parametrics.utility.ParametricsHelper.executeObject(Object object);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the <em>CylinderPipe.mdzip</em> sample. The <strong>action : ExecuteParametric</strong> is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="567" ac:width="594"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavfe88aed5c24cf68dcde9f46c1f9f6e1a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490775 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00149: Paths

- page_id: `170490775`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490775/Paths
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:24.542+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: ['supplier', 'client', 'suplier-piont', 'client-point', 'break-point']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

706205016

INLINE

706205019

INLINE

706205017

INLINE

Paths are presentation elements created for such model elements as Relationships. Path has the following attributes:

- Supplier – the presentation element of a Relationship supplier. Use method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement.getSupplier() to access this element.
- Client – the presentation element of a Relationship client. Use method PathElement.getClient() to access this element.
- Supplier point – the connection point of a path and supplier element. Use method PathElement.getSupplierPoint() to access this point.
- Client point – the connection point of a path and client element. Use method PathElement.getClientPoint() to access this point.
- Break points – a list of path breaking points between supplier and client points. This list can be empty if path is straight. Use method PathElement.getBreakPoints() to access this list.

706205015

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MD2024xR1']
