<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490638 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00150: Plugin class loading

- page_id: `170490638`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490638/Plugin+class+loading
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:54.160+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins > Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All modeling tool plugins (classes and runtime libraries) are loaded by the **one** classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, conflicts appear in versions of plugin libraries or other), their descriptors should be defined to use own classloaders. In this case, the plugin classes are loaded by a separate classloader.

An optional property "class-lookup" controls how classes are loaded, if the plugin has its own classloader. If the value of this property is *LocalFirst*, the class is loaded from the plugin classpath even if such class is already loaded in the global core classloader of your modeling tool. This property is important if you want to use different versions of the same classes (libraries) used in the program core.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All modeling tool plugins (classes and runtime libraries) are loaded by the <strong>one</strong> classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, conflicts appear in versions of plugin libraries or other), their descriptors should be defined to use own classloaders. In this case, the plugin classes are loaded by a separate classloader.</p><p>An optional property &quot;class-lookup&quot; controls how classes are loaded, if the plugin has its own classloader. If the value of this property is <em>LocalFirst</em>, the class is loaded from the plugin classpath even if such class is already loaded in the global core classloader of your modeling tool. This property is important if you want to use different versions of the same classes (libraries) used in the program core.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490635 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00151: Plugin classes

- page_id: `170490635`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490635/Plugin+classes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:19:46.152+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins > Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin* is the base abstract class for any modeling tool plugin. A plugin written by a user must be extended from this class. Every plugin has its own descriptor set by a plugin's manager. A plugin has three special methods:

- a *init()* meth od is called on a program startup. The plugin must override this method and implement its own functionality there.
- a *close()* method is called on a program exit. The plugin must override this method and return the value true if the plugin is ready to exit. In other case, it should return the value false . If the plugin returns false , the program exit is canceled.
- a *isSupported()* method is called before the plugin initialization. If this method returns false , the plugin is not initialized. isSupported() may be used to check if the plugin can be started, for example, on this OS.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginUtils* to get references to loaded plugins. This is useful if you have dependencies among plugins and want to get a reference to other plugin object.

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor* is the class that provides information loaded from the *plugin.xml* file (a plugin descriptor) to the plugin. Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.getDescriptor() to get the descriptor.*

More information is available in javadoc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin</em> is the base abstract class for any modeling tool plugin. A plugin written by a user must be extended from this class. Every plugin has its own descriptor set by a plugin's manager. A plugin has three special methods:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">a <em>init()</em> </span> meth</span>od is called on a program startup. The plugin must override this method and implement its own functionality there.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>close()</em> </span> method is called on a program exit. The plugin must override this method and return the value <em>true</em> if the plugin is ready to exit. In other case, it should return the value <em>false</em>. If the plugin returns <em>false</em>, the program exit is canceled.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>isSupported()</em> </span> method is called before the plugin initialization. If this method returns <em>false</em>, the plugin is not initialized. <em>isSupported()</em> may be used to check if the plugin can be started, for example, on this OS.</li></ul><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginUtils</em> to get references to loaded plugins. This is useful if you have dependencies among plugins and want to get a reference to other plugin object.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline _mce_tagged_br" /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor </em> is</span> the class that provides information loaded from the <em>plugin.xml</em> file (a plugin descriptor) to the plugin. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.getDescriptor() to get the descriptor.</em></p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Plugins manager workflow" ac:alt="Plugins manager workflow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="plugins_manager_workflow.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin classes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490634 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00152: Plugin descriptor

- page_id: `170490634`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490634/Plugin+descriptor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:54.219+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins > Detail information
- labels: ['plugin-descriptor']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A plugin descriptor is a file written in XML and named *plugin.xml*. Each descriptor contains properties of the one plugin. The descriptor file should contain the definitions of elements of the one plugin.

The *plugin.xml* file consists of the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='DEVG2024xR1']

The following tables describe them in more details.

plugin**plugin**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | A plugin ID should be unique. It is used to identify a plugin by a plugin's manager internals and by requirements of other plugins. For example, my.first.plugin.0. |
| name | A plugin name. There is no strict rules applied to this attribute. For example, Example plugin. |
| version | A plugin version. The version can be used to check other plugins dependencies, if internalVersion is not defined. |
| internalVersion | A plugin internal version. It is used to check other plugins dependencies. |
| provider-name | A plugin provider name, which is a company or an author name. For example: No Magic. |
| class | A full qualified class name. The class must be derived from com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins. A plugin and stored in plugin runtime library. This class will be loaded and initialized by the plugin's manager. For example, myplugin.MyPlugin. |
| ownClassloader | Optional; the default value is false. Indicates, if to use an own plugin's classloader (separate from other plugins). All program plugins are loaded by the one classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, because of conflicts in plugin libraries versions or other), their descriptors must be defined to use own class-loaders. |
| class-lookup | Optional; possible values are LocalFirst, GlobalFirst, the default value is GlobalFirst. Specifies the priority of a "parent" class loader, if a plugin uses ownClassloader. LocalFirst forces to load classes from the plugin class loader even if such classes exist in the modeling tool core class path. This option should be used, if, in your plugin, you want to use different versions of same libraries that are used in the core. |

**Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| requires | A modeling tool API version required by the plugin. Plugins and their versions required by the plugin. |
| runtime | Runtime libraries needed by a plugin. |

**requiresrequires** 
 **Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| api | A required modeling tool API version. |
| required-plugin | Plugin(s) that are required to run the plugin. |

**apiapi**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| version | A required modeling tool API version. For example, 1.0. |

**required_pluginrequired-plugin**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | The ID of a required plugin. For example, my.first.plugin.0. |
| name | A name of a required plugin. |
| internalVersion | An internal version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, the "version" is used to check if the required plugin is suitable. |
| version | A version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, any required plugin version is suitable. For example: 1.1. |
| optional | The default value of this attribute is false.If value is false (or attribute is skipped), the plugin will be loaded only if the required plugin is loaded.If value is true, the plugin will be loaded if the required plugin is loaded or required plugin is not installed. |

**runtimeruntime**

**Nested elements**

| Element name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| library | A runtime library for a running plugin. |

**librarylibrary**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | A name (path) of the required library. The path is relative to the plugin descriptor directory (slash should be used to separate files in path).For example, "pluginlib.jar", "lib/otherlib.jar". |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A plugin descriptor is a file written in XML and named <em>plugin.xml</em>. Each descriptor contains properties of the one plugin. The descriptor file should contain the definitions of elements of the one plugin. </p><p>The <em>plugin.xml</em> file consists of the following elements:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="plugin"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="requires"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="api"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="required_plugin"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[required-plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="runtime"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="library"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Plugin descriptor" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>The following tables describe them in more details.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e79d307f-79c3-48e5-9673-99b09ef7bd83"><ac:parameter ac:name="">plugin</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>plugin</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><p>A plugin ID should be unique. It is used to identify a plugin by a plugin's manager internals and by requirements of other plugins. For example, <em>my.first.plugin.0</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>name</p></td><td><p>A plugin name. There is no strict rules applied to this attribute. For example, <em>Example plugin</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><p>A plugin version. The version can be used to check other plugins dependencies, if internalVersion is not defined.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>internalVersion</p></td><td><p>A plugin internal version. It is used to check other plugins dependencies.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>provider-name</p></td><td><p>A plugin provider name, which is a company or an author name. For example: <em>No Magic</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>class</p></td><td><p>A full qualified class name. The class must be derived from <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins</em>. A plugin and stored in plugin runtime library. This class will be loaded and initialized by the plugin's manager. For example, <em>myplugin.MyPlugin</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ownClassloader</p></td><td><p>Optional; the default value is <em>false</em>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Indicates, if to use an own plugin's classloader (separate from other plugins). All program plugins are loaded by the <strong>one</strong> classloader. If there are plugins that cannot be loaded by the same classloader (for example, because of conflicts in plugin libraries versions or other), their descriptors must be defined to use own class-loaders.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>class-lookup</p></td><td><p>Optional; possible values are <em>LocalFirst</em>, <em>GlobalFirst</em>, the default value is <em>GlobalFirst</em>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Specifies the priority of a &quot;parent&quot; class loader, if a plugin uses ownClassloader. LocalFirst forces to load classes from the plugin class loader even if such classes exist in the modeling tool core class path. This option should be used, if, in your plugin, you want to use different versions of same libraries that are used in the core.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Nested elements</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Element name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">requires</span></p></td><td><p>A modeling tool API version required by the plugin. Plugins and their versions required by the plugin.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">runtime</span></p></td><td><p>Runtime libraries needed by a plugin.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="590e5641-f4c0-4019-9525-11a96e3ab2f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">requires</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>requires</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Nested elements</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Element name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">api</span></p></td><td><p>A required modeling tool API version.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">required-plugin</span></p></td><td><p>Plugin(s) that are required to run the plugin.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fffb05b-54b8-464a-bfc9-e3cd262bc19a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">api</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>api</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><th><strong>Attribute</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong> </th></tr><tr><td>version </td><td>A required modeling tool API version. For example, <em>1.0</em>. </td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c70d7151-1037-4d8a-b158-8a7dfa71c940"><ac:parameter ac:name="">required_plugin</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>required-plugin</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Attribute</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><p>The ID of a required plugin. For example, <em>my.first.plugin.0</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>name</p></td><td><p>A name of a required plugin.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>internalVersion</p></td><td><p>An internal version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, the &quot;version&quot; is used to check if the required plugin is suitable.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><p>A version of a required plugin. If it is not defined, any required plugin version is suitable. For example: <em>1.1</em>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">optional</td><td colspan="1"><p>The default value of this attribute is <em>false</em>.</p><p>If value is <em>false</em> (or attribute is skipped), the plugin will be loaded only if the required plugin is loaded.</p><p>If value is <em>true,</em> the plugin will be loaded if the required plugin is loaded or required plugin is not installed.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83d3b430-6f92-4ad6-8bd6-51c7882c5e30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">runtime</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>runtime</strong></p><p><strong>Nested elements</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Element name</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">library</span></td><td>A runtime library for a running plugin. </td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b292c881-dcce-415e-b86d-3da39ebfd807"><ac:parameter ac:name="">library</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>library</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Attribute</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td>name</td><td><p>A name (path) of the required library. The path is relative to the plugin descriptor directory (slash should be used to separate files in path).</p><p>For example, &quot;pluginlib.jar&quot;, &quot;lib/otherlib.jar&quot;.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490622 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: Plugins

- page_id: `170490622`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490622/Plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:53.913+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['plugin', 'plug-in']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. 
 A plugin must contain the following resources:

- A directory

- Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file

- A plugin descriptor file

- Optional files used by the plugin

Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Plugins are the only one way to change the functionality of a modeling tool. The main purpose of the plugin architecture is to add a new functionality to your modeling tool. Although there is a limited ability to remove an existing functionality using plugins. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> A plugin must contain the following resources:</p><ul><li>A directory</li></ul><ul><li>Compiled java files, packaged into a jar file</li></ul><ul><li>A plugin descriptor file</li></ul><ul><li>Optional files used by the plugin</li></ul><p>Typically a plugin creates some GUI components allowing a user to use the plugin functionality. Generally, this is not necessary because the plugin can listen for some changes in a project and activate itself on the desired changes.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cafabac-b175-4310-8c9b-9d91fca265fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aeea49da-d2c5-448f-972b-cc3702986582"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490639 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00154: Plugins directories

- page_id: `170490639`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490639/Plugins+directories
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:23.912+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins > Detail information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.

##### **Installation directory**

Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins*.

This directory can be changed using a *md.plugins.dir* java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.

To change the location of the plugins directory

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dmd.plugins.dir="absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2" Sample property value-Dmd.plugins.dir="C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins" If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (*.zip*) cannot be loaded.Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin *.zip* file are not added to the installation directory like they are when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024xR1'].

##### **Configuration files location**

Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (*<Configuration files location>\plugins*). This allows to have global and user plugins.

See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.

Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Plugins are loaded from two locations on the program startup.</p><h3><strong>Installation directory</strong></h3><p>Plugins are loaded from the program installation directory (a global plugin directory) <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins</em>.</p><p>This directory can be changed using a <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property and specifying paths separated with a semicolon (;) symbol.</p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To change the location of the plugins directory</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file, which is located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:</p><pre>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;absolute path to plugins directory1;absolute path to plugins directory2&quot;</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce50ee8e-88d6-4372-a529-306178a57c78"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample property value</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>-Dmd.plugins.dir=&quot;C:\Program Files\MagicDraw\plugins;D:\MyPlugins&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4fd1934-8587-47f7-8473-59f432781e76"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you are loading plugins from a custom location, make sure the location contains extracted plugin files. Compressed plugin files (<em>.zip</em>) cannot be loaded.</li><li>Besides the plugin itself, any other files (profiles, modelLibraries, samples, etc.) contained in the plugin <em>.zip</em> file are not added to the installation directory like they are when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024xR1" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installing a resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><strong>Configuration files location<br /></strong></h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Additionally, plugins are loaded from a configuration files location (<em>&lt;Configuration files location&gt;\plugins</em>). This allows to have global and user plugins. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2852841e-5fcc-44bf-93d4-82d06a41d199"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> See “MagicDraw Configuration Files Location” in the MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Another issue on Unix systems is related to user permissions to write. If a modeling tool is installed in a root, user is not allowed to write in a global plugin directory if a user has not such permissions.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490907 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00155: Plugins migration

- page_id: `170490907`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490907/Plugins+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:13.646+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We are keeping Open API stable as much as possible, but in some cases API changes in a newer application version. For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.

Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.

We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>We are keeping Open API stable as much as possible, but in some cases API changes in a newer application version. For example this happens if support for a new UML version is added or some very serious refactoring is done in components.</p><p>Sometimes it is enough to recompile plugin code with new libraries and adjust some minor API changes. Sometimes more serious migration is needed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de9e02f-14fe-487a-90bf-e89620726db5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We recommend to recompile a plugin with new libraries to see the incompatibilities if any.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6effda46-6001-4f01-b36f-045a9ad9dab4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac344481-bbee-4176-81b9-ee4c059195f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490920 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00156: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `170490920`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490920/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+15.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:39.525+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version.

Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML metamodel changes' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .
- Some deprecated methods have been removed:

| Removed deprecated method | Substitution |
| --- | --- |
| BaseElement.getProject() | Project.getProject(BaseElement) |
| MainFrame.getDialogParent() | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent() |
| MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame) | MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame) |
| ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype) | ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection) |

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into md_api.jar and md_common_api.jar (was md.jar and md_common.jar ). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “ ProgramInitials_api.jar ” pattern.
- Changes in package names for build-in plugins have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl. **** pattern, now these plugins are moved to com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*, for example com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.* . A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Open API in version 15.0 and later versions has changed together with changes in the UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification. You should read this chapter if you want to migrate your plugin created for earlier MagicDraw version. </p><p>Thereby we made a cleaner UML metamodel implementation API after the UML specification has been updated. There are no big changes in Open API, so migration will not be a long and complicate task for you.</p><p>The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML metamodel changes" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Some<strong> deprecated methods</strong> have been removed:</li></ul><table><thead><tr><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Removed deprecated method</strong></p></div></th><th><div class="tablesorter-header-inner"><p><strong>Substitution</strong></p></div></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><pre>BaseElement.getProject()</pre></td><td><pre>Project.getProject(BaseElement)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.getDialogParent()</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent()</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>MainFrame.setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td><td><pre>MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().setDialogParent(Frame)</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setStereotype(Stereotype)</pre></td><td><pre>ElementListProperty.setSelectableRestrictedElements(Collection)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><ul><li>Changes in<strong> libraries jars</strong> have been made. Open API classes now are packaged into <em>md_api.jar </em>and <em>md_common_api.jar </em>(was <em>md.jar </em>and <em>md_common.jar</em>). If your plugin was using API classes from some program build-in plugin, a plugin jar is also renamed with a “<em>ProgramInitials_api.jar</em>” pattern.</li><li>Changes in<strong> package names for build-in plugins</strong> have been made. In earlier program versions, some build-in plugins exposed their Open API, for example, patterns, transformations, emf, and etc. We have changed a package name pattern. Earlier API versions used a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.</em><strong><em>**</em> </strong>pattern, now these plugins are moved to<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.*.*,</em> for example <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.patterns.*</em>. A simple import statement change in your java source will solve this migration issue.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93c2742d-0888-468c-8234-5dfbb95f3a8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="90dfd71b-5ead-4567-bfcc-bb3acf2ac4f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490912 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00157: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API

- page_id: `170490912`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490912/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+17.0.1+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:56:12.640+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are migrating from earlier than MagicDraw 15.0 version, please read the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API' space='DEVG2024xR1'] chapter first.

The changes that have been made to Open API are the following:

- UML metamodel changes . UML 2.4.1 specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. For the details of changes, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project (decomposition) structure API changes' space='DEVG2024xR1'].
- Changes in methods:
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.ProjectOptionsConfigurator*.**The method *afterLoad(**ProjectOptions)* has been added. An empty implementation of this method must be added to a custom ProjectOptionsConfigurator implementation.
  - ***com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*.**The following methods have been added:

*projectActivatedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectActivatedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectCreated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectCreated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectOpenedFromGUI(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectOpenedFromGUI-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreClosed(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosed-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreDeActivated(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreDeActivated-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreReplaced(Project[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreReplaced-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

*projectPreSaved(Project, boolean)*

*projectPreClosedFinal(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/project/ProjectEventListener.html#projectPreClosedFinal-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*

An empty implementation of these methods must be added to the custom *ProjectEventListener*implementation. The code change is not required, if *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListenerAdapter* is extended.

- Changes in libraries jars have been made. New jar files have been introduced in the lib folder. Do not forget to update your [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compilation classpath' space='DEVG2024xR1'] .

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490910 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API

- page_id: `170490910`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490910/Plugins+migration+to+MagicDraw+18.0+and+later+Open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:15.101+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Open API Changes

**UML 2.5 support related changes**

- UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes.
- c om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2 and com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3 classes have been merged into com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.

**UI refactoring related changes**

UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID* interface and new actions have been added.

IDs of the removed actions are as follows:

- ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED
- ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION
- ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT
- ActionsID.LINK_EDITING
- ActionsID.METRICS
- ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION
- ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS
- ActionsID.REDO_LIST
- ActionsID.UNDO_LIST

**Project Merge related changes**

Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange

**Structured expressions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper*class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).

**UML Interactions related changes**

The *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper* class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 class="heading">Open API Changes</h3><p><strong>UML 2.5 support related changes</strong></p><ul><li>UML metamodel interfaces have been adjusted to UML 2.5 support related changes. </li><li>c<em>om.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL2</em> and <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfileL3</em> classes have been merged into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile.</em></li></ul><p><strong>UI refactoring related changes</strong></p><p>UI of diagrams' windows management, symbols' compartments management, and elements creation in compartments have been refactored. As a consequence, some actions have been removed from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> interface and new actions have been added.</p><p>IDs of the removed actions are as follows:</p><ul><li>ActionsGroups.NEXT_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsGroups.PREVIOUS_DIAGRAM_RELATED</li><li>ActionsID.DIAGRAM_NAVIGATION_GROUP</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_ATTRIBUTE</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_EXTENSION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_LITERAL</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERAND</li><li>ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_OPERATION</li><li> ActionsID.INSERT_NEW_PORT</li><li>ActionsID.LINK_EDITING</li><li>ActionsID.METRICS</li><li> ActionsID.OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB_ACTION</li><li>ActionsID.OPEN_LAST_ActionsID.RECENT_DIAGRAMS</li><li> ActionsID.REDO_LIST</li><li>ActionsID.UNDO_LIST</li></ul><p><strong>Project Merge related changes</strong></p><p>Types of parameters have been changed from Set to Collection, because of performance related improvements in methods of the following interfaces:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Change</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.MacroChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.RelatedChange</em></li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.macro.RelatedMacroChange</em></li></ul><p><strong>Structured expressions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.expressions.ExpressionHelper </em>class has been added. It provides utility methods to use in queries defined with the StructuredExpression language (used by smart packages, derived properties in DSL, and so forth).</p><p><strong>UML Interactions related changes</strong></p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper</em> class has been added. It provides utility methods to access Interactions part of UML metamodel.</p>
````

## PAGE 00161: Presentation element selection events

- page_id: `170490806`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490806/Presentation+element+selection+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:01.492+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to register a *java.beans.PropertyChangeListener* on a project. Selection changes fire *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to register a <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeListener</em> on a project. Selection changes fire <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b2b534-023d-437c-bc7f-b110a6e43dcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Project project = ;

Listener listener = new Listener();
SelectionProvider selectionProvider = SelectionProvider.getInstance(project);
selectionProvider.addSelectionChangedListener(listener);

private static class Listener implements SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedListener
{
	@Override
	public void selectionChanged(SelectionProvider.SelectionChangedEvent event)
	{
		// was selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection oldSelection = event.getOldSelection();
		
		// now selected
		SelectionProvider.Selection newSelection = event.getNewSelection();
		
		// do something
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490763 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00162: Presentation elements (symbols)

- page_id: `170490763`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490763/Presentation+elements+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:50:47.707+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams
- labels: ['path-connector', 'setter', 'set', 'sset']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The UML semantic defines only the UML metamodel. The modeling tool has its own structure of classes for UML elements representation in the diagram. The base class of this structure is *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement*. A presentation element is a textual or graphical presentation of one or more model elements.

Presentation elements are sometimes called symbols also.

In the metamodel, a *PresentationElement* is the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement* that presents a set of model elements to a user. It is the base for all metaclasses used for the presentation. All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of *PresentationElement*.

A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:

Every presentation element can have children. For example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement* has a collection of inner presentation elements. *PresentationElement* of some *Package* can have a collection of presentation elements for inner *Package* elements.

Current version *PresentationElement* API allows you to:

- Access element bounds PresentationElement.getBounds()
- Access a model com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element of a presentation element PresentationElement.getElement() . A presentation element can have no Element , for example, a com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.TextBoxView .
- Access children PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()
- Access properties of the element PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String) , PresentationElement.getPropertyManager(). The sample of properties would be the Suppress Operations property for a class presentation element, the Autosize property for any com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement .
- Select/unselect or access a selection state of the presentation element ( PresentationElement.isSelected() , PresentationElement.setSelected(boolean) ) .

A subclass of presentation elements *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathConnector* provides information about connected paths to the presentation element. To get a collection of connected paths to the presentation element, use the method **PathConnector*.getConnectedPathElements()*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A presentation element is a textual or graphical presentation of one or more model elements.</p><p>Presentation elements are sometimes called symbols also.</p><p>In the metamodel, a <em>PresentationElement</em> is the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</em> that presents a set of model elements to a user. It is the base for all metaclasses used for the presentation. All other metaclasses with this purpose are indirect subclasses of <em>PresentationElement</em>.</p><p>A current version of Open API provides just a basic structure of presentation elements:</p><div class="WordSection1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PresentationElements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Presentation elements (symbols)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><p><br /></p><p>Every presentation element can have children. For example, <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em> has a collection of inner presentation elements. <em>PresentationElement</em> of some <em>Package</em> can have a collection of presentation elements for inner <em>Package</em> elements.</p><p>Current version <em>PresentationElement</em> API allows you to:</p><ul><li>Access element bounds <em>PresentationElement.getBounds()</em></li><li>Access a model <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> of a  presentation element<em> PresentationElement.getElement()</em>. A presentation element can have no <em>Element</em>, for example, a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.TextBoxView</em>.</li><li>Access children <em>PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()</em></li><li>Access properties of the element <em>PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String)</em>, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> PresentationElement.getPropertyManager(). </em> The sample of properties would be the <em>Suppress Operations</em> property for a class presentation element, the <em>Autosize</em> property for any <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em>.</li><li>Select/unselect or access a selection state of the presentation element (<em>PresentationElement.isSelected()</em>, <em>PresentationElement.setSelected(boolean)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</em>.</li></ul><p>A subclass of presentation elements <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathConnector</em> provides information about connected paths to the presentation element. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490805 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00163: Presentation elements modification events

- page_id: `170490805`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490805/Presentation+elements+modification+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:29:08.577+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:

1. The listener receives events from all opened diagrams.
2. The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.

The adapter works in a project scope.

****

To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter* object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.

To create the *DiagramListenerAdapter* object, call:

```java

```

The *DiagramListenerAdapter* object is registered for a project on its creation.

To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.

Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames***class*.*

**Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal**

```java

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to all diagram changes in a single adapter:</p><ol><li>The listener receives events from all <em>opened </em>diagrams.</li><li>The adapter delegates these events to your own listener.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04930027-ff28-4880-92a6-0dd1eb6571da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The adapter works in a project scope.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>            </strong></p><p>To listen to diagram change events, you need to create the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, that is, to pass the property change listener as a delegator, which receives all events.</p><p>To create the <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object, call:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e521b8d-0412-4726-a1e6-ae12e9ae463f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    new DiagramListenerAdapter(propertyChangeListener)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <em>DiagramListenerAdapter</em> object is registered for a project on its creation.</p><p>To start using the adapter, install it. Uninstall it when it is no longer necessary, that is, you do not need to receive any events about diagram changes anymore.</p><p><br /></p><p>Property names related to presentation elements modification are defined in <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ExtendedPropertyNames</em><em> </em>class<em>.</em></p><p><strong>Example: Listening to symbol creation / removal</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed5ff09-ede8-4f7d-bc3f-956244a75f95"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DiagramListenerAdapter adapter = new DiagramListenerAdapter(new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        {
            String propertyName = evt.getPropertyName();
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_ADDED.equals(propertyName) || ExtendedPropertyNames.VIEW_REMOVED.equals(propertyName))
            {
                // an added / removed symbol
                PresentationElement presentationElement = (PresentationElement) evt.getNewValue();
            }
        }
    }
    
    adapter.install(project);
 
    // When the adapter is no longer needed, uninstall it. 
    adapter.uninstall(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490950 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00164: Printing messages in Simulation Console

- page_id: `170490950`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490950/Printing+messages+in+Simulation+Console
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.548+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print different types of messages on the **Simulation Console** pane by using the following codes

- SimulationManager.logConsoleDebug ( Project project , String message)

Displays debugging information.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleInfo ( Project project , String message)

Displays information in general.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleWarn ( Project project , String message)

Displays warning messages.

- SimulationManager.logConsoleError ( Project project , String message)

Displays error messages.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can print different types of messages on the <strong>Simulation Console</strong> pane by using the following codes</p><p><br /></p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleDebug <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ef04b705-9d65-44d1-99f3-bd5e54f5d641">(</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="059ecca9-0690-4430-a621-ab53a63d87d7"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ef04b705-9d65-44d1-99f3-bd5e54f5d641">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays debugging information.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleInfo (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c5afac16-1841-4b97-9e0c-4dded575558c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e53f375f-2031-4bf1-97a7-4b27e9b063fd">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e53f375f-2031-4bf1-97a7-4b27e9b063fd">,</ac:inline-comment-marker> String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays information in general.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleWarn (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="87888861-8b59-4cc0-9d14-4efc4d4fa543"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8088163b-f3b1-4e9f-a968-dc72ec40ef77">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays warning messages.</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.logConsoleError (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03a8dd06-c036-4926-bf31-6bf2ab29409f"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2891690-4874-4215-acbd-2b7d0cefec5a">Project project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker>, String message)</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Displays error messages.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490913 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: Project (decomposition) structure API changes

- page_id: `170490913`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490913/Project+decomposition+structure+API+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:04:40.634+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following figure shows the domain model of the project decomposition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The core change is an introduction of *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject* - *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*. *IPrimaryProject* is a single instance in the scope of a project. *IPrimaryProject* can have attached (used) any number of *IAttachedProject* (used projects), while *IAttachedProject* itself can attach other used projects (*IAttachedProject*).

The earlier than 17.0.1 project structure API made deprecated in version 17.0.1. The deprecated classes from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules*package are:

*Committable*

*IProject*

*ModuleDescriptor*

*ModulesManager*

*MountHelper*

*MountInfo*

*MountTable*

*MountTableListener*

*ShareInfo*

*ShareTable*

There is no straight one to one mapping, but the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint*

*com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint*

*ProjectUtilities* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace *MountHelper*.

*ModuleDescriptor* is replaced with a combination of *IAttachedProject* and *com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration*. There is separate *ModuleDescriptor* for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single *IAttachedProject* for the used project, but a separate *ProjectAttachmentConfiguration* for every project usage in the current version.

The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:

```java
projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]>
```

The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:

```java
projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]>
```

The deprecated classes from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules </em>package are:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Committable</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em> ModuleDescriptor</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ModulesManager </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountHelper </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTable </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MountTableListener </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareInfo </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ShareTable</em></p></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><p>There is no straight one to one mapping, but  the set of deprecated classes are now generally replaced by the set of the following classes:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.mounting.IMountPoint</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.sharing.ISharePoint</em></p><p><em>ProjectUtilities</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> utility classes are introduced to help working with a new project decomposition structure and should replace <em>MountHelper</em>.</p><p><em>ModuleDescriptor</em> is replaced with a combination of <em>IAttachedProject</em> and <em>com.nomagic.ci.persistence.decomposition.ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em>. There is separate <em>ModuleDescriptor</em> for every (same) project usage in MagicDraw 17.0 or earlier versions, while there is a single <em>IAttachedProject</em> for the used project, but a separate <em>ProjectAttachmentConfiguration</em> for every project usage in the current version.</p><p><br /></p><p>The example of the MagicDraw 17.0 and earlier version code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ea0081f-b05b-4ada-a258-f78dd22d3f07"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final Collection<ModuleDescriptor> projectModules = project.getModulesManager().getMountTable().getModules()
	for (ModuleDescriptor moduleDescriptor : projectModules)
	{
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = moduleDescriptor.getAutoLoadType();
		final boolean loadIndex = moduleDescriptor.isLoadIndex();
		final boolean editable = moduleDescriptor.isEditable();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The example of a new MagicDraw 17.0.2 code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c472fef9-e92a-462c-b715-593ad911b154"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	final IPrimaryProject primaryProject = project.getPrimaryProject();
	final Collection<IAttachedProject> projectAttachedProjects = ProjectUtilities.getAttachedProjects(primaryProject);
	for (IAttachedProject attachedProject : projectAttachedProjects)
	{
		final ProjectAttachmentConfiguration attachmentConfiguration = ProjectUtilities.getAttachment(primaryProject, attachedProject);
		// properties of the project usage
		final AutoLoadKind autoLoadKind = ProjectUtilities.getAutoLoadKind(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean loadIndex = ProjectUtilities.isLoadIndex(attachmentConfiguration);
		final boolean editable = !attachmentConfiguration.isReadOnly();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490689 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00166: Project concept

- page_id: `170490689`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490689/Project+concept
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:24.181+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project
- labels: ['application', 'project', 'model', 'root-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.

Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Project

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)

```java

```

The project keeps references to a root *Model(s)*, also has references to all diagrams.

Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR1']. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.

The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:

```java

```

All root models can be accessed in the following way:

```java
models = project.getModels();]]>
```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project structure decomposition' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML model' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with diagrams' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project</em> represents the storage of all project specific data like the UML model, all diagrams, project specific options and etc.</p><p>Multiple projects can be opened at the same time, but just one Project can be active in UI. Data from one project can not reference data in other opened project. The active (current) project can be accessible in the following way:</p><div class="WordSection1"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64d0d6b5-8f4e-4580-85c5-7f67f8f84fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">Also, the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project </em>is accessible for any other <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement (UML Element or Presentation Element)</em></div><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7032c3b8-1f0c-4778-8c65-d8b5e1366e57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Project.getProject(element);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>The project keeps references to a root <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model(s)</em>, also has references to all diagrams.</p><p>Project always have one primary root Model and may have zero or more attached Models from used projects. Attached models are available only in projects loaded from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Dataware server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Locally stored projects or projects have only a primary root model.</p><p>The primary root model can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fc25cc4-311a-4752-9d10-d328721fd47a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Package model = project.getPrimaryModel();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>All root models can be accessed in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48570a3b-95d2-4b8a-8056-d004f14ce5fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List<Package> models = project.getModels();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="579ea26b-cf13-4db3-98f0-b9e6944d72e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project structure decomposition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490692 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00167: Project descriptor

- page_id: `170490692`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490692/Project+descriptor
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:24.253+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project
- labels: ['projectdescriptorsfactory', 'teamworkutils', 'project-descriptors-factory', 'teamwork-utils']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor* represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple *ProjectDescriptors*.

There are two types of *ProjectDescriptors*:

- A local project descriptor. It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.
- A r emote project descriptor. It represents a project stored in the server.

A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.

Every *ProjectDescriptor* provides the following properties:

- URI. The location is a specific java.lang.String value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.
- Representation String. A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).
- Remote flag. The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory*can create an appropriate *ProjectDescriptor* object,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils***helps to find an existing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR1'] (remote) project descriptors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor</em> represents a project (or a used project) as a resource for storing and loading. The same project can have multiple <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>.</p><p>There are two types of <em>ProjectDescriptors</em>:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A local project descriptor. </strong>It represents a local ordinary project. A descriptor can be created for a project or file object.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">A r</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">emote project descriptor.</strong> It represents a project stored in the server.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00f130cd-2a48-40e4-9687-d59f7800d6f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A server project has both descriptors (local and remote) because it can be saved locally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Every <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> provides the following properties:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">URI.</strong> The location is a specific <em>java.lang.String</em> value that holds all information that is needed to access a project.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Representation String.</strong> A string used for a project identification in the user interface (GUI).</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Remote flag. </strong>The remote flag indicates if the project descriptor represents a server project.</li></ul><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptorsFactory </em>can create an appropriate <em>ProjectDescriptor</em> object,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils</em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em>helps to find an existing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (remote) project descriptors.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490705 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: Project life-cycle events

- page_id: `170490705`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490705/Project+life-cycle+events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:36:16.519+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)* to register *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener*. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions.

A recommendation is to register a listener in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()* method.

##### Save participant

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-)*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager.addProjectListener(ProjectEventListener)</em> to register <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectEventListener</em>. The listener will be invoked on various project life-cycle related actions. </p><p>A recommendation is to register a listener in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.Plugin.init()</em> method.</p><h3>Save participant</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(SaveParticipant<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Application.html#addSaveParticipant-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant-">)</a></em>. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3f64e1-1fb4-457e-b9d9-bffd65c50979"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Projects management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490699 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00169: Project loading and saving

- page_id: `170490699`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490699/Project+loading+and+saving
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:09:51.759+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project > Projects management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class.

- saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)
- loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)

“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.

A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases *java.lang.IllegalStateException* is thrown.

The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the **Commit Project**dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the **Save**dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).

##### **Save participant**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant* to plugin into a save/commit operation.

Register it using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(**SaveParticipant)*.

Implementing *SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(**Project, ProjectDescriptor)* you can "disable" the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.

###### Example #1. Saving an active project

```java

```

###### Example #2. Loading a project from the file

The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #3. Importing another project file

The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:

```java

```

###### Example #4. Loading a server project

The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:

```java

```

Related pages
