### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Projects are saved and loaded by using two methods in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> class.</p><ul><li><em>saveProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li><li><em>loadProject(ProjectDescriptor, boolean)</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42fdc785-9a78-4dce-ab21-2f094ef0cbd2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>“Save” means “Commit” for the server project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A project cannot be saved using a descriptor, if the project isn’t specified, and a project cannot be loaded, if the file isn’t specified. In such cases <em>java.lang.IllegalStateException</em> is thrown.</p><p>The silent mode means that during the saving or load process no GUI interruptions for a user input is used, for example, there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Commit Project </strong>dialog box while committing a server project or there is no the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Save </strong>dialog box while saving a new project (a project is saved into the last used directory).</p><h3><strong>Save participant</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.SaveParticipant</em> to plugin into a save/commit operation. </p><p>Register it using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addSaveParticipant(</em><em>SaveParticipant)</em>. </p><p>Implementing <em>SaveParticipant.isReadyForSave(</em><em>Project, ProjectDescriptor)</em> you can &quot;disable&quot; the save/commit operation until some conditions are met.</p><h4>Example #1. Saving an active project</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402ef67b-d6b8-4112-8555-156fa0eff7dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // An active project
    Project project = projectsManager.getActiveProject();
 
    // Get a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.getDescriptorForProject(project);
    
    // Save a project 
    projectsManager.saveProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4>Example #2. Loading a project from the file</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83f7a0b8-25be-4db9-92aa-df95ec7afe16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #3. Importing another project file</h4><p>The project can be imported, if the project's file name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5347718-0fe2-45bb-ba94-786d7bb53ffb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager(); 
    File file = new File(projectFilePath);
    // Create a project descriptor 
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = ProjectDescriptorsFactory.createProjectDescriptor(file.toURI()); 
    projectsManager.importProject(projectDescriptor);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example #4. Loading a server project</h4><p>The project can be loaded, if the project’s qualified name is known:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="070a54dc-b403-4943-bb90-ef37f4e987e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectsManager projectsManager = Application.getInstance().getProjectsManager();
    // Create a project descriptor
    ProjectDescriptor projectDescriptor = TeamworkUtils.getRemoteProjectDescriptorByQualifiedName(remoteProjectQualifiedName);
    if (projectDescriptor != null)
    {
        projectsManager.loadProject(projectDescriptor, true);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d623b2ab-c3d7-4b26-b554-42442b6e0377"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project descriptor" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490704 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00170: Project options

- page_id: `170490704`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490704/Project+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:22:37.350+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager* classes.

Project options belong to three categories:

1. Visible in the Project Options UI window
2. Invisible in the Project Options UI window
3. Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window

Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as "personal". The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.

##### **Retrieving Project Options**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()**.*

##### **Retrieving Project Option value**

The following example shows how to access project option’s value:

```java

```

**Adding custom project options**

Configurators are used for defining a new project options.

The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s *init()* method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every project has project related options. These options are stored together with the project data. Options are organized using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> classes. </p><p>Project options belong to three categories:</p><ol><li>Visible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Invisible in the Project Options UI window</li><li>Personal invisible in the Project Options UI window</li></ol><p>Personal options are specific to every user in a server project. It means, every user may have different values of the same option if it is marked as &quot;personal&quot;. The personal option is treated as a regular option in a local project.</p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Options</strong></h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getOptions()</em><em>.</em></p><h3><strong>Retrieving Project Option value</strong></h3><p>The following example shows how to access project option’s value:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bab1580e-fad4-4325-9c3c-dfc0bac2e96a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //retrieving a visible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving an invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    //retrieving a personal invisible in UI option
    Property property = project.getOptions().getProperty(ProjectOptions.PERSONAL_INVISIBLE_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
    
    //retrieving an option value
    if (property != null)
    {
        Object value = property.getValue();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Adding custom project options</span></strong></p><p class="WordSection1">Configurators are used for defining a new project options. </p><p class="WordSection1">The following example shows, how to add the project option’s configurator at the plugin’s <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">init()</em> method in order to have the additional project option ”Test Property” for every project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="240636e0-34c2-464e-9969-6154f03d6f38"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ProjectOptions.addConfigurator(new ProjectOptionsConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ProjectOptions projectOptions)
        {
            com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property property = projectOptions.getProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, "TEST_PROPERTY_ID");
            if (property == null)
            {
                // Create a property, if it does not exist
                property = new StringProperty("TEST_PROPERTY_ID", "description");
                // Group
                property.setGroup("MY_GROUP");
                // The custom resource provider
                property.setResourceProvider(new PropertyResourceProvider()
                {
                    public String getString(String string, Property property)
                    {
                        if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate ID
                            return "Test Property";
                         }
                         if ("TEST_PROPERTY_ID_DESCRIPTION".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a description
                            return "Test Property in My Group";
                        }
                        if ("MY_GROUP".equals(string))
                        {
                            // Translate a group
                            return "My Group";
                        }
						return string; 
					}
                });
          
                // Add a property
                projectOptions.addProperty(ProjectOptions.PROJECT_GENERAL_PROPERTIES, property);
            }
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08f1f2bc-1687-469f-99c0-4be4352cdb03"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490702 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00171: Project structure decomposition

- page_id: `170490702`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490702/Project+structure+decomposition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:39:25.076+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with project
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from "used projects" (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities* utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService* or*com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* classes for managing attached projects.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects management' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Used project management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Project structure is decomposed into several parts - a primary project and attached projects. The primary project part belong to a project itself. Attached project parts comes from &quot;used projects&quot; (modules). Used projects can use other projects, so there is a deep structure of attached projects.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Project structure decomposition" ac:alt="Project structure decomposition"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project structure decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project structure decomposition" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities</em> utility class for retrieving more project decomposition related information.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService</em> or<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager</em> classes for managing attached projects.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ebbfd8f-20ed-42c8-b119-db7efb958c35"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Projects management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Used project management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00172: Projects management

- page_id: `170490694`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectsManager* class is responsible for containment and management of projects.

Use the code below to access *ProjectsManager*

```java

```

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
*ProjectsManager* provides the API for *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project* creating, closing, saving, loading, and activating. A program can have multiple opened projects, but only one project can be active.

```java
projects = projectsManager.getProjects();
 
    //Gets an active project
    Project activeProject = projectsManager.getActiveProject();]]>
```

An active project can also be accessed directly from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application*:

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\projects*

Related pages

true

A program can have multiple opened projects, but only one project can be active.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae5055ea-ecbd-404d-b564-db739281968b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //Gets all the projects

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490834 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: Properties

- page_id: `170490834`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490834/Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:32:10.163+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['property-editor', 'property-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools include the Open API that provides a set of classes used as properties for diagrams’ symbols, project or environment options and etc.

Every property has two major attributes – the ID and value. A property has a value of the type *java.lang.Object*. Every specific property has a value of the specific type. For example, the value of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.BooleanProperty* is*java.lang.Boolean*, the value of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.StringProperty* is *java.lang.String*.

The property ID identifies the specific property in the properties set.

You must set some *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyResourceProvider* to your property instance in order to display a normal name, not the ID of the property in the UI of a modeling tool. *PropertyResourceProvider* has just one method *getString(java.lang.String , Property[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/properties/PropertyResourceProvider.html#getString-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property-), where a key is an ID of your property.*

The collection of properties is grouped by *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager*. Every *PropertyManager* has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing properties of presentation elements' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A property has a value of the type <em>java.lang.Object</em>. Every specific property has a value of the specific type. For example, the value of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.BooleanProperty</em> is<em> java.lang.Boolean</em>, the value of a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.StringProperty</em> is <em>java.lang.String</em>.</p><p>The property ID identifies the specific property in the properties set.</p><div class="WordSection1"><p><br /></p><p>You must set some <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyResourceProvider</em> to your property instance in order to display a normal name, not the ID of the property in the UI of a modeling tool. <em>PropertyResourceProvider</em> has just one method <em>getString(java.lang.String , Property<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/properties/PropertyResourceProvider.html#getString-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property-">)</a>, where a key is an ID of your property.</em></p></div><p>The collection of properties is grouped by <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em>. Every <em>PropertyManager</em> has a name and a list of properties. It can return the property by the ID, properties with the same value, properties whose values are different.</p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fee02083-faa4-4e60-b526-a587d03e9b37"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Changing properties of presentation elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Project options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00174: Property change event concept

- page_id: `170490741`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490741/Property+change+event+concept
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:36.777+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Event support
- labels: ['new-element-creation-event', 'elements-name-change-event']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML model uses standard *java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent* events for notifying listeners about changes.

The main properties of the *PropertyChangeEvent*are as follows:

- Property name. The changed property name.
- New value. The new (current) changed property value.
- Old value. The old property value, which was before the property change.

Properties names are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML model properties names' space='DEVG2024xR1'] page

###### **Example of the element’s name change event**

```java

```

###### **Example of the new element creation event**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>UML model uses standard <em>java.beans.PropertyChangeEvent</em> events for notifying listeners about changes.  </p><p>The main properties of the <em>PropertyChangeEvent </em>are as follows:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Property name. </strong>The changed property name.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">New value. </strong>The new (current) changed property value.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Old value. </strong>The old property value, which was before the property change.</li></ul><div class="WordSection1"><p>Properties names are described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML model properties names" /></ac:link> page</p><h4><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Example of the element’s name change event</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d8be409-b977-48ee-b9b4-c91bc4eba484"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    propertyChangeEvent.getPropertyName().equals(PropertyNames.NAME); 
    propertyChangeEvent.getNewValue() // will be new name of element; 
    propertyChangeEvent.getOldValue() // will be name of element before change.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example of the new element creation event</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83aa81ad-516b-41fd-a9b0-032f0a869c65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    propertyChangeEvent.getPropertyName().equals(UML2MetamodelConstants.INSTANCE_CREATED); 
    propertyChangeEvent.getNewValue() // will be new created element; 
    propertyChangeEvent.getOldValue() // will be null.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41f3ca8f-847f-429b-91cd-0f08a231b578"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="UML model properties names" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00175: Refactoring model elements

- page_id: `170490724`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490724/Refactoring+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:24:41.372+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To refactor an element in a model, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring***class.

###### **Example #1: Converting an element to an interface**

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Replacing an element with another element**

```java

```

###### **Example #3: Extract refactoring**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting***class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource* and *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget*. Invoke the refactoring with *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()*. Review refactoring results by inspecting *ExtractSource* and *ExtractTarget*.

```java
allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]>
```

###### **Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring**

The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(**[Element](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html))***method.

```java

```

###### **Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection<**Element>, Element)* to move *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and all connected *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)* to a new owner.

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\refactor*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To refactor an element in a model, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring</em><em> </em>class.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #1: Converting an element to an interface</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="996e118e-32cf-4ec3-84a3-d1a0befe5030"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToConvert = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Convert");
 
    // Converts the element to an interface.
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(Interface.class);
 
    // Preserves the old element ID for the new element. 
    info.setPreserveElementID(true);
    Element conversionTarget = Refactoring.Converting.convert(elementToConvert, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>Example #2: Replacing an element with another element</strong></h4><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd05f199-c8c8-46d9-9379-1f52b2421087"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element elementToReplace = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Replace");
 
    ConvertElementInfo info = new ConvertElementInfo(elementToReplace.getClassType());
    info.setConvertOnlyIncomingReferences(true);
 
    Refactoring.Replacing.replace(element, elementToReplace, info);   
    
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><h4><strong>Example #3: Extract refactoring</strong></h4><p>Use the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Extracting</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>class to create the extract manager for a symbol which you want to extract. Configure the extract refactoring by changing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractSource</em> and <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractTarget</em>. Invoke the refactoring with <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.refactor.extract.ExtractManager.extract()</em>. Review refactoring results by inspecting <em>ExtractSource</em> and <em>ExtractTarget</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b25ad79-2609-464f-a3f6-e20f4e785c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Creates an extract refactor manager.
    ExtractManager extractManager = Refactoring.Extracting.createExtractManager(symbols);
    if (extractManager != null)
    {
        Project project = ...;
        // A session has to be started before refactoring. 
        SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
        sessionManager.createSession(project, "Extract Refactor Symbols");
 
        // We may control the extract refactor result by modifying extract target. 
        ExtractTarget extractTarget = extractManager.getExtractTarget();
 
        // Create a namespace to which we are going to refactor. 
        Project project = Project.getProject(symbols[0]);
        Package package1 = project.getElementsFactory().createPackageInstance();
        package1.setOwner(project.getPrimaryModel());


        // Set the namespace to which the extract result should go. 
        extractTarget.setTargetNamespace(package1);
 
        // Choose target diagram type from allowed diagram types if the default type does not suite.
        List<String>  allowedTargetDiagramTypes = extractTarget.getAllowedTargetDiagramTypes();
        extractTarget.setTargetDiagramType(allowedTargetDiagramTypes.get(0));
 
        // Modify reference names which link the extract refactor source to the target.
        List<? extends ExtractReference> references = extractTarget.getReferences();
 
        for (int i = 0; i < references.size(); i++)
        {
            ExtractReference reference = references.get(i);
            reference.setName(Integer.toString(i));
        }
 
        // We may control the extract refactor source by modifying the extract source.
        ExtractSource extractSource = extractManager.getExtractSource();
        extractSource.setElementName("sourceElementName");


        // Perform actual refactoring. 
        extractManager.extract();
        sessionManager.closeSession(project);


        // The element which was created in the source during refactoring. 
        Element sourceElement = extractSource.getElement();


        // The element which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        Element targetElement = extractTarget.getElement();


        // The diagram which was created in the target during refactoring. 
        DiagramPresentationElement targetDiagram = extractTarget.getDiagram();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><h4><strong>Example #4: Reverse relationship refactoring</strong></h4><p>The relation reverse refactoring can be done using the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a>)</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1967114d-d7fe-43aa-b363-92527bbea717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     // We have an arbitrary element or symbol which represents a relationship. 
    BaseElement baseElement = ...;
    Project project = ...; 
    // Relationship reversing should be wrapped with session create/close calls. 
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance(); 
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Reverse relation");  
 
    // Reverse the relationship. 
    Refactoring.RelationReversing.reverseRelationDirection(baseElement);
 
    // Close the session. 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example #5: Moving element with connected relationships to other owner</strong></h4><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Refactoring.Moving.moveElementsWithRelation(java.util.Collection&lt;</em><em>Element&gt;, Element)</em> to move <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> and all connected <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship(s)</em> to a new owner.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\refactor</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00176: Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager

- page_id: `170490947`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490947/Registering+an+execution+engine+to+the+Simulation+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.502+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code

```text

```

In general, a new execution engine is registered at <Plugin Class>.init().

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To register your new execution engine to the Simulation Manager, pass a new instance of your execution engine descriptor to the Simulation Manager by typing the following code</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ac1e768-d1a9-4cdc-9d40-cb4dd54f9240"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[SimulationManager.registerEngine(new MyEngineDescriptor());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In general, a new execution engine is registered at &lt;Plugin Class&gt;.init().</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490833 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00177: Registering custom hyperlink handler

- page_id: `170490833`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490833/Registering+custom+hyperlink+handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:47:24.834+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Managing navigation in model > Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-)* to add a custom handler.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customhyperlink

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to register a new custom hyperlink handler for a custom protocol. The custom hyperlink may have a specific UI editor for editing it's properties.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.addHandler(HyperlinkHandler<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/hyperlinks/HyperlinksHandlersRegistry.html#addHandler-com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkHandler-">)</a></em> to add a custom handler.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customhyperlink</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>

## PAGE 00178: Registering custom symbol renderer provider

- page_id: `170490800`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490800/Registering+custom+symbol+renderer+provider
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:36:47.919+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the *SlotRenderer* for slot symbols, the *PackageRenderer* for package symbols, and the *DependencyRenderer* for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager*.

****

**Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class**

```java

```

**Step 2. Registering RendererProvider**

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The following example shows the custom symbol renderer provider, which provides the <em>SlotRenderer</em> for slot symbols, the <em>PackageRenderer</em> for package symbols, and the <em>DependencyRenderer</em> for a dependency link symbols. The created renderer provider must be registered into the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager</em>.</p></div><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step 1. Creating the RendererProvider class</strong> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd8b2e2c-d3fa-4500-9c26-1299c2ac27e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     /**
    * A custom renderers provider.
    */
    class RendererProvider implements PresentationElementRendererProvider
    {
        private SlotRenderer slotRenderer;
        private DependencyRenderer dependencyRenderer;
        private PackageRenderer packageRenderer;
 
        RendererProvider()
        {
            slotRenderer = new SlotRenderer(); 
            dependencyRenderer= new DependencyRenderer(); 
            packageRenderer = new PackageRenderer();
        }
 
        public PresentationElementRenderer getRenderer(PresentationElement presentationElement)
        {
            if (presentationElement instanceof SlotView || presentationElement instanceof SlotListElementView)
            {
                // A slot renderer 
                return slotRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof DependencyView)
            {
                // dependency renderer 
                return dependencyRenderer;
            }
 
            if (presentationElement instanceof PackageView)
            {
                // package renderer 
                return packageRenderer;
            }
 
            return null;
       }
    };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Step 2. Registering RendererProvider</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4088bf97-912e-4d4b-8ae4-9d5390636717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementRendererManager.getInstance().addProvider(new RendererProvider());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change bounds for the existing *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement*, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(**ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)*:

```java

```

If shape has the *Autosize*****property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change bounds for the existing <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em>, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.reshapeShapeElement(</em><em>ShapeElement, java.awt.Rectangle)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3986e906-8a93-4bdf-a29d-21b2689561be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Rectangle newBounds = new Rectangle(100,100,80,50);
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().reshapeShapeElement(element, newBounds);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>Every shape element has a preferred size. The shape size cannot be smaller than the preferred size. If you will try to set smaller bounds, these bounds will be increased to the preferred size.</div><p>If shape has the <em>Autosize<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </strong></em>property set to TRUE, bounds will be reduced to the preferred size.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20c1f6d-3f0d-462a-adf0-f8b0691eec4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490640 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: Resource dependent plugin

- page_id: `170490640`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490640/Resource+dependent+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:35:54.301+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: ['is-plugin-required', 'get-plugin-name', 'get-plugin-version']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. 
To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin* interface.

*ResourceDependentPlugin* has three special methods:

- a isPluginRequired [*(*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*Project)* method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return true, if a given project uses resources from the plugin.
- a *getPluginName()* method should return a plugin name.
- a *getPluginVersion()*** method should return a version of the plugin.

The following example illustrates an implementation of *ResourceDependentPlugin*:

```java

```

This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project "*my_profile_filename*". The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Starting with MagicDraw version 16.6, there is a new functionality to require to load plugins for a particular project. This feature was created to avoid an incorrect data load because of missing plugins. Every plugin can provide a name and version of a plugin to be required for the correct project load. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To become a resource dependent plugin, your plugin class must implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.ResourceDependentPlugin</em> interface.</p><p><em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em> has three special methods:</p><ul><li>a <em>isPluginRequired</em><em><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/plugins/ResourceDependentPlugin.html#isPluginRequired-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-"><em>(</em></a><em>Project)</em></em> method is called on saving a project. The plugin must return <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">true,</em> if a given project uses resources from the plugin.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginName()</em></span> method should return a plugin name. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <em>getPluginVersion()</em><em> </em></span>method should return a version of the plugin. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following example illustrates an implementation of <em>ResourceDependentPlugin</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f908de-266e-4022-a6a8-91416580f831"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package myplugin;
import ...
public class MyPlugin extends Plugin implements ResourceDependentPlugin
{
   @Override
   public void init()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean close()
   {...}
   @Override
   public boolean isSupported()
   {...}
 
   @Override
   public boolean isPluginRequired(Project project)
   {
      return ProjectUtilities.findAttachedProjectByName(project, "my_profile_filename") != null;
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginName()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getName();
   }
   @Override
   public String getPluginVersion()
   {
      return this.getDescriptor().getVersion();
   }
}


]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This plugin is required for a project, if the project contains a used project &quot;<em>my_profile_filename</em>&quot;. The plugin name and version will be saved into the project's XMI file.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490669 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00181: Resource manager descriptor file

- page_id: `170490669`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490669/Resource+manager+descriptor+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.045+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file.

For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see*MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file naming

The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is*MDR_<type>_<plugin name>_<plugin id>_descriptor.xml.*For example, *MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm**l*.

All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file location

The file location is*<modeling tool installation directory>\data\resourcemanager*.

##### Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample

This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.

You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “*type*“ value.

```xml
Enterprise
    Architect
    Standard
    Professional Java
    Professional C++
    Professional C#
    Professional ArcStyler
    Reader
    OptimalJ
 
    
        
        
        
        
        
        
        

    
]]>
```

The following table describes the terms used in the sample:

| Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | A unique plugin id. id is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at http://www.nomagic.com/support.html. |
| name | A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources. |
| product | A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type. |
| type | A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template. |
| version resource | This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a version internal value multiplied by 10. version resource should be a number. |
| version internal | This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. version internal should be a number. |
| version human | A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The version human number may consist of numbers and/or words. |
| edition | Supported program editions. |
| installation | installation includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.Do not use "*.*" ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if "samples/*.*" is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. |
| critical | If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>If you are planning to import and export your resources with the Resource Manager, you should read this section. For importing resources to your modeling tool, the resource manager descriptor file should be created. In this section you will find information about the descriptor file naming, location, and a sample of this file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e6be16-6307-406c-919c-b44b0ffc07f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>For more information about importing resources with Resource Manager, see </span><em>MagicDraw UserManual.pdf</em><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file naming</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The Resource Manager descriptor file has a specific naming. The structure of the name is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_&lt;type&gt;_&lt;plugin name&gt;_&lt;plugin id&gt;_descriptor.xml.  </em>For example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xm</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">l</em>.</p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f1661a8-55a6-42e9-9e03-aab847223bd8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>  All spaces are replaced with the underscore symbol “_“.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file location</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The file location is </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\data\resourcemanager</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">.</span></p><p> </p><h3>Resource Manager descriptor file content and sample</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">This section presents a basic sample of the Resource Manager descriptor file structure. This sample represents a plugin distribution, which also includes a custom diagram, profile, template, sample, and documentation.</span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can also distribute custom diagrams, profiles, templates, or samples separately, you only need to change the “</span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">type</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">“ value.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dc3b1dc-2ac2-4410-93a8-813a41720afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<resourceDescriptor 
    critical="false" 
    date="2007-12-24+02:00" 
    description="My Plug-in"
    homePage="http://www.nomagic.com" 
    id="3001"
    mdVersionMax="higher" 
    mdVersionMin="15.0" 
    name="CustomPlugin" 
    product="CustomPlugin" 
    type="Plugin">
 
    <version human="1.0 beta" internal="1" resource="10"/>
    <provider name="No Magic" />
 
    <edition>Enterprise</edition>
    <edition>Architect</edition>
    <edition>Standard</edition>
    <edition>Professional Java</edition>
    <edition>Professional C++</edition>
    <edition>Professional C#</edition>
    <edition>Professional ArcStyler</edition>
    <edition>Reader</edition>
    <edition>OptimalJ</edition>
 
    <installation>
        <file from="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" to="data/defaults/data/diagrams/CustomDiagram/descriptor.xml" />
        <file from="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" to="profiles/CustomProfile.xml.zip" />
        <file from="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" to="templates/CustomTemplate.xml.zip" />
        <file from="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" to="samples/CustomPluginSample.mdzip" />
        <file from="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" to="manual/CustomPluginManual.pdf" />
        <file from="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" to="plugins/customPlugin/*.*" />
        <file from="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" to="data/resourcemanager/MDR_Plugin_CustomPlugin_3001_descriptor.xml" />

    </installation>
</resourceDescriptor>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following table describes the terms used in the sample:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>id</strong></p></td><td><p>A unique plugin id. <em>id </em>is used to form a descriptor file name. This id must be unique. To prevent duplicates, use a number starting from 3000 or contact the No Magic support at <a href="http://www.nomagic.com/support.html">http://www.nomagic.com/support.html</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>name</strong></p></td><td><p>A plugin name. A name is used to form a descriptor file name. The plugin name must be unique between all program resources.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>product</strong></p></td><td><p>A product name. Used only for the “Plugin (commercial)” resource type.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>type</strong></p></td><td><p>A type may be one of the following types: Custom Diagram, Plugin, Plugin (commercial), Profile, Sample, Template.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version resource</strong></p></td><td><p>This number is used to identify the resource build/release. For example, a new resource build must be released, while the resource human and internal versions should not change. A suggested value is a <em>version internal </em>value multiplied by 10. <em>version resource </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version internal</strong></p></td><td><p>This version number is not visible to the user and may be used for an internal count. <em>version internal </em>should be a number.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>version human</strong></p></td><td><p>A human readable version number of the resource. This version number is visible to users. The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">version human </em>number may consist of numbers and/or words.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>edition</strong></p></td><td><p>Supported program editions.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>installation</strong></p></td><td><p><em>installation </em>includes files, which will be copied from the custom plugin archive to the program folder.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="227c4851-c138-46de-84f3-55f8f05f6997"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not use &quot;*.*&quot; ! If a file name includes “*.*“, all defined files will be removed when uninstalling the plugin. For example, if &quot;samples/*.*&quot; is defined, all files from the “samples” folder will be removed after uninstalling the resource. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>critical</strong></td><td colspan="1">If the resource is selected as critical, it cannot be removed because MagicDraw does not allow this.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00182: REST API Change Log

- page_id: `170490971`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490971/REST+API+Change+Log
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-22T13:11:40.810+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Changed data types

###### arrayOfUuid

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/workspaces
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/tags
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/elements
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/revisions/{revision}/elements
- /osmc/admin/usergroups
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### arrayOfString

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/branches/{branchId}/locks
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/users
- /osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/resync

###### osmc/admin/config/put

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/config/{key}

###### osmc/admin/roles/roleID/userGroups/post

| 2024x | 2024x Refresh1 |
| --- | --- |
| String | Array |

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resourceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups
- /osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/roles/{roleId}/usergroups

###### osmc/admin/license/apply/put

Parameter “port” removed.

Affected URLs:

- /osmc/admin/license/apply

##### Changed endpoints

###### /osmc/admin/license/query/{licenseServer}

###### GET

Query parameters added:

| Name | Type | Remark |
| --- | --- | --- |
| licenseFramework | String | The licensing framework (DSLS or FlexNet) |
| licensingMode | String | The licensing mode (ORGANIZATION_DEFINED or CUSTOM, for DSLS only) |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490961 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00183: REST APIs

- page_id: `170490961`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490961/REST+APIs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-04T15:48:19.409+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud provides a set of REST APIs for server management. REST APIs are essential properties in Teamwork Cloud that allow you to query data about user accounts, roles, projects, and that provide access and basic operations for the resources. REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.

The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs inboth machine-readable and human-readable formats. Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at [https://osmc.nomagic.com](https://osmc.nomagic.com/). The API document is also bundled with Teamwork Cloud installation. You can access it from https://<Teamwork Cloud IP>:8111/osmc/swagger.

Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. It allows logging in and calling the server from a web browser. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

REST API also provides an administrator-friendly way to manage the server because it can be run in the text mode and in a shell script.</p><p>The documentation of REST API is provided in Swagger, available with Teamwork Cloud installation, where you can find the description of the APIs in<span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> both machine-readable and human-readable formats.</span> Teamwork Cloud REST API documentation in Swagger is accessible at <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/">https://osmc.nomagic.com</a>. The API document is also bundled with Teamwork Cloud installation. You can access it from <span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);">https://&lt;Teamwork Cloud IP&gt;:8111/osmc/swagger</span>. </p><p>Having Swagger come with the Teamwork Cloud installation makes it the most convenient way to issue REST API to the server. It allows logging in and calling the server from a web browser. While calling to the server, Swagger also shows a cURL command that the user can copy to and execute in the command line or use in a batch script instead.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="808393af-a8e7-4889-a3c1-4d4c7eeabe89" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00184: Retrieving element icon

- page_id: `170490738`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490738/Retrieving+element+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:58:48.620+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ElementIcon* utility class for retrieving element icon. It also provides methods for retrieving icons by element metatype, stereotype and etc.

```java

```

```text

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages
