### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ElementIcon</em> utility class for retrieving element icon. It also provides methods for retrieving icons by element metatype, stereotype and etc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f0c01e3-efed-4db4-af5d-21a5ac7a0d1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Element element = ...;
   //get element icon
   ResizableIcon icon = ElementIcon.getIcon(element);
 
   //get UML Package icon
   ResizableIcon packageIcon = ElementIcon.getIconByClassType(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package.class);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre class="auto-cursor-target"> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="449dd841-ade5-40d4-accb-662eacda23b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Retrieving meta information about element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with icons" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490735 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00185: Retrieving meta information about element

- page_id: `170490735`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490735/Retrieving+meta+information+about+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:22:53.550+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every Element is identified by it's "metaclass". Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class*).

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()* to retrieve the element metaclass.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes* for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Every Element is identified by it's &quot;metaclass&quot;. Element metaclass is mapped into java interface (for example <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em>). </p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.getClassType()</em> to retrieve the element metaclass.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ClassTypes</em> for managing the metaclasses - collecting all available, collecting derived and etc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490967 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00186: Reusing Teamwork Cloud session

- page_id: `170490967`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490967/Reusing+Teamwork+Cloud+session
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T14:58:11.970+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is **very** important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.

The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.

The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.

```text
users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]>
```

The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the **cookie.txt** file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.

The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When using cURL, user credentials (user name/password or a token) can be provided in a command-line argument. A new session is created during authentication. It is <strong>very</strong> important to note that a Teamwork Cloud session remains open after a REST API returns. This implies that a Teamwork Cloud session is not closed automatically. If the user’s credentials are still in use (without sending a valid cookie), the license count will be used up, and new sessions cannot be created. The user needs to wait until the session is timed out. The default timeout is 15 minutes.</p><p>The correct way to use REST API is to log in only once, reuse the session for all subsequent calls, and log out once finished.</p><p>The following example is a Bash script used for creating a comma-separated list of email addresses for all users in Teamwork Cloud, to facilitate notification sending.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="154a6874-c10f-4833-bd14-899a0068625c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
# Script to export a unique list of email address for all users in Teamwork Cloud into a singe CSV list (email_list.csv)
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
#
# 
SERVER=127.0.0.1
RESTBASEURL=https://$SERVER:8111/osmc
USERURL=${RESTBASEURL}/admin/users
LOGINURL=${RESTBASEURL}/login
LOGOUTURL=${RESTBASEURL}/logout
username=Administrator
password=Administrator
rm -f email*.*
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt --insecure -u $username:$password $LOGINURL
curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL" -H "accept: application/json" > users.json
sed 's/\"\,\"/\n/g' users.json > users.txt
sed -i 's/\[\"//g' users.txt
sed -i 's/\"\]//g' users.txt
while read username; do
		curl -k -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt --insecure -X GET "$USERURL/$username" -H "accept: application/json" >> email.txt
done < users.txt
curl -s -c cookie.txt -b cookie.txt $LOGOUTURL
grep -E -o "\b[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\b" email.txt > email.lst
sed -i 's/\n/;/g' email.lst
sort email.lst | uniq > email_unique.lst
tr '\n' ',' < email_unique.lst > email_list.csv
truncate -s-1 email_list.csv

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first execution of cURL logs in to the server. The server returns a session ID in a cookie. The cookie is saved into the <strong>cookie.txt</strong> file. The remaining cURL calls send the cookie back to the server to reuse the open session.</p><p>The example below shows how to use PowerShell to create internal users from a CSV file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcf44a99-0ec2-4a02-8871-c1632d46fd92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Script to import users from CSV file into Teamwork Cloud
# Author:  Benjamin Krajmalnik
# Format: non-quoted CSV, no headers
#  userLogin,userPassword,fullUserName,departmentName,emailAddress
#
# Usage:
# PowerShell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ImportUsersFromCSV.ps1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
Add-Type @"
  using System.Net;
  using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;
  public class TrustAllCertificatesPolicy : ICertificatePolicy 
  {
     public bool CheckValidationResult
     (
       ServicePoint srvPoint, X509Certificate certificate
       ,
       WebRequest request, int certificateProblem
     ) 
     {
       return true;
     }
  }
"@
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CheckCertificateRevocationList = $false;
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback += { $true; };
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CertificatePolicy = New-Object TrustAllCertificatesPolicy
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12
$Server = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Name or IP address'
$Admin = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Administrator User'
$Passwd = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the Teamwork Cloud Server Administrator Password'
$Userfile = Read-Host -Prompt 'Input the full path name of the file containing the users'
$RESTBASEURL = "https://"+$Server+":8111/osmc/"
$ADDUSERURL = $RESTBASEURL + "admin/users"
$LOGINURL = $RESTBASEURL   + "login"
$LOGOUTURL = $RESTBASEURL + "logout"
$pair="$($Admin):$($Passwd)"
$bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($pair)
$base64 = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($bytes)
$basicAuthValue = "Basic $base64"
$headers = @{ Authorization = $basicAuthValue }
$RestError = $null
Try {
$response = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$LOGINURL" -Method Post -Headers $headers -SessionVariable 'Session'
} Catch {
	$RestError = $_
}
foreach($line in [System.IO.File]::ReadLines($Userfile))
{
                  $login,$password,$username,$department,$email = $line.split(',').trim()
                  
                  $JSON=@"
                  {
                                             "userName": "$login",
                                             "password": "$password",
                                             "otherAttributes": {
                                                            "mobile": "",
                                                            "name": "$username",
                                                            "department": "$department",
                                                            "email": "$email"
                                             },
                                             "enabled": true
                              }
"@
                              $response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$ADDUSERURL" -Method Post -Body $JSON -ContentType "application/json"  -WebSession $Session
}
 
$response = Invoke-WebRequest  -Uri "$LOGOUTURL" –Method Get -WebSession $Session]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490687 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00187: Running action with progress

- page_id: `170490687`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490687/Running+action+with+progress
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:42:52.686+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Adding new functionality
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.

There are two options to execute these actions:

- a foreground task - use com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress or com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner
- a background task - use com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\progressstatus*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Multi-threading' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>For long lasting actions, there is a need to show a progress and run the asynchronous code.</p><p>There are two options to execute these actions:</p><ul><li>a foreground task - use <em>com.nomagic.task.RunnableWithProgress</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.MagicDrawProgressStatusRunner</em></li><li>a background task - use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.task.BackgroundTaskRunner</em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation direc</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">tory&gt;\openapi\examples\progressstatus</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74b88a6d-75d0-4f9c-9077-d7b780313a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Multi-threading" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490897 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00188: Running programs in batch mode

- page_id: `170490897`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490897/Running+programs+in+batch+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:45:32.669+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are cases when custom task must be executed in the batch (command line) mode. For example, if you want to run MagicDraw or other modeling tool application, open some project, execute code generation and exit application.

API provides two ways to run the tool in the batch mode depending on the required functionality.

1. Functionality is provided by an application core. You need to extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine class.
2. Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction interface.

****You can find the code examples in

1. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\*imagegenerator* . It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.
2. <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin . Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.

The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. The graphical environment is required.

For more information on the launching batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space='DEVG2024xR1'].

For more information on creation of batch mode programs, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementing command line launchers' space='DEVG2024xR1'].

More information is available in javadoc.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You need to extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em> class.</li><li>Functionality is provided by a plugin. Plugin loading is managed by the application core so it is undesirable to add core and plugin classes into the same class path. The different approach accessing plugin functionality is used. You need to implement the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em> interface.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ol><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\<em>imagegenerator</em></em>. It takes a project file and destination directory as arguments and generates images for all diagrams.</li><li><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin</em>. Just prints the program arguments to the application log file.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e86ef95-eade-424e-a877-f424d503c8b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The application cannot be run on the headless device even in a batch mode. ````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5478fe15-51af-4122-aaa4-1a0111f53781" /></h3><h3>Performing the forward engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code generation.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c8fe96a-5d37-4773-afd7-e09335dc20e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet javaGenerationSet = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #2. Adding model elements to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="142f730a-857e-439e-91e1-e2d9f727c166"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject();
 
 	// create a new element
 	ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
 	Class classA = ef.createClassInstance();
 	classA.setName("ClassA");
	classA.setOwner(project.getModel());
	List<BaseElement> modelsForSample = new ArrayList<BaseElement>();
	modelsForSample.add(classA);
	javaGenerationSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(modelsForSample);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Step #3. Performing the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> generation</strong></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a7d6f5-8cf4-4795-960a-8fc7e1d2d6d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    CodeEngineeringManager.generate(javaGenerationSet);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Performing the reverse engineering</h3><p>The example shows how to perform a simple java code reverse.</p><p><strong>Step #1. Creating <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9933ea61-dd6a-4c46-b658-9eef350f9f35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = Application.getInstance().getProject(); 
    String name = "sample CE project";
    String workingDir = OPENAPI_DATA_DIRECTORY_PATH;// e.g C:\myworkingPackage
 
    // create a working package
    ElementsFactory ef = project.getElementsFactory();
    Package workingPackage = ef.createPackageInstance();
    workingPackage.setName("my working package");
    workingPackage.setOwner(project.getModel());
 
    // creating a code engineering set
    CodeEngineeringSet ces = CodeEngineeringManager.createCodeEngineeringSet(
				CodeEngineeringConstants.Languages.JAVA, null, name, project, workingPackage, workingDir);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Here the null dialect is used for the java language, because java doesn't have any dialect.</p><p><strong>Step #2. Adding the source code to <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eccd7ee9-f67e-4c1a-95af-e288323986bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	ces.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(new File(workingDir + File.separator + "test directory")); // starting from C:\myworkingPackage\test directory\]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This sets the given instance of the code engineering set working directory and adds all files from that directory. Set java classpaths for the project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6ba7648-854c-4ea6-a859-f54175d7b845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    String[] claspath = new String[] { path1, path2, path3, path4 }; 
    JavaCodeEngineeringManager.setJavaClasspath(project, claspath);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Step #3. Performing the reverse of <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d41202c-94aa-42f4-bf2e-6a98fb396354"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[     CodeEngineeringManager.reverse(ces, false);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490865 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00190: Script validation rule

- page_id: `170490865`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490865/Script+validation+rule
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:25.263+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to compile and store them in a classpath. Oftentimes, the code is compact and simple.

###### How to create a a script validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Constraints' space='MD2024xR1'].
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).
6. Enter the validation code in the selected language.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation rules' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Groovy, Javascript, or Jython scripting language can be used to create a validation rule which are used to validate elements in your model. The advantage of using script validation rules is that it is not necessary to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="448794bc-8c8a-476a-8727-833273737189">compile and store them</ac:inline-comment-marker> in a classpath. <span>Oftentimes,</span> the code is compact and simple.</p><p> </p><h4>How to create a  a script validation rule</h4><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b66a4fbf-3ce3-492a-a322-9533858e6cfc"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Constraints" ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language (Groovy, Javascript, or Jython).</li><li>Enter the validation code in the selected language.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490711 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00192: Session management

- page_id: `170490711`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490711/Session+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:24:13.057+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: ['create-session', 'close-session']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager* is the singleton manager used for editing model Elements. All modifications to model elements should be performed between the *createSession(Project, java.lang.String)*and *closeSession(Project[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)*method calls.

To edit some *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, a session with this manager must be created. After editing a model element, a session must be closed. After that, all changes will be applied to the model and registered in the command history (for undo/redo) as one command with a session name. Only one session can be active.

The following code can be used for accessing, checking, and creating the session:

```java

```

If other session is already created and not closed*,*the *createSession(Project, java.lang.String)* method throws the *java.lang.IllegalStateException* runtime exception.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em> is the singleton manager used for editing model Elements. All modifications to model elements should be performed between the <em>createSession(Project, java.lang.String) </em>and <em>closeSession(Project<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/SessionManager.html#closeSession-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-">)</a> </em>method calls.</p><p>To edit some <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, a session with this manager must be created. After editing a model element, a session must be closed. After that, all changes will be applied to the model and registered in the command history (for undo/redo) as one command with a session name. Only one session can be active.</p><p>The following code can be used for accessing, checking, and creating the session:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c9feac-4bc9-49c3-9d17-a0ca33e20401"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // access a singleton instance by using getInstance()
    // only one session can be active, so check this.
    if (!SessionManager.getInstance().isSessionCreated(project))
    {
        // create a new session. 
        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit");
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If other session is already created and not closed<em>, </em>the <em>createSession(Project, java.lang.String) </em> method throws the <em>java.lang.IllegalStateException</em> runtime exception.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### **Selectin****g Elements via Element Selection Dialog**

```java

```

You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/elementselection*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h4><strong>Selectin</strong><strong>g Elements via Element Selection Dialog</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cda0261-8c8f-4386-b713-bff08cdad38c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(...) methods to create the element selection dialog.
    Frame dialogParent = MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(); 
    ElementSelectionDlg dlg = ElementSelectionDlgFactory.create(dialogParent);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a single element selection mode.
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initSingle(....);
 
    // Use ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(...) methods to initialize the dialog with a multiple element selection mode. 
    ElementSelectionDlgFactory.initMultiple(....);
 
    // Display the dialog for the user to select elements. 
    dlg.setVisible(true);
 
    // Check if the user has clicked "Ok". 
    if (dlg.isOkClicked())
    {
         // Get the selected element in a single selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElement();
 
        // Get selected elements in a multiple selection mode. 
        BaseElement selected = dlg.getSelectedElements();
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65809b8c-f725-4fb0-8cf4-7b92378b5837"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/elementselection</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490854 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00195: Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window

- page_id: `170490854`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490854/Showing+notifications+adding+text+into+Message+Window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:47:50.275+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use API described bellow to show Notifications (a balloon windows on the bottom right corner of application window) and appending text into a Message Window.

**NotificationManager**

This is an entry point to display notifications in MagicDraw. Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements *com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer* interface.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage.*

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window,

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()* controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.

****

**GUILog**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String)* to put a text into Project Message Window message.

Use *GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)* to log information from notification into a Message Window.

Use *GUILog.openLog()* to open Message Window.

For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**/openapi/examples/notifications*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Showing question, error, warning dialogs' space='DEVG2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use API described bellow to show Notifications (a balloon windows on the bottom right corner of application window) and appending text into a Message Window.</p><p><strong>NotificationManager</strong></p><p>This is an entry point to display notifications in MagicDraw. Two types of notifications can be displayed: application level and container level. Container lever notifications are displayed in special container that implements <em>com.nomagic.ui.notification.NotificationsContainer</em> interface. </p><p>Use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.NotificationManage. </em></p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t</em>o add some information into a Message Window,</p><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.notification.Notification.getContext()</em> controls where notification is logged in the Message Window - into Environment or Project tab.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">GUILog</strong></p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(java.lang.String)</em> to put a text into Project Message Window message.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.log(Notification, boolean)</em> to log information from notification into a Message Window.</p><p>Use <em>GUILog.openLog()</em> to open Message Window.</p><p><br /></p><p>For more details about every specific kind of a property, see javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>/openapi/examples/notifications</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6dfaab-2bb5-4c81-aaca-b9acf1614fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Showing question, error, warning dialogs" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490853 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00196: Showing question, error, warning dialogs

- page_id: `170490853`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490853/Showing+question+error+warning+dialogs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:13:08.465+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.GUILog* class to show various simple UI dialogs.

**Error dialog**

Use *GUILog.showError(java.lang.String)*

Use*GUILog.showError(java.awt.Frame, java.lang.String, java.lang.Throwable)*to show error dialog with exception stack trace

**Warning dialog**

Use *GUILog.showWarning(java.lang.String)*

**Question dialog**

Use *GUILog.showQuestion(java.lang.String)*

**Message dialog**

Use *GUILog.showMessage(java.lang.String)*

Use *GUILog.showHTMLMessage(java.lang.String,**GUILog.URLActionHandler)* to show html message with some active links

**Log messages into Message Window**

Use *GUILog.log(java.lang.String) t*o add some information into a Message Window

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Showing notifications, adding text into Message Window' space='DEVG2024xR1'] for more information about showing Notifications and appending text into a Message Window

For more information**about other API choices see javadoc*.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490898 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00197: Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties

- page_id: `170490898`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490898/Specifying+batch+mode+program+classpath+and+required+system+properties
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-05-05T10:25:14.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Running programs in batch mode
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory

133936365

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory

133936386

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory

133936375

MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.

In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a *java* command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).

There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:

- -Desi.system.config=data/application.conf

The property is specified as a relative path to*<modeling tool installation directory>, therefore,* you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.

There are two types of command lines.

1. Core related batch program: A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.
  - Must extend *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine*
  - Must contain the Java *main* method and implement *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute*
2. Plugin related batch program: A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin
  - `com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action` - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction*) you want to execute, for example, `-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample`
  - Must launch using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher*
  - The *CommandLineAction* must be registered in *CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(new CommandLineActionExample());* e.g. in your plugin *init()* method
  - The plugin where *CommandLineAction* is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)

Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see "Providing licensing information" at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space=''].

The implemented examples can be found in :

- Core batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\imagegenerator
- Plugin batch program: <modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin

The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/patch.jar;lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```text
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/patch.jar;lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/patch.jar\:lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]>
```

```bash
cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/patch.jar\:lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]>
```

You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths).

These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)

A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:

```text

```

A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:

```text

```

The shell script that starts a Core batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:

```bash

```

The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:

```bash

```

133936363

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="617e938b-a10b-4608-bbad-f9827d021a2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133936365</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a16012ea-37e6-42d9-940d-3e5da86089a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133936386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd04f80c-db81-4d26-bc55-6bf6c4014053"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133936375</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>MagicDraw or other modeling tools run as a simple Java program.</p><p>In order to launch the program from the command line, the launcher is started using a <em>java</em> command line. The classpath has to include jar files from the lib folder and its subfolders (additional requirements exist for different command line implementations).</p><p>There is one mandatory property that has to be specified as well:</p><ul><li><code>-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf</code></li></ul><p>The property is specified as a relative path to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;, therefore,</em> you either have to make your current directory before launching your program or specify absolute paths for the above-listed properties.</p><p>There are two types of command lines.</p><ol><li>Core related batch program:<br />A program that does not call/depend on any functionality from any plugins. Should only depend/call core tool functionality.<br /><ul><li><p>Must extend <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine</em></p></li><li><p>Must contain the Java <em>main</em> method and implement <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLine#execute</em></p></li></ul></li><li>Plugin related batch program:<br />A program that can call/depend on any running modeling tool plugin<br /><ul><li><p><code>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action</code> - specifies the batch mode action (implementation of <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineAction</em>) you want to execute, for example, <code>-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample</code> </p></li><li><p>Must launch using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher</em> </p></li><li><p>The <em>CommandLineAction</em> must be registered in <em>CommandLineActionManager.getInstance().addAction(<span style="color: rgb(0,0,128);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">new <span>CommandLineActionExample</span></span>());</em> e.g. in your plugin <em>init()</em> method</p></li><li><p>The plugin where <em>CommandLineAction</em> is registered must load and run, in order for the command line program to launch it. (this means plugin.xml and plugin .jar files must be in the /plugins directory)</p></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Also, it is possible to set up the command line to use the floating license server or license file - see &quot;Providing licensing information&quot; at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Licensing information" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The implemented examples can be found in :</p><ul><li>Core batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\imagegenerator</em></li><li>Plugin batch program: <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\commandlineplugin</em></li></ul><p>The examples of complete command lines that launch the batch mode programs:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99475978-37bf-4c51-bc15-1f59efb94d7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/patch.jar;lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/*;openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68eeab44-55ae-4fbd-a0c6-034bea80270a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\Program Files\MagicDraw>java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
-cp lib/patch.jar;lib/brand.jar;lib/brand_api.jar;lib/*;lib/graphics/* ^
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf ^
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
@bin/vm.options ^
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63cfa508-e9b8-49ee-b849-2f19c8a54bf4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Core batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-cp lib/patch.jar\:lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/*\:openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages project=project.mdzip destination_dir=out]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee546ddc-2f6b-40a3-b63f-52533f990fbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Plugin batch program command line for Mac OS-X and Linux</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[> cd MagicDraw_dir
> java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
-cp lib/patch.jar\:lib/brand.jar\:lib/brand_api.jar\:lib/*\:lib/graphics/* \
-Desi.system.config=data/application.conf \
-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
@bin/vm.options \
-Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher argument1 argument2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can modify the previous examples and create more sophisticated batch files. For example, you can define the directory to run the tool from, or specify to use the same java, as the tool uses itself. The exemplary scripts that can be used to start the command line program are shown in the following script examples. The scripts read and use the same classpath as the modeling tool when launching it manually(this is the preferred way to define classpaths). </p><p>These scripts can be launched from any directory. Can be modified to read other properties files (e.g. csm.properties, cea.properties)</p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Core batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dce90f91-c03a-4ceb-9799-f0f15ffdcb23"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
 
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"

if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)

setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
  
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" 
  
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)

:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
  
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%;openapi\examples\imagegenerator\imagegenerator.jar" ^
	 -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages %*
	 
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>A script that starts the Windows Plugin batch command line program on Windows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a8e4ea4-9487-4856-a3be-e70f1477257a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program Windows batch file that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions
  
:: either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
set "MAGICDRAW_HOME=C:\Program Files\MagicDraw"
 
if "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%" == "" (
    echo MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder
    exit /B 1
)
 
setlocal enableDelayedExpansion
:: change slashes if needed
set MAGICDRAW_HOME=!MAGICDRAW_HOME:\=/!
setlocal disableDelayedExpansion
   
pushd "%MAGICDRAW_HOME%"
   
:: Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
For /F "tokens=1* delims==" %%A IN (bin\magicdraw.properties) DO (
    IF "%%A"=="CLASSPATH" set MD_CLASSPATH=%%B
)
 
:: Modify classpath to a valid Windows style classpath. Replace \: with ;
set MD_CLASSPATH=%MD_CLASSPATH:\:=;%
   
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K ^
     -cp "%MD_CLASSPATH%" ^
     -Desi.system.config="data\application.conf" ^
	 -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 ^
     @bin/vm.options ^
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample ^
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher %*
      
popd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Core batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5be5d473-9a0b-48d2-a3c0-0a76396dbbe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Core batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
    cp_delim=";"
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
    cp_delim=":"
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH${cp_delim}openapi/examples/imagegenerator/imagegenerator.jar" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.imagegenerator.ExportDiagramImages "$@"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The shell script that starts a Plugin batch command line program on Mac OS-X, Linux, and Windows cygwin/msys:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85328b8b-15bf-4f75-8650-aaf9919a4c60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">Emacs</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command line Plugin batch program shell script that is able to launch program from any directory</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

# either local script variable or environment variable MAGICDRAW_HOME should be defined
MAGICDRAW_HOME="/home/myuser/Desktop/MagicDraw"

if [ -z "$MAGICDRAW_HOME" ]; then
    echo "MAGICDRAW_HOME environment variable not set, please set it to the MagicDraw installation folder"
    return
fi
 
cd "$MAGICDRAW_HOME"

# Reads CLASSPATH value from magicdraw.properties files (change to your properties file name)
MD_CLASSPATH=`grep "CLASSPATH" bin/magicdraw.properties | cut -d'=' -f 2`

if [ "$OS" = Windows_NT ]; then
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/;/g")
else
    MD_CLASSPATH=$(echo "$MD_CLASSPATH" | sed "s/\\\:/:/g")
fi
 
java -Xmx1200M -Xss1024K \
     -cp "$MD_CLASSPATH" \
     -Desi.system.config="data/application.conf" \
     -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 \
     @bin/vm.options \
     -Dcom.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.action=com.nomagic.magicdraw.examples.commandlineplugin.CommandLineActionExample \
     com.nomagic.magicdraw.commandline.CommandLineActionLauncher "$@"

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04912857-be80-439b-bbb9-2eb86b29731e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">133936363</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Licensing information" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490824 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00198: Specifying the appearance of cells and the row or column headers

- page_id: `170490824`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490824/Specifying+the+appearance+of+cells+and+the+row+or+column+headers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:58:02.659+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix > Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['cell-renderer', 'column-header-cell-renderer', 'row', 'header-cell-renderer', 'dependency-matrix-configurator', 'table-cell-renderer', 'matrix-cell-view', 'abstract-matrix-cell']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:

- A cell renderer
- A column header cell renderer
- A row header cell renderer

To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard *javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer* interface.

The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the *Integer* class or the instance of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView* class. The *java.lang.Integer* class represents dependency count. And the *MatrixCellView* class has a reference to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell* class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.

The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)* method.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To customize the appearance of a dependency matrix, you have to register cell renderer components. There are three types of the renderers:</p><ul><li>A cell renderer</li><li>A column header cell renderer</li><li>A row header cell renderer</li></ul><p>To register a cell renderer component, use the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class, created while registering a custom dependency matrix. All these renderers should implement the standard <em>javax.swing.table.TableCellRenderer</em> interface.</p><p>The cell renderer is used to represent dependencies in a dependency matrix. The renderer component of a single cell gets either the value of the <em>Integer</em> class or the instance of the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.MatrixCellView</em> class. The <em>java.lang.Integer</em> class represents dependency count. And the <em>MatrixCellView</em> class has a reference to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell</em> class, which has all the information about the dependencies in the cell.</p><p>The row and column cell renderers works the same way. To get the particular row or column header element inside the cell, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.ui.table.renderer.RendererHelper.getHeaderElement(javax.swing.JTable, int, int)</em> method.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490753 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00199: Standard profiles implementation

- page_id: `170490753`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490753/Standard+profiles+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:35:29.637+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Working with stereotypes and tagged values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile* to access "MagicDraw Profile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile* to access "StandardProfile" elements

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile***to access "Validation Profile" elements

MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Standard profiles implementation are available as part of API.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile</em> to access &quot;MagicDraw Profile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.StandardProfile</em> to access &quot;StandardProfile&quot; elements</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ValidationProfile</em><em> </em>to access &quot;Validation Profile&quot; elements </p><p><br /></p><p>MagicDraw plugins (like SysML) can also expose implementations of profiles. Check their APIs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490940 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00200: Starting execution

- page_id: `170490940`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490940/Starting+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.338+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs

- SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession
- SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession

Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows

- The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.
- If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the "isMainSession" flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.
- If the "start" flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>With Cameo Simulation Toolkit, you can start executing an element using the following Open APIs</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12a1efe7-0e1f-4d71-b787-b371ebf298cc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ee8cf1d-fc1b-45b8-96f9-007dadbadfc4">SimulationManager.execute(Element element) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21e3b246-3329-4c35-ad35-45db8faa59ca"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9dd29a01-11b3-4d69-a624-de481b5d9573">SimulationManager.execute(Element element, Boolean isMainSession, Boolean start) : SimulationSession</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>Once you use the aforementioned Open APIs, Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates corresponding simulation sessions. The conditions when using the Open APIs to execute an element are as follows</p><ul><li>The specified element could be any executable element, such as a Class, a Behavior, or an simulation configuration element.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="469dc2f0-9737-41d3-a2a0-95d515839ccc"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e768531-ccd4-45a4-a498-fc8a90ebe099">If the executed element is intended to be the main element of the execution, set the &quot;isMainSession&quot; flag to true. Then, the created session will become the main session of the execution. The main session is the first session created once the execution starts. If the main session is terminated, all executions will be terminated accordingly.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>If the &quot;start&quot; flag is set to true, the created session will start itself automatically (similar to the 'autorun' option in an SimulationConfig).</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490900 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00201: Starting MagicDraw or other modeling tool as part of another application

- page_id: `170490900`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490900/Starting+MagicDraw+or+other+modeling+tool+as+part+of+another+application
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:10:36.753+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.

In order to do that you must:

- Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath
- Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate start(...) method. (see Javadoc)
- Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM)

For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running programs in batch mode' space='DEVG2024xR1'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties' space='DEVG2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To integrate MagicDraw or other modeling tool into your application you should launch MagicDraw directly from your Java application. This would allow you to interact with MagicDraw or other modeling tool classes directly.</p><p>In order to do that you must:</p><ul><li>Include lib/ folder and all of its subfolder into your project classpath</li><li>Import class com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application and call the appropriate <em>start(...)</em> method. (see Javadoc)</li><li>Make sure there is only one instance of the modeling tool running in the same JVM(multiple instances cannot be started in the same JVM) </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>For launching the modeling tool straight from the command line, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Running programs in batch mode" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying batch mode program classpath and required system properties" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490941 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00202: Stopping execution

- page_id: `170490941`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490941/Stopping+execution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.356+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API

- SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void

Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can stop an execution by terminating its main session. Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to terminate any simulation session using the following Open API</p><ul><li>SimulationManager.terminateSession(SimulationSession session) : void</li></ul><p>Terminating a session will also cause all children of the specific session to be terminated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490911 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00203: Supported Changes from UML 2.4.1 to UML 2.5

- page_id: `170490911`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490911/Supported+Changes+from+UML+2.4.1+to+UML+2.5
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:15.273+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 18.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33

##### Metamodel Changes

All the changes are grouped by the categories:

44

###### New property names added

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Classifier::conveyingFlow | - |
| DeployedArtifact::deploymentForArtifact |  |
| PackageableElement::import |  |

###### Properties renamed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| NamedElement::classifierinheritingClassifier | - |
| Classifier::classnestingClass |  |
| ParameterableElement::parameterSubstitutionowningTemplateParameterSubstitution |  |

###### Derivation changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/classifier : Classifier [0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Feature::featuringClassifier, subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | All these properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. |
| InputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Action::/input : InputPin[*]{ordered, readOnly, union, subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| OutputPin::/action : Action[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| NamedElement::/namespace : Namespace[0..1]{readOnly, union, subsets Element::owner} |  |
| DirectedRelationship::/source : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | Both properties have been made automatically derived and read-only unions. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| DirectedRelationship::/target : Element [1..*]{readOnly, union, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} |  |
| NamedElement::/clientDependency | The Client Dependency property of a named element has been made automatically derived. This feature has been supported in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Order changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| AcceptEventAction::result : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The ability to order output pins holding the values received from an event occurrence, has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |
| Association::/endType : Type [1..*]{ordered, subsets Relationship::relatedElement} | The set of classifiers that are used as end types of the association, is no longer ordered. |
| Classifier::/attribute : Property [0..*]{ordered, union, subsets Classifier::feature} | The ability to order the attributes, which are direct properties of a classifier has been available in earlier versions of MagicDraw.Though the Attribute property cannot be edited, its value displays the same order of the attributes as is defined in other places of the classifier specification, such as the Owned Attribute property or the Attributes property group. Thus to change the Attribute property value, change the value of a relevant property. |
| ConnectableElement::/end : ConnectorEnd [0..*]{ordered} | A set of connector ends that attach to the particular connectable element, is no longer ordered. |
| DurationObservation::event : NamedElement [1..2]{ordered} | Event elements are now automatically ordered. |
| ReplyAction::♦ replyValue : InputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Action::input} | The set of input pins can now be ordered. In the Reply Value property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |
| UnmarshallAction::♦result : OutputPin [1..*]{ordered, subsets Action::output} | The set of output pins can now be ordered. In the Result property value cell, click the button to open the dialog for reordering. |

###### Other changes

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| LoopNode::♦loopVariable : OutputPin [0..*]{ordered, subsets Element::ownedElement} | The Loop Variable property has been made composite. |
| OutputPin::loopNode : LoopNode[0..1]{subsets Element::owner} |  |
| Activity::structuredNode : StructuredActivityNode [0..*]{readOnly, subsets Activity::node, Activity::group} | The property has been made read-only in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |

###### Properties removed

| Property | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Property::/default : String[0..1] | The Default property no longer appears in a property specification. The value of the Default Value property will be used instead of the removed property value. |

##### Notation Changes

From now on, the inherited members are denoted with the caret "^" sign.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Profile changes

Standard/system profiles *StandardProfileL2* and *StandardProfileL3* have been merged into a single one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /><h3 class="bold">Metamodel Changes</h3><p>All the changes are grouped by the categories:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc"
