<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490915 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00204: Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1

- page_id: `170490915`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490915/Supported+UML+Specification+Changes+from+Version+2.3+to+2.4.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:35.380+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 17.0.1 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Supported UML specification changes include:

44

###### Metamodel Changes

| Metamodel Change | Description / Comment |
| --- | --- |
| DATA TYPES ADDED |  |
| Real | Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models. |
| METACLASSES ADDED |  |
| LiteralReal | Specifies the real value. |
| METACLASSES CHANGED |  |
| DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification | The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification. |
| METACLASSES REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionEvent | These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types. |
| CreationEvent |  |
| ReceiveOperationEvent |  |
| ReceiveSignalEvent |  |
| SendOperationEvent |  |
| SendSignalEvent |  |
| PROPERTIES ADDED |  |
| Package::URI:String[0..1] {id} | Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see . |
| Property::isID:Boolean=False | Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see . |
| InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1] | Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction. |
| InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1] | Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction. |
| EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1] | The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier. |
| ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED |  |
| ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1] | The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1 |
| OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1] | The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more. |
| BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*] | Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more. |
| SUBSETTING CHANGES |  |
| Interface::redefinedInterface {subsets redefinedElement → redefinedClassifier} | An interface now references all the interfaces that are redefined by this interface. |
| SUBSETTING ADDEDNote: These features were already available in earlier versions of MagicDraw. |  |
| Duration::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} | Element or elements can be selected or created only under the owned element’s scope. |
| LinkEndData::qualifier:QualifierValue[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| LinkAction::endData:LinkEndData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| CreateLinkAction::endData:LinkEndCreationData[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| DestroyLinkAction::endData:LinkEndDestructionData[2..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeExpression::expr:ValueSpecification[0..1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValuePin::value:ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| State::deferrableTrigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::edge:ActivityEdge[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| ValueSpecificationAction::value: ValueSpecification[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| AcceptEventAction::trigger:Triger[1..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Stereotype::icon:Image[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| TimeEvent::when:TimeExpression[1] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets Element::ownedElement} |  |
| Classifier::/feature {subsets Namespace::member} | The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property. |
| Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets NamedElement::memberNamespace} | The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property. |
| Property::owningAssociation {subsets RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext} | A property now references the owning association of this property. |
| DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES |  |
| DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True | The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty. |
| DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] =True |  |
| PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public | The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public. |
| MULTIPLICITY CHANGES |  |
| EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1] | The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory. |
| Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2] | ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior. |
| DERIVATION CHANGES |  |
| Constraint::/context → context | The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property. |
| Message::/signature → signature | The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only. |

###### Notation changes

1. If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Supported UML specification changes include:</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="170747f4-008e-4e01-8a1b-db29bbb3d7ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4 class="bold">Metamodel Changes</h4><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Metamodel Change</strong></th><th><strong>Description / Comment</strong></th></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DATA TYPES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Real</td><td>Added to the PrimitiveTypes package. You can now use values of a real type in your models.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>LiteralReal</td><td>Specifies the real value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES CHANGED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>DestructionEvent → DestructionOccurrenceSpecification</td><td>The DestructionEvent class has been renamed to DestructionOccurrenceSpecification. It represents the destruction of an instance described by the lifeline that contains this instance.<br />DestructionOccurrenceSpecification is a specialization of MessageOccurrenceSpecification.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>METACLASSES REMOVED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>ExecutionEvent</td><td colspan="1" rowspan="6">These metaclasses have been removed from the Interactions package due to unsupported event types.</td></tr><tr><td>CreationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>ReceiveOperationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>ReceiveSignalEvent</td></tr><tr><td>SendOperationEvent</td></tr><tr><td>SendSignalEvent</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>PROPERTIES ADDED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Package::URI:String[0..1] {id}</td><td>Provides the package with an identifier that can be used for many purposes. URI is the universally unique identification of the package following the IETF URI specification, RFC 2396 <a href="http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt">http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2396.txt</a> and it must comply with those syntax rules. You can now specify this property for packages, profiles, and models. For the notation of the URI property, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Notation changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Notation Changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>Property::isID:Boolean=False</td><td>Indicates that the property can be used to uniquely identify an instance of the containing class, when the value is set to true. The default value is false. For the notation of the isID property modifier, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Notation changes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Notation Changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::returnValue:ValueSpecification [0..1]</td><td>Specifies the value returned by the executed interaction.</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::returnValueRecipient:Property [0..1]</td><td>Specifies the recipient of the value which is returned by the executed interaction.</td></tr><tr><td>EnumerationLiteral::/classifier:Enumaration[1]</td><td>The classifier of this EnumerationLiteral should now be equal to the enumeration that contains this enumeration literal. Redefines InstanceSpecification::classifier.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>ASSOCIATIONS REMOVED</strong></td></tr><tr><td>ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification::event: ExecutionEvent[1]</td><td>The association from ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification to ExecutionEvent has been removed, as the ExecutionEvent metaclass does not exist in the UML 2.4.1</td></tr><tr><td>OccurenceSpecification::event:Event[1]</td><td>The specification of the occurring event is not referenced any more.</td></tr><tr><td>BehavioredClassifier::ownedTrigger:Trigger[0..*]</td><td>Trigger descriptions owned by a classifier are not referenced any more.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>InteractionUse::argument:ValueSpecification[*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>StructuredActivityNode::node:ActivityNode [0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>SequenceNode::executableNode: ExecutableNode[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>Transition::trigger:Trigger[0..*] {subsets <em>Element::ownedElement</em>}</td></tr><tr><td>Classifier::/feature {subsets <em>Namespace::member</em>}</td><td>The /feature property of Classifier is now included as a part of the member property.</td></tr><tr><td>Feature::/featuringClassifier {subsets <em>NamedElement::memberNamespace</em>}</td><td>The /featuringClassifier property of Feature is now included as a part of the memberNamespace property.</td></tr><tr><td>Property::owningAssociation {subsets <em>RedefinableElement::redefinitionContext</em>}</td><td>A property now references the owning association of this property.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DEFAULT VALUE CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>DurationObservation::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td><td colspan="1" rowspan="2">The default value has been removed. In effect, the value of the firstEvent property is now by default empty.</td></tr><tr><td>DurationConstraint::firstEvent:Boolean[0..2] <span>=True</span></td></tr><tr><td>PackageableElement::visibility:VisibilityKind[1] =False → Public</td><td>The default value of this property has been changed to Public.This means that packageable elements are now by default public.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>MULTIPLICITY CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>EnumerationLiteral::enumeration:Enumeration [0..1] → [1]</td><td>The Enumeration must now be specified. It has become mandatory.</td></tr><tr><td>Multiplicity of ExecutionSpecification association with ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification: [1] → [0..2]</td><td>ExecutionSpecification is now associated with two ExecutionOccurrenceSpecifications:<br /><ul><li>The start ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the start of an action or behavior.</li><li>The finish ExecutionOccurrenceSpecification that designates the finish of an action or behavior.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" rowspan="1"><strong>DERIVATION CHANGES</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Constraint::/context → context</td><td>The context property of the constraint is no longer derived. It is now a settable property.</td></tr><tr><td>Message::/signature → signature</td><td>The signature of the message is no longer derived. However, it remains read-only.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="bold">Notation changes</h4><ol><li><p>If there is a value defined for the URI property of a package, model, or profile, it is automatically displayed on the corresponding shape. The following figure depicts an example of the package notation with the URI property value defined:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_uri_notation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>If the isID property is set to true for the selected attribute, the {id} modifier is displayed in the property modifiers group on the class shape. The following figure depicts an example of the attribute notation when the isID property is set to true:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="131"><ri:attachment ri:filename="isID_property_notation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>The name of a synchronous message is now displayed on a diagram pane, even if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the synchronous message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchCall_message_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Brackets “()” are no more added to a call message name if the message does not have a signal or an operation assigned. The following figure depicts an example of the call message name notation:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="153"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_message_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Supported UML Specification Changes from Version 2.3 to 2.4.1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490933 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00205: SysML classes for open API

- page_id: `170490933`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490933/SysML+classes+for+open+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:31.745+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in *<SysML plugin installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

The*com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities* class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.

Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Classes which are available for open API are included in SysML plugin open API documentation. Find these in <em>&lt;SysML plugin installation directory&gt;\openapi\docs</em>.</p><p>The<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLUtilities</em> class was added to the open APIs. It provides utility methods for easier work with SysML projects.</p><p>Methods and classes marked as deprecated do not support the development of external plugins.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490929 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00206: SysML Developer Guide

- page_id: `170490929`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490929/SysML+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-08T16:14:34.189+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home
- labels: ['open-api']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard stereotypes in SysML plugin are defined in SysML Profile and MD Customization for SysML Profile. Both profiles have their corresponding API classes: *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/SysMLProfile.html)*and *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/sysml/util/MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.html)*, respectively. Each class allows you to:

- Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag).
- Get a stereotype element.
- Check if an element is stereotyped.

Find JavaDoc of SysML plugin in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/sysml_javadoc.zip*

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>_no_install\openapi\docs\sysml_javadoc.zip*

Find JavaDoc of Requirements plugin in <modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/docs/requirements_javadoc.zip

For example, *<modeling_tool_version_number>**_no_install\openapi\docs\requirements_javadoc.zip*

Extract a .jar file and double-click the*index.html*to open it.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

#### PANEL: Related docs

Related docs

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guide' space='MD']MagicDraw Developer's Guide

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490930 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00207: SysML Profile

- page_id: `170490930`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490930/SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:19.110+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to use this API class.

****

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*STEREOTYPE_NAME*”.

For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.*ID*returns a string of “Id”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.

“SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.

For example, SysMLProfile.*getInstance*(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes SysMLProfile.Stereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) where "Elem" is the element you want to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.*isInstance*(Elem) returns *True* if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns *False* otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Usage includes “SysMLProfile.Stereotype.</span><em style="text-align: left;">STEREOTYPE_NAME</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">”.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">For example, SysMLProfile.RequirementStereotype.</span><em>ID</em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>returns a string of “Id”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes:</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).stereotype().getStereotype()” where project refers to a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">“SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(element).stereotype().getStereotype()“ where element refers to the element in a project that uses SysML Profile.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, SysMLProfile.<em>getInstance</em>(project).block().getStereotype() returns the reference to the «Block» stereotype object.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Usage includes  SysMLProfile.Stereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) where &quot;Elem&quot; is the element you want to check.</p><p style="text-align: left;">For example, given an element “Elem”, SysMLProfile.BlockStereotype.<em>isInstance</em>(Elem) returns <em>True</em> if the element “Elem” has «Block» stereotype applied and returns <em>False</em> otherwise.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490932 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00208: SysML Profile API Changes

- page_id: `170490932`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490932/SysML+Profile+API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:30:46.624+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > SysML Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.

The following constants were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = "ConstraintProperty";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = "QuantityKind";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = "definitionURI";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = "description";

public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = "symbol";

public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = "Unit";

The following methods were moved from the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile* to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile*:

getConstraintProperty()

getQuantityKind()

getUnit()

isQuantityKind()

isUnit()

isConstraintProperty()

The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SysML Profile API changes were made in relation to the SysML 1.4 support.</p><p>The following constants were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String CONSTRAINTPROPERTY_STEREOTYPE = &quot;ConstraintProperty&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_STEREOTYPE = &quot;QuantityKind&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DEFINITIONURI_PROPERTY = &quot;definitionURI&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = &quot;description&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String QUANTITYKIND_SYMBOL_PROPERTY = &quot;symbol&quot;;</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">public static final String UNIT_STEREOTYPE = &quot;Unit&quot;;</p><p>The following methods were moved from the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.SysMLProfile</em> to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em>:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getConstraintProperty()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">getUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isQuantityKind()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isUnit()</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">isConstraintProperty()</p><p>The constant NESTEDCONNECTOREND_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY changed to ELEMENTPROPERTYPATH_PROPERTYPATH_PROPERTY.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490628 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00210: Testing plugin

- page_id: `170490628`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490628/Testing+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:34.191+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can test if your plugin sample works by checking described messages or by checking the *magicdraw.log* file.

To check messages

1. Restart MagicDraw (or other modeling tool you are using). On startup, a message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_init.png']
2. Then exit a program. Another message should appear: [ATTACHMENT filename='message_My_Plugin_close.png']

To check the *magicdraw.log* file

- On the Help menu, click About MagicDraw . Then, in the Environment tab, click the Log File link.
- You can find the*magicdraw.log* file in a configuration file storage:
  - For Windows operating system, it is *C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<MagicDraw version number>*
  - For other operating systems, it is *<user.home>/.magicdraw/<MagicDraw version number>*

After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the *magicdraw.log* file:

```bash

```

Also all plugins and their statuses are listed in the program **Environment****Options** dialog, in the Plugins tab.

Looking at the file is the best way to find problems when the plugin does not work.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can test if your plugin sample works by checking described messages or by checking the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file.</p><p>To check messages</p><hr /><p> </p><ol><li>Restart MagicDraw (or other modeling tool you are using). On startup, a message should appear:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_My_Plugin_init.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Testing plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Then exit a program. Another message should appear: <br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="150"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_My_Plugin_close.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG2024xR1" ri:content-title="Testing plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p><p>To check the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file</p><hr /><p> </p><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">On the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Help</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> menu, click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">About MagicDraw</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. Then, in the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> tab, click the </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Log File</strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> link. </span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">You can find the<em> magicdraw.log</em> file in a configuration file storage:</span><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">For Windows operating system, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">C:\Users\&lt;username&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For other operating systems, it is <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;user.home&gt;/.magicdraw/&lt;MagicDraw version number&gt;</span></em></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p>After successful startup, the following information should be provided at the end of the <em>magicdraw.log</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea4d7537-8edd-4505-8e99-32eb1b6c723f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[LOAD PLUGINS:
com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor@edf730[ id = my.first.plugin, name = My First Plugin, provider = Coder, version = 1.0, class = myplugin.MyPlugin, requires api = 1.0, runtime = \[Ljava.net.URL;@ff94b1\] (mailto:@ff94b1) 

INIT PLUGINS: 
[com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.PluginDescriptor@edf730[ id = my.first.plugin, name =|http://Ljava.net.URL/] My First Plugin, provider = Coder, version = 1.0, class = myplugin.MyPlugin, requires api = 1.0, runtime = [Ljava.net.URL;@ff94b1]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Also all plugins and their statuses are listed in the program <strong>Environment</strong><strong>Options</strong> dialog, in the Plugins tab.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a95de164-a7b2-4279-8ee0-86dfa7ab824f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Looking at the file is the best way to find problems when the plugin does not work. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490964 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00211: Token-based authentication

- page_id: `170490964`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490964/Token-based+authentication
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-20T14:32:25.926+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.

##### Pre-configuring Authentication server

Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to *https://<auth_server_host>:<port>/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration*. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:

```text
:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]>
```

Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the **authserver.properties** file:

1. Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist . This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.
2. Add new client IDs separated with commas: authentication.client.ids . You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept client_id parameters that cannot be found in this list.

There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:

- When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from webappplatform.properties , parameter authentication.client.secret .
- ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property authentication.token.expirity from authserver.properties ), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.

To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request:

```text
]]>
```

##### Authentication with user interaction

The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:

1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (***authorization_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters:

```text
client_id=
response_type=code]]>
```

2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.

3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=authorization_code
code=]]>
```

4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.

5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (***token****_endpoint*** from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

```text
client_id=
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=]]>
```

##### Authentication without user interaction

To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:

- Headers: ]]>

Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password

- Query parameters: ]]>

If your client ID is added into the **authentication.client.permanent**list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter **authentication.permanent.token.expirity**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f592144-074b-424f-be69-8530c09c134b" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use the following procedures to authenticate your application or script with Teamwork Cloud.</p><h3>Pre-configuring Authentication server</h3><p>Authentication server implements the OpenID Connect standard with several customizations. To access the OpenID Connect configuration, go to <em>https://&lt;auth_server_host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/authentication/.well-known/openid-configuration</em>. Here is a sample of a returned configuration:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce45cafd-e140-4db1-8df6-865312107668"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "response_types_supported":["id_token","code"],
    "validation_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/validate",
    "jwks_uri":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
    "subject_types_supported":["public"],
    "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported":["RS256"],
    "signout_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/signout",
    "issuer":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication",
    "authorization_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/authorize",
    "token_endpoint":"https://<hostname>:8443/authentication/api/token"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Configure Authentication server to accept new client applications by changing these parameters in the <strong>authserver.properties</strong> file:</p><ol><li>Add URL of the client app to the whitelist, separating URLs with commas: <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</strong>. This can be either a full URL where users should be redirected back from the Authentication server, or just the beginning of it. The authorization endpoint will not accept redirect uri parameters that cannot be found in the whitelist.</li><li>Add new client IDs separated with commas: <strong>authentication.client.ids</strong>. You might need to uncomment this line first. The authorization endpoint will not accept<em> </em><strong>client_id </strong>parameters that cannot be found in this list.</li></ol><p>There are a few deviations from the standard OpenID Connect specification:</p><ul><li data-uuid="fad5d790-c56d-4253-8142-434bbce1bec6">When invoking the token endpoint, HTTP header X-Auth-Secret with secret must be passed with the value from <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">webappplatform.properties</span></strong>, parameter <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="2aeb51e2-bced-40a1-9063-0e5641fc06ca">ID tokens have expiration time (configuration property <strong>authentication.token.expirity </strong>from <strong>authserver.properties</strong>), they must be refreshed through the token endpoint by passing refresh tokens.</li></ul><p>To call TWC REST API with a generated authentication token, the token should be sent in the header of the request: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19b3247d-b03e-4724-b739-cb2353ae6439"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <received_id_token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Authentication with user interaction</span></h3><p>The basic Authorization flow should be as follows:</p><p>1. Redirect the user to Authentication server (<em><strong>authorization_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP GET parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14ce61e5-bd41-436a-9f42-6f6b75a27d22"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
response_type=code]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2. After the user signs in, receive HTTP GET request with a code parameter.</p><p>3. Send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f8bd4f-e472-4799-8623-8255439ecc88"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=authorization_code
code=<code_received_after_user_signs_in>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>4. Receive the JSON response with ID Token that can be used to authorize with Teamwork Cloud and refresh token that later should be used to refresh ID Token.</p><p>5. Refresh the ID Token by sending HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server (<em><strong>token</strong><strong>_endpoint</strong></em> from OpenID Connect configuration) with HTTP header X-Auth-Secret and parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95aee7b0-dc5f-4adf-88ca-f4f957778264"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scope=openid
redirect_uri=<your_app_url>
client_id=<your_client_id>
grant_type=refresh_token   
refresh_token=<refresh_token_value>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Authentication without user interaction</h3><p>To get a token without user interaction, i.e. using some predefined username and password and make only server-server calls, the system needs to send an HTTP POST request to the token endpoint of the Authentication server with HTTP headers X-Auth-Secret and parameters:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Headers: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb2c4bba-b35c-44aa-a2d2-5463ca3ba029"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[X-Auth-Secret: <secret from authentication.client.secret >]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><span>Authorization: Basic xxxxxxxxx, where xxxxxx is base64 encoded username:password</span></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Query parameters: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e66a619-e9f7-47f4-b0d2-073a8a58f310"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[grant_type=client_credentials
client_id=<your_client_id>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p>If your client ID is added into the <strong>authentication.client.permanent </strong>list, the returned token will have a longer expiration time, configured in the parameter <strong>authentication.permanent.token.expirity</strong>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490965 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00212: Token-based authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API

- page_id: `170490965`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490965/Token-based+authentication+sample+for+Teamwork+Cloud+REST+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-09T08:45:26.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > REST APIs > Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Teamwork Cloud REST API has an endpoint, which implements token-based authentication described in the [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG2024xR1/Token-based+authentication) page.

##### Setting up token-based authentication

To set up Teamwork Cloud and Authentication server for token-based authentication using REST API endpoint

1. Set up Authentication server to work with your SAML/SSO, if needed.
2. Open <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf /authserver.properties .
3. Find key**authentication****.client****.ids** and add **,twc****-rest****-api** at the end of the value. Save and close the file. The comma before **twc-rest-api** is a separator.
4. Open <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration /application.conf .
5. Find the esi .auth block and set the server value to your authentication server IP. Save and close the file.
6. Restart the Authentication server and Teamwork Cloud server.

##### Using token-based authentication

To use token-based authentication

1. Open REST API at the following URL: https ://<ip> :8111 /osmc /authen /login on a browser.
2. You should be redirected to the Authentication server login page.
3. Enter your credentials. The browser shows you a token, usually starting with eyJ … . [ATTACHMENT filename='Login Token.png']
4. Copy the token and use it to log on to REST API. The token is used in an Authorization header with the **Token** Type.

For example (using a token with cURL):

```text

```

The result is as follows:

```text
GET /osmc/login HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8111
> User-Agent: curl/7.55.1
> Accept: */*
> Authorization: Token
eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJBZG1pbmlzdHJhdG9yIiwiYXVkIjoidHdjLXJlc3QtYXBp
IiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6XC9cLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4NTU1XC9hdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiIsImV4cCI6M
TYwNjI5OTc3NywiaWF0IjoxNjA2Mjk4ODc3fQ.bA-
S5hHeSlV8AFoQVzzfIseC3qlmqQoBQREiapHN6I5CcvwetKdSVztWKkssSGjm31Y1zqoULio7_1Ma
mtGBbbzvA1WWQYFRiYk0D612yNDv4uNHBbNLNEv61TYNLwdPwPh0atVRehkh-
LSgjipXTvXj4mZViE0NHKIG9U7htA9Zzvxvck2JDXe_eU2-
4TCNm8II89ROaEb1tZ5nD84ieRbzJWqrcVTdqU2YfbIUeew5Nir8obkLYgixBXFKWsTHi3jNuoBx3
KcAIyZqL6cjtsCER4wbk4PEEDC57UVsOcsXWr6yvXIoVdJMOiDHo_fJMkgOjDqSyIL-2B21O-Y-GA
>
< HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
< Content-Length: 0
< Content-Type: application/octet-stream
< Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:08:44 GMT
< Accept-Ranges: bytes
< Server: Restlet-Framework/2.2.3
< Vary: Accept-Charset, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language, Accept
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-current-user=Administrator; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
< Set-Cookie: twc-rest-session-id=f40ef933-5461-4058-a1e7-9b8d4021aa8a; Path=/osmc; Expires=Wed, 25 Nov 2020 10:23:44 GMT
<
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact]]>
```

##### **Limitations**

This REST API endpoint only displays the ID token. Usually, ID token is not very long-living. You can configure the ID token expiration in *authserver.properties* file (using property **authentication.token.expirity**).

Usually the ID token needs to be refreshed as described in the page [Token-based authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/DEVG2024xR1/Token-based+authentication). However, this REST API does not display a refresh token, which is needed to refresh the ID token.

As a workaround, long-living ID tokens can be generated by adding **,twc****-rest****-api** to the *authserver.properties* file property **authentication.client.unlimited**. In such case, ID token expiration will be calculated using property **authentication.unlimited.token.expirity**.

Use this feature with caution and make sure that such long-living ID token is adequately protected.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490949 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00213: Triggering an Event

- page_id: `170490949`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490949/Triggering+an+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:26:57.436+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Cameo Simulation Toolkit Developer Guide > Java APIs > Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An execution engine can trigger a given Event by calling the triggerEvent(String event) of an ExecutionEngine. Just like the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods, this method is mostly used in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element).

If the triggerElement(String event) is called, the eventTriggered(String eventID) of all registered engine listeners will be activated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An execution engine can trigger a given Event by calling the triggerEvent(String event) of an ExecutionEngine. Just like the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods, this method is mostly used in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element).</p><p>If the triggerElement(String event) is called, the eventTriggered(String eventID) of all registered engine listeners will be activated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490921 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00214: UML metamodel changes

- page_id: `170490921`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490921/UML+metamodel+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:14.111+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Plugins migration > Plugins migration to MagicDraw 15.0 and later Open API
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two types of changes in UML metamodel API.

**UM****L specification changes**

The UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML.

Most changes are made in UML Templates, Simple Time, Interactions. We suggest to look at UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification for such changes if you see some compile errors.

**UM****L metamodel API implementation changes**

Earlier UML metamodel API used several interface layers for every compatibility level described in the UML specification. All these layers were merged into one using package merging, this is why earlier API had so many interfaces for every metamodel element in a different package. Such structure was very complicated and hardly understandable for new UML users.

MagicDraw version 15.0 and later UML metamodel API provides just one final merged layer. All intermediate layers were dropped from API. This change reduced a UML metamodel API size in few times.

Interfaces that are removed form the following packages:

- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.omg

- com.nomagic.uml2.magicdraw

- com.nomagic.uml2.omg

We left the same package name for merged interfaces like in a previous API version, that is *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.*********. You do not need to make any changes in your code if you were using interfaces from this layer. Otherwise you need to import a statement in your java source files, for example, *import**com.nomagic.uml2.omg**.kernel.Element*to *import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*.

UML metamodel interfaces use Java 5 generics, so it is much easier to understand types of various collections in the metamodel.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are two types of changes in UML metamodel API.</p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">UM</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">L specification changes</strong></p><p>The UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification introduced few changes in the UML metamodel itself. Some metamodel classes were added, some of them were removed, some metaclasses properties were changed. Most of these changes are not in a core UML, so they will affect you if your plugin is oriented to complex things in UML. </p><p>Most changes are made in UML Templates, Simple Time, Interactions. We suggest to look at UML 2.1.2 (2.2) specification for such changes if you see some compile errors.</p><p> </p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">UM</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">L metamodel API implementation changes</strong></p><p>Earlier UML metamodel API used several interface layers for every compatibility level described in the UML specification. All these layers were merged into one using package merging, this is why earlier API had so many interfaces for every metamodel element in a different package. Such structure was very complicated and hardly understandable for new UML users. </p><p>MagicDraw version 15.0 and later UML metamodel API provides just one final merged layer. All intermediate layers were dropped from API. This change reduced a UML metamodel API size in few times.</p><p>Interfaces that are removed form the following packages:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.omg</em></li></ul><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.magicdraw</em></li></ul><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.uml2.omg</em> </li></ul><p>We left the same package name for merged interfaces like in a previous API version, that is <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.</em><strong><em>** </em></strong>. You do not need to make any changes in your code if you were using interfaces from this layer. Otherwise you need to import a statement in your java source files, for example, <em>import </em><em>com.nomagic.uml2.omg</em><em>.kernel.Element </em>to <em>import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>.</p><p>UML metamodel interfaces use Java 5 generics, so it is much easier to understand types of various collections in the metamodel.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490707 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: UML model

- page_id: `170490707`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490707/UML+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:53:48.968+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide
- labels: ['omg-uml-metamodel', 'element', 'base-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In projects created with our modeling tools the UML model is an implementation of the OMG UML 2.5.1 metamodel. We do not provide a very detailed description of all UML metamodel elements and their properties in this documentation or javadoc. Our UML implementation covers whole UML specification as its and also extends it by introducing some additional Elements (like Diagram). You can find all this information in the UML 2.5.1 superstructure specification on the Object Management Group official Web site at[http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/.](http://www.omg.org/spec/UML/)

You should use UML model interfaces from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw*package.

Never use implementation classes like com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.impl.ElementImpl in your code!

The base interface of all model classes is *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, it implements the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement* inteface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

All structure derived from the *Element*you can see in the OMG-UML 2.5.1 Superstructure Specification.

All attributes and references described in the UML specification are implemented and accessible through setters and getters.

The UML model is thread safe for reading, but still the **UML model writing (modification) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Multi-threading' space='DEVG2024xR1']**.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490756 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00216: UML model implementation in 17.0.1 and up details

- page_id: `170490756`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490756/UML+model+implementation+in+17.0.1+and+up+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:48:59.682+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > UML Model Implementation Using EMF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In version 17.0.1 of MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us), the UML metamodel has only one package - UML, and all UML metadata exist in this package. All UML metadata can be found in *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLPackage*. For example:

```java

```

UML model elements can be created using the EMF factory *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory*. For example:

```java

```

In version 17.0.1, an element created using the EMF factory will be placed in the repository that does not belong to any project. The created object will be automatically added into the project’s repository after it will be added into the container that is in the project’s repository.

All UML model elements are directly or indirectly contained by EMF resources. Resource of an element can be retrieved in the standard way:

```java

```

There is no strict definition how EMF resources are organized in MagicDraw project. It depends on the project type (local or from the Teamwork Cloud server).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

All UML metadata can be found in <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLPackage</em>. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db420e1-89f5-41ea-a07a-52ebf32eed6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EAttribute nameAttribute = UMLPackage.Literals.NAMED_ELEMENT__NAME;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>UML model elements can be created using the EMF factory <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory</em>. The created object will be automatically added into the project’s repository after it will be added into the container that is in the project’s repository.</p><p>All UML model elements are directly or indirectly contained by EMF resources. Resource of an element can be retrieved in the standard way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba770e0c-7b9e-4cf1-8f5f-35a04d29e3ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element;

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490755 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00217: UML model implementation in version 17.0 details

- page_id: `170490755`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490755/UML+model+implementation+in+version+17.0+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:57.345+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > UML Model Implementation Using EMF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The MagicDraw (or other modeling tool developed by us) 17.0 UML metamodel has a nested package structure. UML is a root package that has nested subpackages, for example, classes/mdkernel, components/mdbasiccomponents, mdusecases. Metadata about a specific model element can be found in the package of the element. For example, a UML model element *Property*is from the package *uml/classes/mdkernel*, so metadata about *Property*can be found in the package *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.metadata.MdkernelPackage*.

```java

```

Model elements can be created using the EMF UML factory *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory*: For example:

```java

```

The element will be created in the repository of the active project. UML model elements are not contained by any EMF resource.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The MagicDraw  (or other modeling tool developed by us) 17.0 UML metamodel has a nested package structure. UML is a root package that has nested subpackages, for example, classes/mdkernel, components/mdbasiccomponents, mdusecases. Metadata about a specific model element can be found in the package of the element. For example, a UML model element <em>Property </em>is from the package <em>uml/classes/mdkernel</em>, so metadata about <em>Property </em>can be found in the package <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.metadata.MdkernelPackage</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16b492e9-24a0-4813-919c-268a9a8195c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EAttribute nameAttribute = UMLPackage.Literals.NAMED_ELEMENT__NAME;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Model elements can be created using the EMF UML factory <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.metadata.UMLFactory</em>: For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d59b466-59fc-4a77-ab1c-35971a01e854"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Component component = UMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createComponent();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The element will be created in the repository of the active project. UML model elements are not contained by any EMF resource.</p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490754 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00218: UML Model Implementation Using EMF

- page_id: `170490754`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490754/UML+Model+Implementation+Using+EMF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:23:57.269+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model
- labels: ['uml-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Starting from version 17.0, a UML model is implemented using Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF), that is the UML model is an EMF model. All UML model classes implement the *org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EObject* interface. The UML model can be accessed and changed using the EMF API. For example:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Starting from version 17.0, a UML model is implemented using Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF), that is the UML model is an EMF model. All UML model classes implement the <em>org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EObject</em> interface. The UML model can be accessed and changed using the EMF API. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a0b1bbe-90bf-4573-9e51-51f77796eef7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // get project model
    Package model = project.getPrimaryModel();
    // create session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "create class");


    // get name attribute
    EAttribute element_name = UMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getNamedElement_Name();
    // get name value (same as model.getName())
    Object name = model.eGet(element_name);
    System.out.println("name = " + name);
    // change name value (same as model.setName(name + "_1");)
    model.eSet(element_name, name + "_1");


    Class aClass = UMLFactory.eINSTANCE.createClass();


    // get packaged element collection
    Collection collection = (Collection)
    model.eGet(UMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getPackage_PackagedElement());
    // add new class (same result as model.getPackagedElement().add(aClass))
    collection.add(aClass);


    // close session
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af33752-130c-44db-a684-a1347acc778b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aca68646-5aeb-4b2c-99bf-d4236f7b83b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490743 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00219: UML model properties names

- page_id: `170490743`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490743/UML+model+properties+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-13T10:07:11.625+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > UML model > Event support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Constants for UML model properties names are defined in several classes.

Use*com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PropertyNames* for standart UML metamodel property names.

Use*com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PrivatePropertyNames*for auto-generated opposite property names.

The events’ names related with model creation/ deletion are listed in the*com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.UML2MetamodelConstants*class.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Constants for UML model properties names are defined in several classes.</p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: black;">Use </span><span style="color: black;"><em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PropertyNames</em> for standart UML metamodel property names.</span></p><p><span style="color: black;">Use </span><em style="color: black;line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.PrivatePropertyNames</em><span style="color: black;"> for auto-generated opposite property names. </span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The events’ names related with model creation/ deletion are listed in the </span><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.UML2MetamodelConstants</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> class.</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=170490893 space=DEVG2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00220: Understanding the Oracle DDL template structure

- page_id: `170490893`
- space_key: `DEVG2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR1/pages/170490893/Understanding+the+Oracle+DDL+template+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T15:24:11.620+01:00`
- ancestors: Developer Guides Home > Modeling Tools Developer Guide > Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default Oracle DDL template is stored in *<program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template*folder. The template consists of many macros - velocity functions. Each macro is dedicated for a particular object generation.

This example shows the velocity macro for the Oracle VIEW object generation:

```text
0 )
        #if( $force ) FORCE
        #else NO FORCE
        #end
    #end 
    VIEW $oracleHelper.getQualifiedName( $data )$columns AS" $nospace)
    #if ( $sql )
        #writeLine( $sql $tab )
    #end
    #writeText( ${sqlrestriction} ${space}${nospace})${semicolon}
    #end

]]>
```

A model element with the stereotype “view” is passed to a macro function, and then all element information is retrieved by the *$oracleHelper* utility class. The Oracle *View* stereotype has 3 properties. These tagged values store information about the force, query restriction, and query statements.

```text

```

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The default Oracle DDL template is stored in <em>&lt;program installation folder&gt;\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder. The template consists of many macros - velocity functions. Each macro is dedicated for a particular object generation.</p><p>This example shows the velocity macro for the Oracle VIEW object generation:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1754f03-c020-44f8-880b-cfa82d9449d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro ( generateView $data )
    #set ( $QUERY_RESTICTION = "query restriction")
    #set ( $force = false)
    #set ( $force = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $FORCE_TAG ) )
    #set ( $sqlrestriction = false)
    #set ( $sqlrestriction = $ oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUERY_RESTICTION ) )
    #set ( $sql = false)
    #set ( $sql = $ oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUERY_TAG ))
    #set ( $columns = false)
    #set ( $columns = $ oracleHelper.getViewColumnList( $data ) )
    #if ($columns)
        #set ( $columns = " ($columns)" )
    #else
        #set ( $columns = $nospace )
    #end
    #writeDocumentation ( $data $nospace )
    #writeLine( "$ oracleHelper.getCreate($data)
    #if ( $oracleHelper.getPropertiesListFromDefaultProfile($data, $VIEW_STEREOTYPE,$FORCE_TAG).size()>0 )
        #if( $force ) FORCE
        #else NO FORCE
        #end
    #end 
    VIEW $oracleHelper.getQualifiedName( $data )$columns AS" $nospace)
    #if ( $sql )
        #writeLine( $sql $tab )
    #end
    #writeText( ${sqlrestriction} ${space}${nospace})${semicolon}
    #end

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p></div><p>A model element with the stereotype “view” is passed to a macro function, and then all element information is retrieved by the <em>$oracleHelper</em> utility class. The Oracle <em>View</em> stereotype has 3 properties. These tagged values store information about the force, query restriction, and query statements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d60ef16-76d4-4295-a5d9-13307027b8b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Method $oracleHelper.getFirstPropertyValueFromProfile( $data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, $QUE- RY_RESTICTION) gets query restriction statement from element $data.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre> </pre></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
